user_comment: Love these joggers!! Love the buttery soft material of these joggers. I've purchased quite a few variations over the last couple of years. The full length cargo jogger in neon pink lived up to my expectations! It fits the same as all the other pairs I own, and the color is just as expected. The other pairs I have held up nicely in the wash - for the longest time I didn't dry them, but lately I've been lazy & threw them in the dryer. No shrinking, fading or pilling! Current stats: 5'7", 161 lbs, 42 bust, 34 waist, 41 hips, and I ordered the XL so they're not tight on my 24" thighs. Even when I got down to 138 lbs, 38 bust, 30 waist, 39 hips, 22 thigh they were still flattering, just a bit baggier.

Sooo SOFT I love the feel of these pants! I would’ve given them a 5 but they are long for those with short torsos or petite people. I am 5’2” 155lbs and I had to fold them over. I am a pear shape person and the Medium fit well snug on my bottom but that’s ok ;) if I don’t fold them over I look like I’m wearing geanie pants.

Very comfortable, pretty high waisted I love these for covid times. They are very very soft. I ordered XL and I am 158 lbs, 5’6”. I find the waist sits really high and that’s my only dislike of these pants. I can fold over the waist band and it hits at a better spot for my waist, but the look isn’t as good this way. Also this isn't a true Heather Grey, it's printed on, but I don't really care.

Very comfy. Very soft. Very comfortable gonna order more of these for sure. Very soft. Great to work in pockets on the side of very convenient. .5’5 170lbs ordered medium

I think these khakis are great! They have gotten me through all the seasons, washed, and dried. The color is typically khaki color, cotton, so definitely consider drying. I knew buying the 4 would be a little big but anticipated shrinking them some and now they fit perfect, not as small as a 2 though. They were long on me and I could hem them but just decided to fold them under and they stay very nicely. I wear here with booties, sandals, and keds. Th pockets are great also, none of those fake or itty bitty ones. Zipper and 1 button are of good quality and I’ve never had to iron either. I liked so much I ordered the lighter version too but they’re almost a pale pink so these are my favorite because they go with more items in my wardrobe. I’m 4’11”, 120lbs, 36 hips for reference.

Love these joggers! I am 5'2" and weigh 115 lbs. My inseam is 29" and my waist is about the same. I ordered a small because I wanted these pants to be loose for travel, and they did not disappoint! For a more fitted, polished look, the XS would have probably fit as well. They were plenty long enough and did not run up my ankles. We went on a 3 week trip to Europe and they felt like Pajamas on the long plane ride. They have washed and dried very well. No pilling so far. The rise is perfect for me. The waistband is very comfortable. The drawstring made me chuckle because it is very long, but that is no big deal. The material is very soft but keeps its shape. I wore these pants several days on vacation before washing then, and they held up great. The pockets are big enough to fit my Samsung S22 Ultra securely! They also dry quickly. Great buy for the money.

Runs Large, Fit is Awkward I’m 5’4” and 110 lb and a size 25 waist. I ordered the XS, and it was WAY too big in the waist and really really long in the crotch area. Even folded over once and pulled as tight as the ties go it was still awkwardly big as the pics show. But the rear was tight. These aren’t a good choice for those with smaller waists and larger legs/glutes, and I found the shape overall awkward.

Short girl tested, giant man approved As a petite, 5’0” female, I was disappointed to find that the XS was at least six inches too long... or so I thought. I missed the return cutoff date, so I jokingly gave them to my 6’5” boyfriend as a “gift.” Well, the joke was on me. He loves them (apparently they work well as compression pants post-workout), and he refuses to give them back. So if you need some sweats for that special giant in your life, these are the pants for you.

These are AMAZING lounge/sleep joggers!!! They are the perfect lounge/sleep jogger pants! They are so soft and have an adjustable waist tie. They are a tad long on me, ( I’m 5’ 3”) but don’t drag on the ground since they are joggers. They run pretty true to size and I ordered a medium. I love a loose lounge/sleep pant so they are PERFECT!!! Love them!!

Sooo comfortable.... I bought these in a medium and they fit just right, they are definitely not tight and not loose either. They drape nicely in all the right places. I would wear these pants anywhere that is a casual laid-back atmosphere...they look good and also feel like you're wearing your favorite pajama bottoms! I am 5' 6" and weigh about 130.
Love these! True to size. I’m 5’8 and 150 pounds and they fit perfect. Size up if you want a more slouchy fit.
So comfy!! Perfect light weight stretchy fabric. High waisted on me, but I’m 5’2. 145lbs and bought a small. Normally a 4/6

Soft like butter! Butter left out on the counter. Or margarine. Soft like margarine. A slightly smaller then expected compared to other Amazon joggers. A little snug on the band, but also not form fitting, I’m 139lbs. A little shorter than expected. I’m 5’8” so an inch longer would have been perfect. So it hits weird at the ankle. Pull up? Pull down? No, it’s fine. The material shows off the bum bum well. Maybe like pjs I do agree with the lighter grey. Material is thin but when I touch my leg the softness makes me feel warm inside. I’m ordering blue. I like blue. Oh, I got a small.

Nice pajamas that run big These are very nice, soft, pajama bottoms. They have a flannel feel but are not thick. As other reviewers have mentioned, they run big. I ordered a size Small and was worried they might be snug but they are loose on me, which is perfect for wearing in bed. The length is perfect for me too. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and typically wear a Small or Medium. I would have been swimming in the Medium.

Red and “White” polka dots no more They looked cute, cherry red with White dots. But the fabric smelled awful, so I washed them in cold water before wearing. The dots are no longer white. They are all pink, and not so cute any more. Thank goodness for the Color Catcher I tossed into the machine. It “caught” much of the red dye, but not enough. To address other reviews: yes, this is flannel, which is a napped cotton that can be thick or lightweight. Both are still “flannel”. And yes, they are long, which works for me at 5’8”.

Very roomy I liked the Amazon Essentials Women's Flannel Pajama Pant colors the best. These pajama pants are thinner than I would have expected. I really like that. I tend to sleep hot. These are a little large and long overall. I am 180 5'8". I imagine that they will shrink slightly once washed and dried. Also you will like that they are very smooth and comfortable feeling. I won't wear them outside so I don't need them to make a huge fashion statement, but I do need them to feel good and these do that well.

i mean they’re ok they are super thin and the hips are wierd i ordered an xxs they’re a little to big. i’m 5 3 1/2 and 95 pounds but idk i probably wouldn’t buy them again but whatevr

Loose, comfy, thin - Size M measurements below Measurements of size M/Medium in inches: -Full Length: 41” -Inseam: 29” -Width/waistband: 15” -Front rise: 12” -Ankle: 5” (measured after washing/drying) It’s soft, stretchy, lightweight, breathable, and flexible. I fell in love with it instantly. I bought it in every color over the months when I saw a sale. Keep in mind it’s thin and can be damaged if washed/dried improperly. It can be see-through if you bend over or if it’s form fitting on you. Imagine a panty hose. The more it stretches the more visible the skin is underneath. It’s loose from my butt to legs so I don’t have to worry. Since I have skinny ankles and calves, the cuffs rise up during sleep. It pills a little bit around the ankles. I usually prefer a jogger with a drawstring but for sleeping no. It’s hard for me to find comfortable sleeping pants. I guess I’m picky. It’s even harder to find one with pockets. This hits almost all of my checkboxes for a sleeping pant. I am only 4’11” and 95 lbs, but I ordered a medium since I prefer to wear all of my pants low-rise. I have a pudgy sensitive stomach. High-waist is not comfortable to me. I am one of the few waiting for that trend to end. Well, it doesn’t have to end but why not design low-rise at the same time? I don’t really have any complaints besides a suggestion: Please make a silky satin version! -Made in Philippines -95% modal, 5% elastane -Machine wash cold -Gentle cycle -Tumble dry low ——————- *UPDATE/EDIT* I lost weight over the year so I repurchased all four colors in size Small. 1 out of 4 is bigger than usual. Did they sow the wrong size tag? It feels more like a medium. I encountered the same problem with their thicker joggers. Inconsistent sizing is annoying because it is a hassle to return. However, I will put up with it because I love these joggers! I’m also sad they discontinued the light blue colors.

Exactly what I wanted! I wanted a lightweight, soft pair of lounge pants for the house. These are perfect! They are super-soft, light and cool, and the cuffs fit perfectly without being tight. One of my best purchases from Amazon! I'm 5'7", 140 lbs and the medium is a perfect fit.

Larger than Expected. Nice Material for House/Sleep Pants These night pants run a little big and baggy. I have a waist size of 27 inches so I thought the S would be good, but the waist band fell to my hip bone. As far as the length, I’m 5’4" with short legs, and I thought it was a little too long for me. The length from the waist to the bottom is 37 inches and the inseam is 26 inches for size S. I think the XS would have fit me better. I've included pictures of the measurements for size S. Otherwise, I really like the material of the pants. It is very soft, it as some stretch to it and a little sheer. These are good to wear around the house, sleep in, and they will keep you cool on a warm Texas day; I wouldn’t wear them outside. The pants also have pockets which is a nice addition and makes them very convenient for stashing things when moving about the house.

Lightweight and cozy. I'm 5'7, 195, and ordered XL. These are thin and comfortable. I have not washed them, but they fit pretty large. The material is listed as 64% viscose, 32% cotton, and 4% elastine. They feel like cotton. If tight, you will be able to see through them. There are no pockets.

Lightweight and cozy. I'm 5'7, 195, and ordered XL. These are thin and comfortable. I have not washed them, but they fit pretty large. The material is listed as 64% viscose, 32% cotton, and 4% elastine. They feel like cotton. If tight, you will be able to see through them. There are no pockets.

Very comfortable but strictly at-home pants for me These are, as described, a nice, lightweight pair of sweats (I don't really think they look like joggers because of the thin elastic at the bottom, but maybe that's just me). They fit a bit on the large side, especially through the hips and bum on me. The tie waist is adjustable, so they stay up, but they're loose everywhere else. The material is very soft. I washed on cold and dried on medium (hoping they might shrink a bit); they didn't shrink or fade. These are very comfortable at-home pants, but I wouldn't wear them out because of the fit and the fact that they're so thin. However, for lounging around, they're awesome. I couldn't wear them to jog because they're too loose and they would chafe. I'l have a 34" inseam and the length is just right. I would worry that they'd be too short if my legs were longer, and too long if I was very petite; I would say 5'4"-5'11" would be about the right range is this size (just guessing, though). If I were to order another pair, I'd probably size down to a medium because I prefer them a bit more fitted in the booty. For reference, I'm 5'10", 180 lbs, 36dd, 32" waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the large.

Black women's joggers Small women's drawstring joggers. These are very nice soft and good quality. They do run larger in size the drawstring helps keep them up. I ordered a small and they are more like a size medium. I am 5'4 and 110lbs and these are large on me. They are long with a slight gather at the ankle that isn't tight. The joggers are comfortable and great loung pants. The color is also great they are black with grey in them. Great joggers just remember to order a size down.

Black women's joggers Small women's drawstring joggers. These are very nice soft and good quality. They do run larger in size the drawstring helps keep them up. I ordered a small and they are more like a size medium. I am 5'4 and 110lbs and these are large on me. They are long with a slight gather at the ankle that isn't tight. The joggers are comfortable and great loung pants. The color is also great they are black with grey in them. Great joggers just remember to order a size down.

An odd length that looks and feels funny These are made of a really nice fleece-lined fabric that is soft and warm without being too thick or bulky. I love that they have deep pockets and an adjustable drawstring waist and are baggy and stretchy. What I don't love: I know these are advertised as "cropped". However, they aren't cropped at a length that works well for my 5'5" height. If you are 5'7" and up, these are cropped and hit you about like they do on the Amazon model. If you are 5'5" or 5'4", they look and feel like pants you put in the dryer and they shrunk and now you look dorky and embarrassing. They aren't cropped enough to be anything but just shy of ankle length and this feels weird and uncomfortable when you move or walk or sleep. Basically, they are too short to be pants but not short enough to register as capris. The length annoys my ankles and I'm constantly pulling them down or up, ANYTHING but the weird spot where they choose to hug my legs. If you are 5'3" and under, these will fit just like regular joggers, no "cropped" style at all. Also, while I like these a bit baggy, I ordered a small and they fit a tad big on my 26" waist, the waist sagging down a lot if I don't tie them. I prefer pants that are low-rise and sit below my navel for comfort but if you are tiny/thin, realize that you will be pulling these up alot.

Soft, comfy and warm These are really nice joggers. I have several pairs but none are fleece-lined, so these are my only winter-appropriate pair. They're lightweight but warm. I have some similar pants that are meant for winter wear but they're very heavy and make me itch (I have eczema). These are very soft, not bulky, stretchy, and seem well-made (stitching and material are all solid). They washed well in cold and I hung them to dry. We weren't given a size choice on these, and I ended up with a medium. If I wanted the fit in the picture (the looser, somewhat slouchy look), I would need the large for my size (XL would be too big in the waist). However, the mediums are very comfortable even though they're more fitted, and IME these types of pants stretch out after awhile. I'm very happy with these and will get a lot of use out of them since I work from home most days (and have 2 dogs). For reference, I'm 5'10", 180 lbs, 36dd, 32" waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the medium.

Great joggers These joggers are so comfy! They are soft sweatpant material on the outside and fleecy on the inside. They are a lighter weight. The length is perfect for me, I always have issues with the length of joggers. They go right above my ankles so I can scrunch them up to mid calf and they don't look too bunchy. The leg cuffs stay in place. They are a medium rise, and a slimmer style in the legs, not too tight like leggings, but not sloppy and baggy. I think they run pretty true to size, maybe a bit bigger, but sweatpants should be comfy, so I wouldn't size down unless you are between sizes. I'm 5'9, 165lbs, and I ordered my normal size, medium. They have a drawstring waist and pockets. The quality seems very good, I think these will last a while. I'm definitely going to stock up on these!

I've searched high and low for a capri/cropped jogger and finally found it. They are not the thin, flimsy or shiny material, they are a nice soft and for me whose always cold they are warm and the drawstring actually works. For reference I'm 5'4, around 150lbs,and wear anywhere between a small medium or large lol I also have chronic inflammation so I went with the large to be on the safe side. I find the large does run a little big even when tied and they will stretch as well. I would say order your regular size.

Really soft on the inside. I’m short and these are definitely still a cropped fit (I’m okay with this) some reviews mentioned them fitting like normal joggers but unless you’re under 5 feet I don’t see how this would be the case. I typically am between a size 8&10. I got the medium and still have a bit of room in my thigh, and had to tighten the waist drawstrings a bit. I maaaay have been able to get away with a small but I think it would have been snug.

Good bang for your buck I only give 4 stars because I did not find the sizing to be accurate. I got these for a work trip, the husband loves them and he’s not normally a pants-complimenter. I really like the length but they did run tight on my thighs so there was some pull in the crotch area. I had looked at previous reviews and thought I would be a size 2 and I liked the short look so I got them in short (I think!). I think the quality is good for the cost. Unfortunately I did not have time to make a return before the work trip and now they are my back up work pants. I have a small waist so I always have a hard time finding proportional pants that fit my thighs, butt, and waist but I am overall fairly skinny. 125 lbs and 5’5. I would buy again and in different colors if I needed more work clothes since they are so affordable but I WFH.

Thick, stretch, nice quality black pants! I am 5’5” about 120-125lbs. These pants fit perfectly!! I was looking for pants this length. So if shorter or taller, you might want to adjust which type of pants you buy. They are stretchy so they fit nicely without looking like leggings. The material is thick so they look and feel like nice quality. My only concern is the waist. I’m concerned if the fasteners will cause the pants to stick out in the front? I will update this post if after I wear them a few times this happens. But for now, I am super excited have a new pair of travel pants!!

Very comfortable 😇 I was scared of these pants not fitting me. I went based off the reviews and thank y’all for your help 🙂 I'm 5’3 and fit 8-10 pants. I ordered a size 10. I still have plenty of room! Feels stretchy and not too tight. I could size down to an 8 if I wanted to. I’ll be ordering more colors 😇

Perfect! I first got these pants in size 6, which is my usual size (I'm 5'8", 120 lbs.); they fit, but I wanted them a little looser for comfort (I'm 71 years old and don't like them "skin tight"). I returned them for a size 8. They fit perfectly! Very sleek and sophisticated looking! I love the longer length also! The quality of the construction, sewing, etc. is exceptional, as is the quality of the fabric. The colors are great also! I purchased the pink and navy too (would have got more, but I restrained myself!). Overall I'm VERY satisfied with this purchase and will look to buying more from Amazon Essentials in the future!

The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. Not my favorite I ordered a 3x based on reviews. They fit too large, but not sure a 2x would have been too tight. Wanted to give myself a little room with as many reviews saying they ripped. I have a big badonk and huge thighs. I wear a US size 20-ish. 5'5" The legs are weird. I was hoping for wide leg, but these are just cut wide everywhere and slightly less wide at the ankle, if that makes sense. They feel good on. A tad stiff, but ok. Smooth. Thick. Have MULTIPLE pair👍⭐️ I own three pair of these PJ bottoms. I’m very picky with the fabric for any PJ bottoms. Poplin is the best for me. I call these my house-wearing pants because when I’m home, all I wear are PJ bottoms. I WISH THEY HAD POCKETS. That is the only issue. They are always a little longer and bigger, but once I wash them, they fit perfectly—the NAVY, FLORAL pair I’ve had since May and washed numerous times. As you can see, they still fit perfectly. I ordered a LARGE, and they fit me as a LARGE usually does. (5’ 7”, 145 - 150 lbs) MATERIAL: 97% Cotton 3% Elastane WASHING: I washed them in WARM water 💦 on the REGULAR cycle and tumbled dried on NORMAL. The PRICE for these PJ Bottoms is in the usual range. They’re exactly as advertised and well-made.

40” waist without being stretched is not an XL. That is way too big for an XL. An XL should be 36” in the waist without being stretched, and up to 38” when being stretched, according to their size chart (I took a screenshot of the size chart for these). As you can see in the 2nd photo, the waist is 40” (20” x 2) without being stretched in the slightest. It won’t stay up because it’s so loose. I think the pair they sent me should have been XXL instead of XL. I can’t wear these, and because I waited until the weather warmed up again briefly before I washed and wore them for the first time, now I believe I am 2 days late to request a replacement. Uugh!

Thin and Comfortable Fabric, Size Runs Larger. These Amazon Essentials Women's Poplin Sleep Pants feel pretty comfortable to me. The fabric is thin, which is what I want. The style is classic and casual, perfect for the wearing inside the house. Size wise, I am 5'3" and 135lb, my usual size is S. I did chose the S when ordering, after trying it, I think it is a little bit large for me. I am not going to return or exchange, I personally don't have issue wearing slight larger pants, especially sleep pants. If you want the exact fit, I would recommend ordering one size smaller.

Good bang for your buck I only give 4 stars because I did not find the sizing to be accurate. I got these for a work trip, the husband loves them and he’s not normally a pants-complimenter. I really like the length but they did run tight on my thighs so there was some pull in the crotch area. I had looked at previous reviews and thought I would be a size 2 and I liked the short look so I got them in short (I think!). I think the quality is good for the cost. Unfortunately I did not have time to make a return before the work trip and now they are my back up work pants. I have a small waist so I always have a hard time finding proportional pants that fit my thighs, butt, and waist but I am overall fairly skinny. 125 lbs and 5’5. I would buy again and in different colors if I needed more work clothes since they are so affordable but I WFH.

Thick, stretch, nice quality black pants! I am 5’5” about 120-125lbs. These pants fit perfectly!! I was looking for pants this length. So if shorter or taller, you might want to adjust which type of pants you buy. They are stretchy so they fit nicely without looking like leggings. The material is thick so they look and feel like nice quality. My only concern is the waist. I’m concerned if the fasteners will cause the pants to stick out in the front? I will update this post if after I wear them a few times this happens. But for now, I am super excited have a new pair of travel pants!!

LOVE these jeggings! I am 5'9" and have longer legs so finding jeans that fit AND are long enough has always been a challenge. These are stretchy, nice waistband that helps with tummy control and are long enough! Plus they are super cute and look great! Never thought I would wear 'jeggings' but here I am loving these! I have already ordered another pair and will stalk...I mean patiently wait for the black version in my size to restock! And you cannot beat this price!

I like to read reviews before buying an item because I am 6' tall which makes it difficult to shop for clothes. To be honest, I forgot what size I got but I think it was 10-long. They are not long to my standards - on the photo you can see that "high waist" is not up to the waist and leg length is barely enough to look ok when walking. It looks too short when sitting (also on the photo). I like the material, so that's a plus. Pants are super hard to put on because they are tight in the waist area and I barely squeeze it through my hips. Also, as a side note - while I am tall, I think I have a very proportionate body, so my legs aren't long compared to the rest of my body. I am keeping them and might even get another pare when this one is done, but there are things that I wish were better :)

Long-Legged Luxury: Perfect White Jeggings for Tall Travelers!" Finding the right fit for jeggings can be a tall order when you are 5'9" with long legs, but these white jeggings in size 6 Long were a fabulous find! I took them on a trip to Italy in March, and they were a dream come true. Not only did they fit my long legs perfectly, but they were also incredibly comfortable and flattering, making them the ideal travel companion. Worn on the stunning Amalfi Coast, they matched the beauty of the surroundings and handled everything from long walks to leisurely dinners. Great fit true to size I am 5'7" and weigh 280 lbs. (I know) I am posting some maybe not so flattering pics of my belly pouch but hopefully gives someone with same stats an idea of how these fit. I would definitely recommend. Love the price too as I recently had gastric sleeve and am having to buy others more often I definitely will buy these at the next size down. Overall great buy for the money. Very satisfied!!

LOVE THESE I am middle-aged and all my 9/10 Old Navy jeans are too tight. I have extra weight around hips/tummy that I want to loose (hopefully), and I am not ready to give up. So meanwhile, I thought I would try a size 10 Long in these no-zip Amazon jeans. I am 5'7", and as you can see from the full leg picture, maybe I did not need the "Long". But I just now pulled them on and have not washed them yet. I would rather have a little bit of bagginess by the knees then too short at ankles. I am very satisfied with the feel and fit. It is a decent thickness of fabric --not super thin, but not thick and heavy. These are very comfortable everywhere on my crotch and legs, including the waist. The waist band seems midwaist and sits about 1.5 in below navel. Not highrise or lowrise or hipsters, etc. Some of my belly is inside the jeans, some above, but nothing feels constricted. Snug but not too much. Super comfortble when sitting and bending/squatting and my crack does not show. Hope this review helps you. I would try them out at least, since Amazon Prime gives you time to try them within the return window. I only bought one pair for now, but wow! I am excited about these. Have a blessed day.🙏💖

Color Unexpected. Otherwise Nice The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

Perfect for postpartum jeans I really like these right out of the bag. I had two babies back-to-Back and I have DR and lots of skin. The waist band on these are gentle and feel forgiving when I sit and stand. The butt fits great! Now I’m 5’7 and ordered a tall so that I can fold the bottom for cuffs and these actually have a normal length, but it also gave me a longer hip and thigh which I LOVE. It’s been why I order long pants. The demon is a great color. Need to wash before wearing because it smells like manufacturing and it’s mildly irritating to the skin. The denim is thin and has the perfect amount of stretch. I would buy these in every color! 10/10 would recommend

Not bad but I can't seem to get them in black. I purchased these pants in camel and white. I am 5'10" 222lbs and I bought them in xxl long. I love the length. I think the colors are true to pictured. The quality seems good. As you can see, I carry a lot of my weight around my middle. These pants have a wide waistband that helps suck me in a little. My only complaint is that, even with my big middle, the waist could be a little with belt loops added. They stretch out a little after wearing. I've tried to order them in black but twice I received a different pant, one being a Levi's brand, neither did I like. I'd really like to have the same pant in black. They're comfortable pants. I like them.

Stretchy yet firm I'm 5'3" 172 lbs and wear a L in most things. I have short legs so I purchased these in a 12 short. They are slightly snug at the waist, but too much looser and I'd worry about them sliding down. So far they work for me. I haven't noticed any sliding yet and the stretchiness is forgiving yet firm, so I'm not too concerned about the knees getting baggy. The back pockets are real, but not the front. I would definitely recommend these. Hopefully I still like these at the end of the day. There was a weird spot on the waist band where the stitching was done over several times. It's pretty itchy right now, maybe I'll just get used to it. Or maybe it rubs my stomach raw and I curse Amazon as I'm putting bandaids on the spot. only time will tell.

comfortable & attractive pull-on jeans, good value for the price The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

these are perfect! LOVE THEMMMMMMMM!!!! i love that these fit “fitted” on me and not tight. they are super comfy and flattering. i could probably wear a 2 short but i think they would be tight like leggings and that’s not the look i am going for. the fabric is a medium weight twill w just the right amount of stretch and the ankle fits more like a skinny jean opening rather than a legging. These are SO worth it. These jeans are awesome. I was so worried about the fit because I am so oddly shaped. I am 5'2 and 170lbs. I have a larger midsection and smaller legs. Good fit and comfortable, but created a muffin top I ordered the short size 12 and they are good but not the ideal jegging for me but still worth keeping. I have taken two photos with two different shirts and you can see that with the loose shirt, it camouflages my waist area. With the tighter shirt, it created an unflattering fit to see my fat bulging from the tight waistband. Ideally a looser band would be preferred, but I have a difficult body to fit jeans and pants since I am 5’3” and I have more of a “gut” than butt. So to get pants to fit in the butt, I have to order a size down but then it creates a tight waist and sometimes a muffin top and this is exactly what these jeggings did for me. But other than that, the length is great, the material nice and stretchy while still looking like jeans, they are comfy, and the waist band is still comfortable, despite the muffin top effect. I think I’m going to keep and just wear with looser shirts.

Super comfortable and cute These pants are super comfy & look great on. I bought them in both black & blue, and I love them. For reference, I’m 5’5” & 140 lbs wearing a 6R and they fit perfect.

Great fit, comfortable I’m 5’8”, 165 lbs, carry weight in my stomach. I chose a 12 Long and it’s a perfect fit! They have the thickness of real jeans and look totally real but the waistband is super comfy. It’s just long enough for me, but may not be long enough for anyone over 5’9” or so.

SO flattering!!! These are the best office pants I’ve found. They look dressier than just different colored jeans. They are made out of a slightly thicker material than just jeggings which is much more flattering to the figure. There is enough stretch, and they are thick enough to hold everything in with out lumps or dimples. I have curves and thicker legs so some jeggings make my legs just look like stuffed lil sausages. I gained and then lost a significant amount of weight, and now that I’m 40, being able to smooth out lumps and bumps without shapewear is really important to me. I bought a size 8 and the fit was perfect for my 5ft 3in 145-148lb frame. I am still a little wider in the stomach from having my baby a few months ago, so if you have a very narrow waist, then you might have a little gap in the back since they are pull on without buttons/drawstring etc. For me though, they were PERFECT for my post partum post weight loss middle aged lady body.

Best pant fabric ever! Love the fit even though I could of sized down to a large- the waist still showed the high waist cinch and only issue is I’m 5’3” so a little longer crotch area to hike up and hem under pant length but really cute and fab AA pants! Grease pants lol

Great value and nice enough to wear in public I liked these palazzo pants so much I ordered a second pair in black and white polka dot. They are soft and drape nicely from the hip. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. They are very long and almost touch the ground. They fit well but I think the XL would have been fine as they are stretchy. They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Size up I'm 6ft tall 215lbs I have a nice foundation type body... my legs are long AF I GOT THE XL my first ones and they were to short... my second ones I got 2xl and they still ran a little flood but the 3xl are perfect in length... I'm a size 12 in jeans and these are soft and flowy and I freaking love them in the 3xl.... the xl fit me perfectly in size except length so I sized up and they're perfect for lounging and looking cute

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Not the best material, but very comfy and stretchy I have purchased 3 pairs of these over the course of the past few years and I’ll probably continue to in the future. They’re definitely not of the highest quality, but all 3 pairs have held up so far and are fantastic for lounging around the house. It’s such a huge bonus for me that they have worked for pregnancy and non-pregnancy wear, without stretching out. I’ve noticed these are generally true to size as well. I love the length, being that i am 5’ 7” with long legs. The pictures are of my most recent purchase; I’m about 33 weeks pregnant, roughly a size 10/12 in regular sizes at this point, and I’m wearing a size large of these pants. I probably could’ve sized up for a looser fit, but I found these comfy and not restricting at all. Love them!

Comfy and VERY long These pants are about as expected, they're soft and comfy, very baggy and I like the high rise that is not elastic. The print is not great, it's obviously a printed image that looks kind of cheap(and it is cheap), but they're just lounge pants so it doesn't bother me much. The print is still pretty. I am very short, 5'1", and I'm used to pants being too long, but these were WAY too long. I cut 4 inches of fabric off the bottom, hemmed about 1/2 inch, and they still pool on the floor. One picture is with 1 leg hemmed and 1 leg with the original length. The other 2 more full size photos are after the pants were hemmed. I'm about 110lb and I got a small.

Comfy and VERY long These pants are about as expected, they're soft and comfy, very baggy and I like the high rise that is not elastic. The print is not great, it's obviously a printed image that looks kind of cheap(and it is cheap), but they're just lounge pants so it doesn't bother me much. The print is still pretty. I am very short, 5'1", and I'm used to pants being too long, but these were WAY too long. I cut 4 inches of fabric off the bottom, hemmed about 1/2 inch, and they still pool on the floor. One picture is with 1 leg hemmed and 1 leg with the original length. The other 2 more full size photos are after the pants were hemmed. I'm about 110lb and I got a small.

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

Comfortable and beautiful! I have been seeing pants like these for several months on Pinterest and they just seemed too good to be true. Cute, affordable, and comfy- could I really have stumbled upon the trifecta of pants? I did! I am 5’7” and as I’m on the curvier side and have long legs, I ordered the XL. They fit perfectly and the pattern looks just like the pictures. I know they’re intended to be more of a lounge pant, but the print is so beautiful I think I want to dress them up. With a fitted top and nice sandal these might be cute dresses up. They’re so light they’ll do well in warm or cool spring or summer weather. I hope they hold up. I’ll likely wash them in cold on the delicate cycle and line dry. If they survive their first few washes, I’ll be buying these for every woman I know.

Nice pants They’re definitely a silky material, which is what I was expecting from the reviews I had read before I bought them. The crotch hangs kind of low, but if I roll them or hike them up to my belly button, then they’re too short. But overall they’re good pants. I don’t know if I’d buy again, but I’m glad I got these ones. I’m 5’7” and weigh about 140lbs and I wanted them to be long, which they are. The string is super long. On the pink/ “khaki” ones, I did notice some strings hanging from one of the pant legs.

Comfy and cute! Looks just like the picture and feels super soft! The material is good quality and an appropriate density. It isn't thin enough to be see-through, but it isn't so thick that it's hot. The pants do run large, though. I'm a 6 in pants and usually size up for PJ's, but the medium swallowed me. The small is still a little big, but fine for PJs. When sizing down from a medium to a small, the pants lost a few inches in length. I'm almost 5'8", and the medium was quite long (I loved it, but the length might not be for everyone). The small is a fine length for my height, though I wouldn't want them to be any shorter. Overall, great PJs, just go down a size!

Soft Roomy & Comfy I found that reviews that had height and weight to be the most , I’m 5‘3“ & 150-ish pounds, depending on the day. I got a medium and it’s a generous medium so it’s actually a little loose. I have thick hips or a big booty and so I usually have to get a large… these fit great, in a medium.

Runs Small- size up at least two The pants are cute and comfy. The material is not what I’d expected- it’s more of a slick feel. I am 185 lbs. & 5’10” and I ordered an XL. The waist fits perfectly on the XL but I think I will get XXL next time for some more length (so I’ll be sizing up 3 times 😳).

Wouldn’t call these yellow... They are stretchy/roomy but I don’t like the fit on me. The material is fairly thin. I am 33wks (5’6” and 185lbs) and bought a large, I’m proportionally smaller in the hip/rear area, so these fit over my bump but are kind of baggy in the groin area. I bought these for postpartum wear and think they will be way too big (probably could pull up almost to my braline without a bump, and I have a fairly long torso. The length is good! Also, I bought to go with a yellow nursing top and these are not the yellow they appear to be in the listing and do not go with the top I bought (see my review pic). I was anticipating a darker gray and marigold yellow, but these are lighter gray and more peach than yellow.

Great purchase, but expect a higher rise I really like these because of the material that’s soft, light, and stretchy. Good, size up for loungy fir These are way cute. I normally wear a us 4/6 and sized up to the large (8) as I wanted them to be a bit baggy and lounge like. They fit perfect and are nice and long for me (5'5"). I will say they are way light weight and stretchy. But they are printed to look like sweats/knit fabric. They aren't perfect but for the price I'd buy another pair.

SOOOOOOOOO COMFORTABLE!! These are the most COMFORTABLE pj pants EVER! And sooooooooo CUTE!! They’re thin but not see through thin, just cool. They wash well and no shrinkage! I ordered a small. I’m 5ft 3” 120 pounds and they fit me perfectly with a little room. Gonna order some more! Love all the different kitties on them too! You will not be disappointed if you buy these!!
Very Comfortable These lounge pants are super comfortable and fit pretty loose. I am 5'9 147 LBS and these are still pretty long on me unless I pull them up way over my bellybutton.

Inconsistent sizes between colors I ordered a flowered print and loved the fit, very comfortable and perfect length (I’m 5’7). So I ordered a second pair in black, same size. They’re a good 2” shorter and look like high waters. A little bit tighter leg as well. Returning those ones.

Perfect for my belly! Love! Perfect for my big belly! I'm 27 weeks pregnant. I'm 5'8 normally 140 lbs currently 160. I ordered a large. Fits perfect! Finding a lounge pant that is long enough for me is about impossible. These are perfect. Light weight and so comfy!

Perfect for my belly! Love! Perfect for my big belly! I'm 27 weeks pregnant. I'm 5'8 normally 140 lbs currently 160. I ordered a large. Fits perfect! Finding a lounge pant that is long enough for me is about impossible. These are perfect. Light weight and so comfy!

Very big, size down 1 or 2 sizes They're so so comfortable! Way too big though. I suggest size down 1, or even 2 sizes! Unless you like your clothing VERY baggy. I ordered a large first, they were extremely big, so I returned and ordered a medium. They're still very big but wearable. Should've ordered a small. I'm 5'5", 160lbs. Very long too. I will be keeping these for a swimsuit cover/lounge pants.
Super comfy! I love these pants! They are a lightweight and soft material AND they are long! I like my pants to touch the floor and these deliver. I am 5'8, 200lbs and the 2XL is perfect.

These are super soft and comfy! I’m 25 weeks pregnant and the waistband fits great and come up super high on my waist! (I’m 5” 4’ And about 145lbs right now) I got the large to be safe! Not just for PJs These pants are amazing. I am 5’10 and they are long which I love. The black ones were too short but I own two pairs of flowery ones. These are great for casual nights out. I don’t even use mine as pjs. I got so many compliments. They are super soft and make you feel sexy. Buy them!

This is why we can’t have nice things Soooo disappointed. I own two other pairs of these pants that I purchased last year and *love*. It’s so hard to find pants long enough for women over 5’2” and all of the comments agreed- these are comfy and long! I bought these specifically because the reviews raved about how they were actually long enough! I decided to buy these in black so I’d have a 3rd pair and when they arrived they were 2.5” shorter than the ones I own, which landed them above my ankle. (I’m 5’6” and ordered an XL.) When I messaged the seller they told me they redesigned the pants “based on customer feedback” and that they are supposed to be much shorter now. I guess it was too much work for a couple of people to hem their pants and now the rest of us are back to high water PJs. So, thanks?

Great! I'm 20 weeks pregnant and these are absolutely fantastic! So comfortable and hit at about the belly button with a loose waist band. I am 5"5 205lbs and got the Xlarge. They are a tad to long but I'll just hem them.

Great! I'm 20 weeks pregnant and these are absolutely fantastic! So comfortable and hit at about the belly button with a loose waist band. I am 5"5 205lbs and got the Xlarge. They are a tad to long but I'll just hem them.

Comfy, adjustable high waist. I'm pretty short- 5' 4", so I had to hem these about 3.5 inches. I love the high waist! I carry all my weight in my thighs, they stretch over them just fine. I ordered a L. I measure 30" waist, 45" hips.

Comfy, adjustable high waist. I'm pretty short- 5' 4", so I had to hem these about 3.5 inches. I love the high waist! I carry all my weight in my thighs, they stretch over them just fine. I ordered a L. I measure 30" waist, 45" hips.
Nice and comfortable. True to picture. Should have gotten a small for a snug fit. I'm 5'4 1/2, 157lbs with long legs. Size 8-10 and Large are my normal sizes, but I got a medium.

Best pajama pants I’ve ever had! I wasn’t going to order these because I kept seeing that they ran really long but figured I could return them. They arrived today and I LOVE THEM! They fit me perfectly and are super soft almost satin feeling. Im 5’7” and weigh 140. Ordering more!

I just got these and I love them! I ordered the light gray and the ... I just got these and I love them! I ordered the light gray and the dark blue pair and they're both great! I am 5'9" and regularly size 16, I ordered the 3x and they fit great! A little baggy like I like but not too much. So happy I took the risk and ordered these! The material is soft and slinky and breathable. Love!

Great value and nice enough to wear in public I liked these palazzo pants so much I ordered a second pair in black and white polka dot. They are soft and drape nicely from the hip. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. They are very long and almost touch the ground. They fit well but I think the XL would have been fine as they are stretchy. They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Size up I'm 6ft tall 215lbs I have a nice foundation type body... my legs are long AF I GOT THE XL my first ones and they were to short... my second ones I got 2xl and they still ran a little flood but the 3xl are perfect in length... I'm a size 12 in jeans and these are soft and flowy and I freaking love them in the 3xl.... the xl fit me perfectly in size except length so I sized up and they're perfect for lounging and looking cute

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Not the best material, but very comfy and stretchy I have purchased 3 pairs of these over the course of the past few years and I’ll probably continue to in the future. They’re definitely not of the highest quality, but all 3 pairs have held up so far and are fantastic for lounging around the house. It’s such a huge bonus for me that they have worked for pregnancy and non-pregnancy wear, without stretching out. I’ve noticed these are generally true to size as well. I love the length, being that i am 5’ 7” with long legs. The pictures are of my most recent purchase; I’m about 33 weeks pregnant, roughly a size 10/12 in regular sizes at this point, and I’m wearing a size large of these pants. I probably could’ve sized up for a looser fit, but I found these comfy and not restricting at all. Love them!

Comfy and VERY long These pants are about as expected, they're soft and comfy, very baggy and I like the high rise that is not elastic. The print is not great, it's obviously a printed image that looks kind of cheap(and it is cheap), but they're just lounge pants so it doesn't bother me much. The print is still pretty. I am very short, 5'1", and I'm used to pants being too long, but these were WAY too long. I cut 4 inches of fabric off the bottom, hemmed about 1/2 inch, and they still pool on the floor. One picture is with 1 leg hemmed and 1 leg with the original length. The other 2 more full size photos are after the pants were hemmed. I'm about 110lb and I got a small.

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. So nice for travel and hot summer weather! I got size Medium, color Beige. I'm 5'5, 145lbs, waist 32, hips 41. I probably could've sized up to a Large for a better fit, and a little more length. I was hesitant to get these, but I needed something light to wear during a recent work trip to Bahamas. I wanted to look put together, while still being comfortable. I wore these on the plane, and I was very comfortable - not too hot or cold. I also wore them around the resort where it was hot and humid, and again, I was super comfortable! I was worried about my mom belly being emphasized, but the loose linen material helped to camouflage it! For a sleeker look, I did wear shape wear, but it really wasn't needed. They're somewhat sheer, I wore shape wear underneath and didn't have any issues with anything showing through. The pockets are deep, and they don't look too bulky when you put items in them. I carried my phone, a small wallet, and lipgloss, and my phone was really the only noticeable item in my pocket. They look cute with sneakers or sandals. I'm sure they'd look cute with heels, too. Note - At first they felt too short and frumpy, but I just adjusted how they sat on my hips and waist until I was happy. I think a t-shirt with a front tuck or knot looked really cute. They do wrinkle, but spritz them with a little water, and the wrinkles will shake right out. I took one star off because they do feel slightly rough, and they're dry-clean only.

Best pants I’ve ever gotten from Amazon These pants are fantastic. They fit me (short legged 5’4” and apple shaped) perfectly without the legs dragging on the ground at all. The elasticated waist is so comfortable and I’m pretty sure someone at least two sizes smaller or larger than me (usually a 2XL) could wear them easily due to the way the waist is sewn. Size DOWN at least 1 size! 5'2" - 165-170 lbs - 41"-36"-41" - White is lined! Hot weather approved. TLDR — Size down at least 1 size, if not 2. Length is good for some petites (5'1" to 5'3") and regular height (up to 5'7"). I'm 5'2" and weigh between 165-170 lbs. depending on the time of the month. My measurements are 41" Bust - 36" Waist - 41" Hips. I generally wear size 10-12 or L-XL depending on the brand, fabric, type of clothing, etc. I have a small FUPA but no butt, so occasionally I can size down to a size 6-8 or M (Medium) depending on the stretch of the fabric. I'm short-waisted and usually wear mid- or high-rise with a short or regular inseam depending on the pants and type of shoes I'm wearing. I ordered size L (Large) as suggested by Amazon in the color white, and it's too loose and baggy overall. The smocked waistband is comfortable and super stretchy. The white linen pants have lining inside all the way down except 6" from the bottom so it's not see through! You can't see the deep pockets... maybe very faintly if you're looking really hard in the picture. (I wish other white pants are lined like this, because there is nothing more annoying than having white pants that are practically transparent where you can see the pockets or even your nude underwear.) Because the pants are lined, it does add some noticeable weight to the overall feel of the pants, and along with the super stretchy smocked waistband, it causes them to slowly slide down. The pockets are deep and a great addition, and you could fit a phone in there, but because of the overall "heaviness" of the fabric and lining, the phone would definitely pull down the pants because the waistband won't support it. For this reason, I would size down to a size Medium for a relaxed fit or even a Small for a tighter fit. I'm only 5'2" and have the smocked waistband pulled above my belly button. The length of these pants are 41.5" with an inseam of 27.5". I'm wearing 2" platform sandals where the pants are almost grazing the floor. If you're shorter than 5'2" and don't want to wear 3" heels/ wedges/ platform sandals, these may be too long for you. Check the size chart for the overall length of the pants. According to the tag, this is made in China and it's only 30% linen and 70% rayon. It says, "Dry clean only. Do not bleach. Steam iron." I used a garment steamer to get the wrinkles out. The fabric isn't scratchy like typical linen since this isn't 100% linen. It felt light and breathable, even in hot, humid 92°F weather. Sitting on them obviously causes them to wrinkle in the back but the front remains mostly wrinkle free. Based on the size chart, I think going by the hip measurement would be best. Size medium for me would provide a much better fit where the waistband is hopefully a little tighter but the pants are still relaxed enough, while size small would provide an overall tighter fit. I'm debating on washing these to shrink them down a little as others have mentioned. Aside from the dry cleaning care instructions, who has time for that?! I do like these pants a lot and will order another pair or two in different colors and smaller sizes. I'll update my review later if I remember.

Perfect travel, dress-up/dress down pant. Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

Comfy, flowy palazzo pants. Very soft, comfy fabric. Nice big pockets. At 5’2”, the medium runs long on me. Unless I hike it up at the waist, they will need to be hemmed to wear with flat shoes. Shipping was very prompt.

LOVE! I ordered these pants looking for a cooler option in the Texas heat. These are perfect for dressing up or down. I got tons of compliments, I need them in every color! I’m 5’2 and 120lbs, these are a small and fit perfectly.

I'm neutral on these I really wanted to love these linen/cotton blend pants. SUPER CUTE SWEATS! I ordered these ANRABESS Women Wide Leg Baggy Cargo Sweatpants in a size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she loves the way they fit! For reference, she's 5' tall and 105lbs-ish with a 26" waist. Though these pants are a little long on her, she insists that is fashion... and I'm here for it! Here's what we like about these sweatpants: •QUALITY GARMENT: These sweats are well made. The stitching is straight and secure. They feel more like a higher end pair of sweatpants than fast fashion. •SOFT: These sweatpants are made of very soft, cozy material. While they are warm, they aren't extremely thick or heavily lined with fleece. •FASHION FORWARD: The actual cut of these pants is really nice! They are very baggy in the legs, specifically at the knees. But they taper up at the waist, giving them a really good shape. My daughter loves the cargo pockets on the sides. •WASH & WEAR! These pants can be washed, dried, and worn dozens of times without compromising the quality. Mom win! Overall, my daughter is just obsessed with these! She likes to wear them with tank tops and a bunch of accessories (not shown in the picture, but she was just lounging). She also wears them with graphic tees and athletic shoes. I just like that they are affordable, comfortable ad made well. We highly recommend them!

THESE ARE MY NEW FAVORITE LEGGINGS - FROM PICKIEST PERSON EVER To start, I am almost 5'9 and about 145 lbs. I wear a size 7-9 for pants. I got a size medium in charcoal grey and these seriously could not have fit any better. First of all, I have been looking for the perfect pair of leggings for YEARS. Leggings seem to always either slip down or stretch out while working out, have too much fabric around the crotch, have an uncomfortable/unflattering waistband, are see-through, etc. Ladies, you understand all these issues. These leggings were so great that I am 100% convinced that I will buy a pair in every color and throw all of my current leggings away. All of the issues I listed above are nonexistent with these leggings. The waistband is not lined so it doesn't give unflattering shape and it goes above the bellybutton very comfortably. These leggings have the PERFECT amount of stretch and DO NOT STRETCH OUT while working out which seems to always be an issue. They contour to your body amazingly and have those details that make your butt look perfect. They are super comfy. The fact that I got the grey colored leggings and they barely show any sweat is unreal. I have worn these heavy lifting, to cycle class, and more and they have definitely held up with any exercise. I have already washed them a couple of times (on delicate/gentle heat) and they still are good as new. Any way, this is my first and probably last review of anything I have ever bought from anywhere but I just had to share how much I love these leggings because I know there are lots of other women out there who were just as frustrated with finding the perfect leggings as I was. For $27, you cannot do any better.

Some great and okay qualities! Just received the product today. I got a size small and I'm 5'2.5" 128 Ibs size 27" waist. I did have to size down from my typical medium size. I own a few gymshark leggings. These fit just right, 7/8 length and comes right above my belly button. The elastic part of the waist is nice and compressive however I wish the rest of the material was the same way. Right below the elastic my lower abdomen tends to pop out a little more because the rest of the leggings are not compressive like the waist. The material itself is a little thinner than what I had expected, but it is soft and not uncomfortable. Because of this, I'm not sure how well these leggings will hold up in the washer, weightlifting and core exercises. I will try a few washes and wears and give my second opinion after. Futher, the small dotted holes are not actually holes, they are darker dots printed onto the pants. Overall, fit is great and accentuates the butt area. Very flattering! For the price, I did expect a slightly thicker material with a bit more compression -just because there are other leggings on Amazon that are <$29. If you are not looking for too much compression or looking to wear these for light training, highly recommend. Rated 3/5 for now because of material, if they hold up well in the long run will give 4/5 rating! UPDATE: decided to return these. material for the front end is too thin for me, and gives you camel toe. UPDATE 2: i was contacted by the seller. I received a second pair from a list of suggestions (i got khaki grey marl) and was told that these would be more compressive and have less camel toe. Upon trying them on, they were more compressive but they still did have camel toe. I suspect that even though i like the tighter fit I should size up to a medium to avoid camel toe. Otherwise, great customer service, seller seems to really care about its customers!! 4/5 stars.

Size comparison: M vs L Squat Proof. These leggings are soft, a good mix between an align and a wunder under. Good for the gym, walks, pilates etc but I got them for the gym. Now I’m 5’5, 170 and I carry my weight moreso in my lower half. Usually a medium across the board, sometimes in strechier leggings I can do a small. I initially ordered these in a M Blueberry and they felt tight! I wanted good compression for a flattering fit but these felt a little too tight so I ordered a L. I was also bloated the first day I tried them so I figured I’d just compare. The L came and they were sooo soft, so comfortable that I could probably take a nap in them. Not what you typically want in a gym legging lol. They had less compression and I noticed neither pair completely smoothed me out in the front. The M fit a little better on this day since I was less bloated but still somewhat bloated. They felt tight but I figured they were a way better fit between the two. The L also flattened my booty and the M made it look great, I guess the compression did a little lifting in that area. I ended up returning the L right away since they are definitely too big, and I’m debating returning the M too because maybe these are just not for me? I want something that smooths and flatters and the compression on these wasn’t as much as I was hoping for. The waistband on both pairs was restricting so if you’re expecting or TTC, these will not grow with the bump much despite the legs being super stretchy just FYI. Both waistbands rolled down a little when I was trying them on, especially with the L, but I don’t think that’s a deal breaker for weight lifting and light cardio. Would not recommend for running.

Quality, Practical, Flattering I am 5'7", 165lbs, long legs and torso. Usually wear medium in shorts and leggings in other brands like gymshark but size up to a large for this brand but I might consider a medium next time to help them stay up better. I have these in the bike short version and they are my favorite shorts to wear under my uniform for Brazilian Jiu-jitsu because they stay in place and comfortable. After a year and mannny washings they are still holding up perfectly! These leggings are long enough have a perfect high-rise that is slightly higher in the back so there is no gapping. If you wanted a lower rise you can fold down the waistband. They have no front seam and have a long crotch gusset, so full range of movement, no wedgies/camel toe. The fabric is very soft and stretchy and not sheer at all so definitely squat proof. They fit like a second skin, not too much compression but keep their shape and elasticity over long term use. I only have them in black and they do not show sweat much. I love them for lifting weights and for just lounging around. Definitely recommending to my friends and family! This brand has great knock offs!

Nice Lightweight Pants For Travel. Love The Color. I got these cargo/travel pants for my wife. I selected the Green/Grey color which was a nice earthy and natural tone. The stitching and zippers are good quality. She is 5'3" 135 lbs and we found the medium size fit her well. The pants are fitted at the hip with a looser style fit in the leg. The pants were not too long for her(so if you are tall these might not be for you). There are 4 zippered pockets - 2 slash pockets on the either side of the hip and 2 cargo pockets further down on the thigh. The cargo pockets will be convenient for carrying a phone or other items securely. The material is lightweight and dries quickly. Only drawback is the swishy noise when you walk. That is common to this type of pants. Overall a good lightweight pant for travel.

Super Comfy! I am absolutely loving these sweater pants. They are wide legged, high waisted, stretchy, and comfortable as can be.
