Silky soft and warm knit pants with a bit of style. These straight leg, business / casual pants are quite comfortable. They are made of a super soft knit material that is a little fuzzy too. I got the X-Large Petite size and they fit well around my waist, but were a little shorter than I prefer on leg length. I am 5' 4", and often pants are too long. Since they had the petite sizes I went with that, but I guess I am borderline between sizes for the sizing of these pants. According to the chart and X-Large in regular would have 42.1" legs and I know those would be too long. Sort of a pickle on that one, lol. They are well made with solid stitching. The pleat down the front gives them a more stylish look for wearing away from home as well as lounging at home. They have a drawstring for waist adjustment, if needed. There are no pockets, though. With the thickness of the material, I thought they would be very warm, but the knit material must breath well as I wore them for 15 minutes or more while doing a couple things in the house and they were just as warm as the thin pants I had on before trying these on. They arrived in a zip bag and folded with them was a piece of tissue paper. Not sure why, but the tissue paper left tiny (pin-head sized) pieces of white here and there on the pants. The paper looked fine with no tears, but somehow it shed onto the pants, lol. Just mentioning as they were very visible on the black pair of pants I got. Over all, I'm recommending these knit pants. They are of a nice quality knit material and well made. Only caution I will say is study the size chart and hopefully the sizes will work better for the next person!

👠 Elongated Crotch - Runs True To Size For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

Size up if you want that low-slung fit. My new favorite "I don't want to wear pants" pants The instant I tried these on, I knew I had found my new favorite pants for the days when, no matter how we may try to fight it, wearing pants is necessary. First, I'm 5'3" so finding joggers that aren't half a mile too long is a bit of a unicorn all on its own. Usually even the ones that have elasticized ankles will have fabric bunched up and puddling on top of my feet, which really is just more kinds of attractive than I can find words for. These fit true to the size chart in every measure provided. The fabric is like fleece and cotton jersey had a baby. Think the heaviest heavyweight t-shirt you've ever had, and add like 12% more heft to that fabric. So if you're looking for a pair of fleece sweatpants, these are not the pants you're looking for. But if you, like me, enjoy something a bit lighter and more breathable and just generally less there than fleece while it's still providing coziness, you will probably love these pants as much as I do. I have a 29" waist and 40ish" hips and I went with a Large to make sure they were a bit loose. The fabric has enough stretch to it that you could size down for a slimmer fit and still be able to move. The elastic waist and drawstring mean you can also size up if you want more of a "walking around in a blanket but not tripping over it because the universe loves me and wants me to be happy" fit. With pockets actually big enough that your phone isn't constantly falling out. So I will be grabbing these in at least one other color. Possibly four. That's the future and I know not what it holds. All I know is, I love these pants and think there's a good chance you may also love these pants.

High waisted and baggy! Yea.... I can see how these would make one feel like MC Hammer. These are definitely ultimate lounge pants. I measure 32-24-36 and ordered the xs which is my normal size. The waistband is indeed very tight, it only fits best way up on my waist. The crotch is super super long. Don't even bother rolling these bad boys down either because you will have created one huge spare tire around your hips. Big enough to keep people out of your personal bubble though, so maybe that's a good thing. The material is very thick and warm which I absolutely love because I am always cold. Its soft on the inside. The pockets are nice as usual. After seeing how big these pants were when I opened the package I though maybe I could use them for period week pants, but because the waistband is so tight, I don't honestly know if that would give me the relief I'd be looking for. These feel pretty sturdy though and as long as I don't gain any inches right below my ribs I should be able to wear them for a long time.

really long rise, consider sizing down Maybe I should have sized down. I am 5’4” 115 lbs and my measurements say I should be a small on the size chart, but these pants were very large on me. The most surprising thing was how long the rise is (13”). I have a long torso and this looked too long on me. I didn’t see high rise anywhere in the description. The extra long rise was almost enough to roll the waistband down once. The waistband is quite wide though. Also the waist band was too big at nearly 28” around before stretching the elastic. According to the size chart, should have been a size medium measurement. The length was 30” long. The pockets easily accommodated my I phone mini; however, if you wear the pants as high rise, the dimensions no longer allow for sitting while keeping the phone in your pocket. The pants are lightweight and the color was as pictured. The material would be good for warmer days (60+ degrees Fahrenheit). The fabric makes it tempting to order a size XS and hem the length, but if the rise is only slightly smaller that becomes too much to be worth altering.

29" are too short for 5'7", 32" are a bit long I am 5'7", and the 29" (blue) are a bit short for me, at least to wear with sneakers. The 32" alternative (wine red in the picture) were initually too long, and I ended up folding the bottoms before the first wash. For my legs, 30-31" would be ideal. If you plan to wear these pants for yoga practice, then the 29" is good length. I think after a warm wash the 32" wine pants became OK, still a tiny bit long, but don't require folding anymore and don't sweep the ground, just touch it in sneakers. The pants are pretty thick and a bit compressing, comparing to a lighter version I have from another brand. Love the shape of the front, and deep side pockets. The wine color works great with blue (bright, light and sky), or pink/light purple.

Warm and comfy; LOVE the zippered pockets! These are awesome leggings! They are warm and very comfortable, with a wide waistband that doesn't roll down, or dig into my skin. They help hold my jiggly belly in, without feeling constrictive. The zippered pockets are nice and deep (large enough to hold my cellphone), and I like the extra pocket on the back (it is quite large, and can even fit my oversized Galaxy S22 Ultra). I haven't yet worn them in wet conditions, but did splash them with water to see if they were actually water resistant as claimed, and the water beaded up without soaking into the fabric, so it seems they are. These are NOT see-through, and the black is a true, deep black. They are full-length leggings, and on my body go past my ankles. For size reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 165 pounds, have a short torso with a flat derriere and big stomach, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 x 32 (medium leggings and pants at Old Navy and H&M). I ordered these leggings in a medium, and they fit perfectly (see my pics). My one minor complaint about these leggings is the small reflective stripe on the lower sides of the legs. I would like to wear these not just as active wear, but also under my winter dresses and sweaters, and the stripe makes them look too sporty. Overall, these are very comfortable, warm leggings. I am happy with the fit and quality, and would not hesitate to order from this company again.

Fits well, but too long for a 5’4” curvy woman These leggings are butter smooth, soft inside and out, and comfy snug fitting. Like most leggings and pants for me, they are a bit too long and they gathered at my ankles a bit. I love all of the pockets and I especially love the rear pocket. I plan to wear these hiking in cooler to colder temperatures, so the length does not bother me.

Highly recommend!

Warm Winter Pants This review is for the "BALEAF Women's Thick Fleece Lined Winter Leggings..." These heavy warm pants will be just the thing for winter hikes in the metro parks this winter. I'm 5'4" 145LB and size medium fit well. The fabric has a sheen and water resistance that will be good if there is snow. They have a key loop and hip pocket for phone.

Buy these! I’m not a yoga/workout pants person due to not liking how tight to my leg they typically are. However, I thought I’d try these and I’m in love! I put them on for the first time this morning and am very happy I bought them. I’m 5’5” and usually wear a size 14 bottom. I bought these in xl and the fit is perfect to my shape. They’re soft and seem to be pretty sturdy. Haven’t washed them yet but I think they’ll hold up great.

Super comfy! I bought more! Love, love the waist design on these! Super comfy if you've got a belly and flattering. The fleece lining is pretty thin so these aren't super warm but warmer than no lining. The flare bottoms obviously let in some cold air. I'm always cold, so these help me at 40 degrees, but any colder and they would not be warm enough for me. I live in Colorado. I would buy again. I really wish they came in some fun colors, like dark purple or fushia! Update: A year later and I've bought 4 more pairs of these and I'm still a BIG fan. Very comfy. I think I'll spend most of winter wearing these in the house. I gained weight so I had to buy new ones in XL. If you carry your weight in the middle, I feel the waist can be a tad tight. I'm 5'4 and 165 pounds. I'm 5'4 and I feel like the 29" length is perfect, which makes me think anyone taller might find them too short. But I'm grateful they aren't dragging like so many brands! One complaint now that I've bought 5 pairs. 2 pairs were perfect. But 3 pairs had what I consider a flaw in the stitching in the right cargo pocket that made the material kind of ripple or pucker. One pair was bad enough I returned it. I decided to live with it in the other two because it's not that noticeable. One last concern. I think the "wine red" is the ugliest color pant I've ever owned. Lol. I should have returned it but that's just personal preference. I like a burgundy to veer toward purple more than brown. This is more of a rust, depending on the light. I've attached a couple photos to show the color in different light. And also showing the length on my shoe. Fits my 5'4 perfectly. The rust colored ones didn't have the stitching flaw so I kept them even tho I hate the color because they are so comfortable!

Comfortable postpartum work pant! Fabric is thick and warm. Definitely squat proof if they are the correct size for you. Flexible for active jobs or postpartum/pregnancy. There is a seam to hold the criscross front in place. I’m 5’4 130 wearing size medium because I have muscular thighs I like a looser fit. I work at a gym so I want to look professional and ordered a khaki color so I’m not always dressed in black. I took off a star because these do show panty lines but these are comfortable enough to go commando or thong. The seam at the crotch can become uncomfortable so make sure it does not fit you too tight there. Keeping these and ordering another in another color.

The best! Very comfy/warm I ordered a small and they fit very well. I am 125 pounds and 5’3 for reference. By no means do I have the flattest stomach and it sucks my tummy right in comfortably. They are fleece lined and soft on the inside. There are pockets as well. Buy them!

Smooth, stretchy material but size up! I received the BALEAF Womens Fleece Lined Flare Leggings in Black. I am normally a size medium, however I ordered a size large as I already own a pair of BALEAF Leggings in Large that fit well. Thank god I ordered up because these are form fitting leggings except for the flare leg. The flare leg is not a tiny flare leg, but it is more pronounced than I expected. I am 140 lbs and 5’7” and the length is perfect! Even though these are form fitted, the material is so smooth and stretchy, so it does not feel uncomfortable. I love the side pockets and there is a flap that is sewed on each end that covers the opening to the pockets to really hold in all your valuables like a phone or money. The crisscross waistband is so comfortable and stylish if you tuck your shirt in. These are warm fleece pants so they are ideal for walking or hiking in the colder months, and it’s a bonus they are water resistant too! They would probably be too warm to wear to the gym or do indoor activities. The only negative I have about these pants is they are tight fitting so it shows your underwear lines. Definitely size up!

I am 140 lbs and 5’7” and the length is perfect! Even though these are form fitted, the material is so smooth and stretchy, so it does not feel uncomfortable. I love the side pockets and there is a flap that is sewed on each end that covers the opening to the pockets to really hold in all your valuables like a phone or money. The crisscross waistband is so comfortable and stylish if you tuck your shirt in. These are warm fleece pants so they are ideal for walking or hiking in the colder months, and it’s a bonus they are water resistant too! They would probably be too warm to wear to the gym or do indoor activities. The only negative I have about these pants is they are tight fitting so it shows your underwear lines. Definitely size up!

Cute and Comfy! I’m 5’4”, 205 pounds, with a 36 inch waist and 46” inch hips. I normally wear a 12-14 in pants and bought an XL for these and found that they fit true to size. Over all seem like a well made, warm, waterproof pant that was reasonably priced that I'll be returning. Hope this helps! :D

Very nice quality, do run long. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and a size L. I have had a lot of experience with Baleaf running small, so I sized up to an XL in these. The XL fit great around the waist, butt, and thighs but was longer than expected. I normally wear a 30-31" inseam and as you can see from the pictures the inseam on these was longer than that.These are amazing quality. The sweatpant material is nice and thick with a super soft fleece inside. The seam details are beautifully done. The pockets are nice and deep. Everything about these screams quality and they look and feel very high end.They didn't show any shrinkage or pilling after washing and feel amazing on. I would highly recommend and think they are worth the price. If you are shorter I would be cautious, these run very long. I like the extra length, but I am on the taller side at 5'7" with a 30.5" inseam.Highly recommended.

Thermal pants! I get cold super easily, so I was excited to try these thermal pants. They are so warm and comfortable! They are basically a stretchy legging material on the outside, but they have a thin but very warm inner fleece lining that makes them very cozy. I love that they have several well-placed pockets. Since I have long legs I wish they were maybe 1/2-inch longer but I think the length will be good for most people. As far as fit goes, they do fit in my usual size but are definitely more slim-fitting than my other Baleaf pants (in the jogger style) in the same size. So consider sizing up if you are between sizes. For reference, I am 5'9" around 160lb and got a Large.

Perfect travel pants These pants are fantastic. I've been a Baleaf fan for years, and am impressed with how long their products hold up and how thoughtfully designed their pants and shorts are. For reference, I am 5'6", size 16W bottoms. Both the xl and xxl fit me, but I personally prefer a slightly looser fit so I went with xxl. As with their other shorts and pants, the pockets are nice and deep. This style has two front pockets and one deeper zippered pocket on the right thigh. That pocket is big enough to hold my large samsung ultra in it's case. The fabric is lightweight, but not thin enough to see dimpled skin under it. At 5'6", the xxl comes down to almost the bottom of my heel, but not long enough to touch the floor when barefoot. I'm happy that the bottoms are straight (not flared), yet are wide enough to roll up and still have wiggle room around my plus sized calves. These pants are great for travel, since they don't wrinkle easily, stretch nicely to move with you whilehiking, & dry quickly. They are not "noisy" pants. The zippers are the same color as the pants, so at first glance they don't scream "hiking pants". Because of that, you can get away with wearing them with a dressier top for dinners out, which really makes them multi-functional.

Just buy the pants! Form fitting with no pudge The color was right on with the image. The pants are a slightly thicker jegging like material. Form fitting and moveable! I am a midi size so I need plus’s size hips but a form fitting waist and this absolutely did it! No baggyness on the crotch and not too tight on the legs. The button does seem a bit fake like a child’s plastic snap but it’s covered by my shirt anyways. JUST BUY THE PANTS! I wear a size 2XL am 5”7

Love how they look and feel. Very happy I have been looking for a pair of pants like this for a long time. I am 5’4” and ordered the Large 29”. They are perfect and I received many compliments on them today. Also the shirt is from Amazon and it made a nice outfit. I love the material, look, and feel. The outfit is very comfortable and stretch in all the right spots.

Fits good I got the straight leg khaki pants in a medium. I’m 5”2 and 147 lbs and the pants fit well. I like the way they feel. I just don’t like the front pockets because the pants are so thin that you can see the lining of the pockets. I wear these pants with long shirts so you can’t see the pockets. I still like these pants because they are very comfortable.

She Loves Baleaf I got these pants in size medium for my wife to try. She is 5'3 and 135 lbs. Other than being a tad too long on her short legs, these pants fit her very well. They are a pull on pant with no zipper. The fabric is fairly thick and have a nice soft lining inside, so they are cozy pants for cooler weather. Because of the moderate amount of stretch they would still be good for fitness activities. The grey color is a nice neutral option to black. Since these pants have belt loops, a mock "fly" , 2 zippered pockets on the back and slash pockets on the front, they look a bit dressier than simple leggings making them a comfortable option for work or travel. She loves these and I think they look great on her as well.

Who you Hiking with? BALEAF, duh I purchased the BALEAF Women's Hiking Pants Quick Dry Lightweight Water Resistant Elastic Waist Cargo Pants/Black/Tall/Large for my trip to Costa Rica (July 21-26 2024). I WISH I HAD PURCHASED MORE THAN ONE PAIR! The pants were comfortable to wear and easy to pack. The pants kept my legs dry during the two mile trek through the rain forest, pouring rain included. They allowed for plenty of movement as I awkwardly climbed over lava rocks, during our volcano hike. Quick dry? Yes! I washed my pants after each hike and hung them on the line to dry. The pants would be dry by the next morning! For reference, I'm 5'7" and 168lbs (not curvy on the bottom half, lol) The Tall/Large fit a little tighter than I wanted. My second order of the BALEAF Women's Hiking Pants Quick Dry Lightweight Water Resistant Elastic Waist Cargo Pants will be in the size XL/Tall.

My New Favorite Hiking Pants At 5'7" and 135-140 pounds, these Baleaf hiking pants fit comfortably. I can sit, bend, and crouch easily because the fabric stretches. There is a drawstring waist, which is forgiving for women with a bit of a tummy, as well as four zippered pockets: two slash pockets, two cargo pockets on the side of the leg, and one rear pocket. All of the zippers move smoothly; I was even able to operate them with one hand while hiking. The cargo pockets are deep enough to hold my iPhone 15 Pro and still zip closed. There are drawstrings at the cuff. Water beaded up on the pants when I got caught in a light rain shower while hiking. The fabric is very lightweight and comfortable, washes well and hang dries quickly. I haven't had an opportunity to test abrasion resistance yet. I'll probably buy additional pairs of these pants.

Lightweight, comfortable, great fit Ordered these pants last minute for a trip to Costa Rica. They were very comfortable for hiking. Lightweight. Worked fantastically for the transition from hot, humid to cool, rainy. I was very surprised. Loved the zippered pockets and the drawstring ankles that I could use to convert the pants to capris. I am 5’1, 109lbs and ordered the XS. Highly recommend these for warm/hot weather hiking.

Just Buy The Pants! I bought two pair (in grey and black) of these pants for a 3 week backpacking trip in New Zealand. These pants were perfect for the variable weather conditions, and withstood rainstorms and tramping through mud pits. They were super comfortable, regardless of how cold (as low as 2 degrees fahrenheit) or how hot (~75 degrees fahrenheit). I love the breathable netting, pockets, and adjustable ankles. As for sizing, I'm 5'3" and about 115lbs. I first ordered them in Small, which was way too baggy for me. I then exchanged for the XS, which was a much more flattering fit, with plenty of room left to be comfortable. Next time, I might try the petite XS, as these pants are slightly longer than ideal, so I have to wear extra high on my waist. I should also note that after hiking 100 miles in just these 2 pairs, I experienced zero chaffing the entire time. TLDR: Great pants for variable weather conditions, water resistance is excellent, consider ordering a size down, excellent quality for the price.

Have to wear as high rise or the crotch fabric irritates thighs I ordered the Baleaf in dark grey in a Medium. I’m 5’8”, ~120 lbs, usually a size 4 (My measurements from 2 years ago: Bra: 32A/B - 34 A/B, Waist: 65cm, Hips 92cm). Although they are cute, these are meant to be worn high waisted. When you do, they are comfy but the butt is less flattering. I prefer wearing my pants on my hips unless they are leggings in which I don’t mind them being high waisted. However, if I put the waist band at my hips, the crotch becomes low and the fabric rubs against my thighs and irritates my skin. Could I wear these high waisted? Yes. But Id like the option of being able not to. They do have some great features. The material is very lightweight and I’m sure would be quick to dry as advertised. The pockets are also very spacious and zippered (two hip pockets, one left thigh pocket, one right butt pocket). They also have drawstring cinching ankle cuffs and a drawstring to tie at the waist if you need extra support in addition to the elastic waistband.

Surpasses expectations I bought these pants as loungewear and to have something comfortable for traveling. They have surpassed my expectations. I have worn them several times and they wash up like a dream. They're lightweight but feel substantial. They'll pack well when going on trips. They're a little like parachute pants from the 80's but are a bit thinner and not quite as stiff. As for fit, I am 5'5" and weigh around 215. I bought the XL and they fit beautifully. I plan to buy more, definitely recommend! Edit: I purchased another pair if these pants and there's apparently a problem with their sizing. The first pair I bought was a size XL and fit perfectly. The second pair, size XL, were snug. They also had a flaw in the stitching. I did an exchange and received the 2nd pair, which were also snug. I returned them, again, and ordered a pair of XXL. I received them today and they're marked 3XL. I'll be keeping this pair because although they're a little big, it's better than snug. So, I suppose the very first pair I bought (steel gray) were perfect, and this second pair (suntan color) has been a struggle.

A+ Water Proof Outdoor Pants, Hands Down The Best! Love these pants. Absolutely waterproof, and well made. The best I've ever purchased for outdoors/kayaking. Water all over front legs and It never came through the pants. These pants dried quickly. Black Pant Size Med. Petite 27" L 132lbs 5'3"

Great pants! After getting bitten by mosquitos, gnats, and no see'ums when I am out gardening, I looked for some long pants made out of the quick dry, cooling fabric. I am short, 5'3, and weigh 166. The legs are long on me but with the draw cord are fine. I have large thighs also, but the large fit me perfectly. They wash well and dry quickly. They are no hotter than shorts, and if wet, they feel even cooler, so when I get really hot I will get the hose out and wet my clothes to cool off. Summers are really hot on the Georgia coast and the bugs are vicious, but with these and my mosquito netting under my cap and my long sleeve shirt I don't have to slather down in sunscreen and bug spray, and I don't get all scratched when I work. When I ride my bike the air just seems to go right through them, keeping me even cooler than the shorts I used to wear. Highly recommend them.

Comfortable, lightweight,perfection These fit absolutely perfect, you could use these for yoga with no issue, they are not restricting in any of the “problem areas” us ladies have like thighs and buttocks. I am a slender build but still have enough junk in my trunk to testify:) the length hits perfect on me to cover my ankles and the very top of my hiking boots. I did see a lot of people comment on the length being too short. If you wear an extra small but 5’7 or taller these may be too short. I purchased for an upcoming 3 day hiking trip so I have not tested the water resistance but I am confident I will continue to love them. Highly recommend so far

Tall girl approved - Comfy and functional These are exactly what I needed - cute, comfy and functional - for travel days and lounging. I was skeptical because there wasn’t much info on fit for taller women, but I’m comfy with the length of these. I was actually looking for a slightly cropped/ankle cut so these are perfect. For reference, I’m 5’9 and my inseam is 32/33in - These stop just above my ankle when I wear them at mid rise on my waist. Great Exercise Pants for Curvy Women These pants are great for women with thick thighs and a bubble booty. The drawstring waist takes care of any gap, but the elastic waistband does great on it's own for hugging your waistline and not having gap. This is a pant that is great for hourglass shapes. The pants stretch just enough after wearing them for a couple hours to give you a little more room in the thigh. They are very comfortable, pajama pant level of comfort. They are thin, so panty lines and lumps are visible. With a darker color, that may help hide imperfections. They are great for working out in so far. Hopefully, they will be great in the summer months outdoors too. I ordered an XL in this pant. I'm happy with the size. I'm in between a 14 and 16 size pant. (16 is more comfortable for me in the butt and thigh, but the 14 fits better at the waist) I'm 5'9". The length seems perfect for me.

BUY THEM! Wind & rain proof! Super lightweight, BUT worked like magic. I Might Be a Little Too Short for These.... I like that these pants have 5 pockets (3 that zip). The fabric feels nice and is comfortable to wear. Unfortunately, I just don't care for the length of these pants on my 5'3" frame. The pants are two long to be a 3/4 pant and too short to be a full-length pant. They sit on me more like an ankle pant but because the pant does not fit close to my ankle, I'm just not a fan of how the bottom of the pant looks on me.

PERFECT stretchy pants! LOVE these pants! These are EXACTLY what I was looking for. They’re stretchy, flattering and have REAL pockets, ladies. PERFECT for work and literally anything else since they’re so comfortable and flattering. I usually wear a medium, but took everyone’s advice from the comments and sized up to a large. For reference I’m about 5’5 and 136 pounds and usually wear an either 6 or 8. Don’t spend hours on amazon looking and preparing just get these.

My favorite! These were kind of an accidental purchase, they’d been in the cart to be considered and ended up overlooked and bought, and I’m so glad. They are my favorite cozy pants. I have a different pair of similar pants and I far prefer these because of the way the waist elastic feels. They are light and have held up to my shenanigans for a few months now. I personally like to wear them lower, but you could really pull these up quite a lot. I’m 5’2 and a little less than 145lb. This is the fit with them tucked into my knee high DMs A few notes: -no pockets. There is a linen-y feeling liner that I’d assume is to keep the sheer nature of the fabric under control. It’s a perfect opportunity for a big, baggy pocket, but instead it’s just some extra white fabric that drapes around the hips under the elastic gathered bit (which is approx as long down as my hand is from heel to the tip of my middle finger) and it’s disappointing. It is easy to just put your phone in the elastic and for me at least it holds relatively well for just walking around the house and baking and whatnot, though I wouldn’t trust it if I were to do something outside. -there is an odd seam somewhere near below the knee that makes these really difficult to just pull on, your foot gets caught in it. I end up having to sit to fix it. It’s understandable to create the shape but I feel it could be adjusted to preserve the silhouette and feel and also be easier to pull on. -the ankle is very small. I wear knee high boots so I wear large socks as well and I absolutely have to put the socks on before the pants because I physically can’t pull the ankle back up more than about halfway up my shin. -I exclusively wear these as pants and the strings that are intended to go around the neck are really annoying. I guess I could just snip them, but I think it would be nice to have them maybe clip on? Like an adjustable bra strap, a wide hook and loop, or something similar. -my favorite thing about these is that they’re very light and stay off my skin, but if you’re trying to have pants that are warm or feel like you’re actually wearing pants, these don’t do that.

Paper thin I was excited to try this when the price dropped by $2. Took a chance and placed an order. Overall I like the way they look. I’m 5”8 and I can’t wear it as a dress. Too short. That’s fine. The waistbstrings whic can be used as a neck strap when worn as a rompers isn’t tighten around the waist. Overall I’m happy but the fabric is too thin you can see through depending on the lighting.

Design needs improvement I ordered your Bangkok harem style pans. I love the style but there are 2 major flaws in the way they made. 1.They are advertised the way that you can pull them up to your chest and over your boobs. It is not the main reason why I bought them, but I still would like to provide a feedback. I am 150 lbs, 5.7 tall and I was not able to do that. Because it would not pull up my caves . This pants are so tight around the bottom of my legs that I can’t even go halfway up.. But as I said that was not the reason why I bought the pants, I just really like the look of them. 2. This flaw is more major and pretty much a deal breaker for me. I do love the material, and I love how thin these pants are and I don’t mind that it’s see-through at all. But why in the world the inside material is the different color from the pants?! My pants are black and the under skirt is white. And because the pants material is see-through everyone can see the white under skirt. That would be fine if it was black, but it’s white and you can see that it’s white. And it’s so much more noticeable during yoga practice in a well-lit room than it is in my picture. That’s to me is just not putting enough consideration and care into making a quality product. So please please please make the under skirts the same color as the rest of the pants. The way it is right now males it look very silly and cheap

Not good for people taller than 5'6", but I love the stripes I'm 5'6" and 129# average...the pants have a thick waistband to serve as a jumpsuit as well. Una prenda cómoda y de buena calidad, llegó antes de lo previsto, el diseño es tal cual la imagen y el ajuste es genial para tallas de 28 a 32 aprox. También es genial que incluyan un recuerdo tan inusual 🦂 lástima que no llegó vivo. Pero cuenta la intención, le doy un 10 de 10.

Fun to wear I have a similar pair of pants, so I was familiar with how they can be worn -- with either the tie in the front or back. I prefer to initially tie the pants in the front, and then bring panel between my legs to the front, and tie them in the back -- especially with the pattern of these pants. It looks more finished with the pattern unobstructed by the tied. However you choose to tie these pants, they're nicely made with lightweight rayon and all the edges seamed. The instructions say to hand-wash in cool water and not tumble dry. I'm 5'2" and a bit round (size 10). You can see the pants reach the floor, and the straps are long enough to tie around my waist, in the front and back. They would be shorter on a taller person. I'm looking forward to wearing them over a bathing suit or after I get out of a hot tub... or when out-and-about on a warm day. They're light enough to not feel hot, yet provides protection from the sun. The label also says, "carbon neutral," which is cool. I'm guessing the factory where the pants are sewn is environmentally friendly.

Very nice pants! Ordered size 2x. I'm 5'10" 230 lbs and they fit. I was afraid they would be too tight around my pudgy waist but the shirring is just right. I usually can't stand elastic around my waist but these are comfortable. They are just a little bit too short, showing quite a bit of ankle, but for this type of pants that's OK and it looks good. They do not have in-seam pockets, just a patch pocket on the side. You can wear them backward or forward to put the pocket on whichever side you prefer, but having no other pockets for my keys and wallet mean they are not good for me to wear anywhere requiring keys or wallet. The print is beautiful. I looked over them and they look like they are put together well. They are supposed to be made of bamboo rayon, a fabric I know nothing about. They are not sheer at all. The weight of the fabric is like a thin silk. They feel dressy. I would be hesitant to wear them casually like to work or school (where jeans would be appropriate), but they would be great for around the house where they won't get a lot of action or alternately to something where you stand around looking pretty. They definitely feel like something you would want to hand wash. However, they are lightweight so they will dry quickly just on a hangar. I bought several different kinds but this one came in today. Will update if there is any issue. My mirror has a scratch across it. Sorry if it makes the photos look weird. Normally I eschew having my picture on the internet but I found it very to see the fit from another buyer. I have a weight loss goal and these pants will fit as I work toward it, so that's a big plus. (pun intended)

Buy the pants-No buy 2 I am 5'8 tall and weigh somewhere between 175-180 lbs. My height is due to my longs legs and a shorter torso. My weight for the most part, I believe to be pretty much evenly distributed. I usually wear size 8-10 jeans, or Med-Large spandex or athletic stretch pant, or a size 10-12 in a suit. I purchased size L and they are so roomy without being HUGE and puffy! I consider myself to be a large woman, and when I put on big items I feel it makes me look bigger than I already am, so I tend to wear my clothes more so fitted, not skin tight, but "fitted"... These Harem Hippie, Boho-Yoga or Palazzo pant ties comfortably above my waist ( belly button), full coverage in the back with plenty of room bending over, doing squats, or home. The length lays relaxed below my ankle complete with elastic to keep pants off the ground. I will order another pair in a small size (just a little more fitted than before) and wear a cute ankle strap or wedge slide shoe, with a solid color fitted top or crop top, when I finally go out with Bae for fun!***Please note*** I opened this package and they smelled horrible. I could clearly see they were perfect and new, but the chemical processing must be gassy or sulphur maybe? Idk.... The instruction says to hand wash in cold. My weight for the most part, I believe to be pretty much evenly distributed. I usually wear size 8-10 jeans, or Med-Large spandex or athletic stretch pant, or a size 10-12 in a suit. I purchased size L and they are so roomy without being HUGE and puffy! I consider myself to be a large woman, and when I put on big items I feel it makes me look bigger than I already am, so I tend to wear my clothes more so fitted, not skin tight, but "fitted"... These Harem Hippie, Boho-Yoga or Palazzo pant ties comfortably above my waist ( belly button), full coverage in the back with plenty of room bending over, doing squats, or home. The length lays relaxed below my ankle complete with elastic to keep pants off the ground. I will order another pair in a small size (just a little more fitted than before) and wear a cute ankle strap or wedge slide shoe, with a solid color fitted top or crop top, when I finally go out with Bae for fun!***Please note*** I opened this package and they smelled horrible. I could clearly see they were perfect and new, but the chemical processing must be gassy or sulphur maybe? Idk.... The instruction says to hand wash in cold. I machined washed cold with like colors, and hung them dry. The material did not shrink, fade, colors did not run and the stitching held perfect.

I feel like I’m not wearing pants! These are probably the most inexpensive pants ever.....and the most comfortable!! They are so comfortable I literally feel like I’m not wearing pants! These are my new favorite lounging pants, and they definitely passed the sleep test. They are great for summer. I’m sure you could wear leggings under them for cooler seasons. I usually wear a size xl in pants...I’m 5’6” and 187 pounds and I carry all my weight in my hips thighs and butt. The XL was perfect! They should fit baggy like pictured. Otherwise I’m sure you might get an embarrassing butt rip while moving around in public. Honestly if these pants don’t last long, I wouldn’t be too bent about it. I mean I would be out $17.00...but they seem sturdy enough that if you follow care instructions they should hold up. Minus 1 star because they have to be hand washed and line dry otherwise they will shrink and fall apart. Kind of a double edge sword there, because if they weren’t so delicate, they wouldn’t be nearly as comfortable...so hand wash needed for this level of comfort. Did I mention that I feel like I’m not wearing pants?!

Getting ready to order another pair!!! I love, love, LOVE!! These pants!! I pretty much always have these boho, breezy pants as pjs because they are also perfect for running errands! I am 5'3, 126lbs and ordered the medium. It fit perfect; maybe even a little big depending on how tight or loose you like your clothing. I like mine a little baggy and comfy and the medium was perfect for that. As you can see the elastic band is very forgiving and doesn't really cut into your skin like some. I was also hesitant about the bands on the ankle but they are super loose so you barely even feel them against your skin, but they also keep the pant hem from rising up. Keep in mind that there are care instructions included with the package. Since these pants are handmade you should only hand wash them. I did throw mine in the washer and hung them to dry and they were fine, just may wear faster that way. And my favorite part of these pants- the pocket!! Deep enough to fit my hand, and roomy enough for a cell phone or such.

Super comfy! A little long but love them! Bought 3 pairs and they are SO comfy. I originally ordered XS (I'm usually an XS to S and other barco smalls were too big on me) but the XS were tight. Exchanged for a S and they are perfect (pictures are from the XS order, not S.) My only issue with my first order is that I ordered 2 pairs of blue and 1 pair of black bottoms. I posted pictures so you can see, but one pair of blue drags on the ground and the other pair is too short?? I compared the tags and they were identical. Both regulars. The shorter pair had to have been mis-marked and actually a petite because the black pair is the same size/fit as the longer blue ones. Thought this was a little odd. I’d assume this isn’t a common occurrence, but I’d suggest definitely trying on ALL of them before you cut tags and wash them. Once I exchanged for a small they are awesome and all the same length! They do run a bit long so if you’re in between petite and regular, I’d go with petite. I’m 5’6’’ and a solid regular and found them to be a little long. With tennis shoes on they are okay though. Overall, I’d recommend these pants!

Faux leather, actual comfort I'm fairly certain this sort of garment is ordered for looks, not comfort. I'm weird that I want both.After taking my measurements, I ordered a size up to ensure I'd still be able to breathe and not just be an uncomfortable sausage in a black pleather casing. This was a good call as they fit well without being suffocating.I'm about 5'8", and the length and hip to waist ratio are both appropriate - that's often a challenge, so I love when it works. The fabric is slick looking, which means it may not hide a variety of imperfections. (I saw imperfections, my partner drooled. Take from that what you will.)They're soft, pliable, and flexible enough that moving and sitting in them was easy without suffocating. The fit is close enough to look good, and not to where it feels like it will fall off. Overall, a good purchase that will be happily used.I initially rated these as a 4 after a quick try on - I'm raising it to a 5 after an evening's wear as they're more comfortable than I realized from a short time. They are quite warm. For me that's a plus, but it may not be in warmer climes and times.

The only reason I can't give these five stars is because I wanted them a little bit more elasticy around the waist to give a little more suck in power and because the sequins did rub a little and get caught on each other where my thighs rub together on the inside. I think the rubbing is a fix that you could probably craft with some sealant or maybe fabric adhesive. I got compliments on these all night long and I could have stayed in them for hours! I would say these are a must purchase for any party!!

They make you look much thicker. I think it's a combination of the lining and outer layer, but they do add some thickness which was not the look I wanted going into the new year 2. One the black pants, the white lining shows significantly at the top of the waist (which I would have covered with a belt), but also at the bottom of each leg (which I had no option to cover so this is the primary reason for returning them). 3. They're short... I'm 5'8" with a high waist. I was so excited that these actually did hit my waist (which is uncommon) but disappointed when I looked down and noticed how short the legs were on me... which means when I put shoes on they would be even shorter.... Whole these didn't work great for me (I opted to return them), I think they could be a hood option for someone shorter. They are comfortable and not itchy

Comfortable, very pretty, great fit I love how they look on and feel. Got a lot of compliments! I ordered them for Taylor Swift concert. The inside of my thighs close to my knees tho we’re snagging together and at one point got stuck and almost pulled a thread out which holds the sequins together. So I will be returning as I feel like they will not make it thru a 2nd wear without tearing. Great for a short one time occasion in my option. The sequins also rubbed my stomach and was a little scratchy. I wear a Medium and I’m 6’6 tall.
Fit -XL 21 weeks pregnant 208lb. Ordered an XL. Fit was great. Not itchy at all. Wore to a concert 6 + hours. Fun pants.

Fun, good looking 1-time use! These had a LOT of stretch and were SHORT with a saggy crotch area. Not sure if washing them will reset the material.

I want them in every color! I'm in love with these pants. My only complaint is that they are LONG!!! But other than that, they are flattering, sexy and FUN! I want them in every color they come in!
Pink Nik Loved the fit, quality & print. I’m 5’2”, 140 lbs. purchased a medium the length was perfect no heals needed. I definitely recommend purchasing them. I’m going to get another color.

Fit great! I bought these to wear to an Elton John concert. The fit was great, except they were a bit too long for me as I am only 5’4”. Super comfy These pants were great! Stayed on well with good thick lining. I am 5’5” and all leg and these pants are very long. Just something to keep in mind.

Disco Must I am over 200lb 5’9”, a tiny bit short on length, decently high waisted. The sequence stayed on well. I wore a body suit so the waistline sequence didn’t irritate my skin but once I did that I loved it!!!

Fun & Frilly Pants! The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

These pants are just as cute as depicted in the description photos. The material is a thin but opaque, lightweight fabric with a little texture that drapes beautifully and doesn't wrinkle easily. They are NOT linen, as some of the lines in the item description suggest. The bow in front is sewn to either side, and is not a drawstring, while the rest of the waist is smocked and very stretchy. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in the "large" size, which I think was the right fit for me (see my pics). While I don't think these are the most flattering pants for my apple shaped flabby stomach, they are super comfortable to wear with no pinching at the waist, and I can see myself wearing them often as soon as the weather warms up. Overall, these are cute, comfortable summer pants, and I'm happy with the quality. I would order from this brand again.

The pants is a little too long for me I’m 5.3 feet and 120 lbs, and I usually wear size M pants. For this pair of size M pants, the pants are a bit long for me. I have to wear thick-soled shoes to make them work, but they are very flattering and look great with various outfits. The only issue is that the pant legs tend to drag on the ground and get dirty.

Super comfortable and nicely made. These pants are as described and do not disappoint. My pics are very poor quality but it does show the approximate fit on my 5'3" 128 lb body. I wear a medium and could easily have worn that in this pant, but I wanted to be sure of a comfortable fit. The Large fits just fine. The elastic waist is not overly tight and the drawstring works for pulling the waist tighter if wanted. The wrap design provides full coverage. The length is just enough for a small wedge heel. The material has a luxurious feel and the pants have an overall high quality,chic look. I'll be wearing these frequently!

Cute and comfy! I love these pants!!! I ordered them for a trip to Amsterdam next month and I wanted pants other than jeans. I figured I'd give these a try because they seemed to get really great reviews and I found a pattern I love so why not give them a shot. When opened the package, the leg part looked a bit big and I thought they would look like Aladdin pants (LOL) but they don't seem so big when they're actually on. They're roomy but not too big and the material is light but comfy and soft-ish.I love how these come in a variety of prints/colors and are super easy to coordinate an outfit! I plan to wear these with a black tank or black tee and sandals (like I'm wearing in the photo) but I could totally see myself wearing a pair of flats or even heels to dress them up if I wanted to. I also think these would be comfy enough to wear all day while I'm sight-seeing, visiting museums, strolling around town, or going to a flea market.I typically dress in darker clothes but wanted something different, something funky for my trip to Amsterdam. I love bohemian, though I never thought I'd actually like these so much! It might be hard to tell in my photo but they are a perfect dark blue and black floral/mandala type pattern (the color is actually called "Mandala Blue"). The pattern looks exactly how I thought it would be (or is even better!) in person!For reference, I am 5'9" and about 180 pounds. The length of the pants is just right, a little above my ankles. Not too long and not too short even for a taller person. My only beef with these is that I think they look better high waisted however doing so gives me a little bit of camel toe which isn't ideal so I have to pull them down a bit more to my hips for them to be more comfortable (or maybe with a different tee or tank they could work better for me high waisted). I wish the waist part was a bit stretchier or longer, which is the only reason why I gave them 4 stars, but this is definitely not a deal breaker. I was more concerned about these being long enough for my liking and roomy in the leg/thigh area which they very much are.Aside from the high waisted fit thing (which is probably more on my body than the pants themselves), I am absolutely obsessed with these! I didn't expect to love them so much but I really do. They are effortless, super cute and versatile! I can't wait to wear these! I'm so glad I took a chance and tried something I might not have if it weren't for my trip. I will definitely be wearing these into the summer well after my trip to Europe! Thanks so much for a great item!Update: washed these once and I am noticing a few lines on these where the print has faded or the black bled through the fabric? Not sure whether I'm explaining right but they are definitely not wet spots from water as they're a straight line. I did not dry these in the dryer, I let them hang up to dry so I'm not sure why there is a blemish in the fabric. My OCD is killing me knowing these spots exist, but I don't think they would be noticeable to anyone else and I still love these pants.

These are my new favorite pants and as soon as I am done writing this review I am promptly ordering more in different colors. Easy to dress up or lounge around in. Fabric is perfect thickness for summer but it does wrinkle easy; nothing a trip through the dryer won’t fix. Says handwash (who has time for that?)but I threw it in a cold wash with the rest of my clothes and they fared well. I am 5’5” and 190lbs (and losing) and the large (10-16) fit perfect and I think they will still fit well as I lose more weight.

Great, Comfy, Relaxing Pants! Loved everything about these. They are thin and breeze goes right thru them which I loved vacationing in PR was hot and these pants were perfect. I don't wear shorts so I always looks for thin pants during the summer. They were a kittle shiny material at first but once u was them they are a regular shade. I ordered size 16-24 for a lose fit. I'm 5'7" weight 195lbs

Diggin' my I Dream of Jeannie Pants! I just got them, so too soon to tell for the long term, but right off the bat: the material and fit is super cute, comfortable, and flattering. (For reference- I tend to be a size 8/medium and am about 5'4" tall and I got the 2-10 size) The material does seem a bit cheap so I can see how they would tear like in other reviews. Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Will likely launder in a bag and on the gentle cycle in attempt to keep them in tact for awhile (fingers crossed!) Update: even being very careful with washing- got a tear right after the first use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

Woven Material Means No Stretch I usually would wear a 12 in modern women’s pants sizes because everything has some stretch to it. Keep in mind these pants do not stretch at all. I’m a dude, and these are the perfect 90’s jeans. I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

Versatile jeans, loose legs without being frumpy Great jeans! I’m 5’1” 145lbs so a little stocky, and bought a medium. I thought the waist fit was a tad small but once I wore them a while, it relaxed a little and now fits well - though I probably won’t run them through the drier in case they shrink. I love the high waisted style - the loops are large enough to fit a nice belt and ALL pockets are actually pockets ☺️ I have to hem them a little since jeans are usually long on me which I don’t mind. I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.

Baggiest jeans I've worn in a long time, but FUN, received compliments, fairly comfy! I've been wearing skinny jeans and straight leg jeans for a long time, so I decided to 'step out' with these Ripped Baggy Wide Leg Jeans! They are regular denim, non-stretchy. I ordered my usual size 4 and they fit pretty well. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#. The length is perfect, the waist is pretty good (very high-waisted) although a little snug, and the butt fits okay (a little loose). They are baggy and wide from the upper thighs all the way down to the wide bottoms. I'd say the legs are super loose and comfy, but the waist is what is snug since it's so high-waisted. The amount of rips is perfect, and I think necessary to off-set the bagginess and amount (lots) of material that these jeans have. I was able to stay in them all day, although would have been more comfortable if the waist was not so snug. I love the way these jeans look in the front, but do not love the way my butt looks. I guess since there is no design on the back pockets and since the butt is loose, it makes my big butt look big :(. I'm giving these jeans 4 stars as I wish the waist was slightly looser to be more comfortable, but I wish the butt was tighter to look better. Glad to own these fun new jeans though! My teenage daughter saw me wearing these jeans and said, "What are those? You are turning into me!" (And she approved!) Thank you!

Teen daughter likes these My teen daughter is 5’4”, 118 lbs, and has 27.5” waist. She wears size small or 4 pants. She got these jeans in size medium and she’s happy with the fit. The waist is snug but it is not too tight. The hips and legs have a wide relaxed cut. The pants are a bit too long for her to wear with flat shoes so she usually folds them up unless she’s wearing heels, platforms, or bulkier sneakers. There is no fabric content tag. The fabric feels like a quality heavier type of denim with a very small spandex addition. These jeans have a little give but they are not stretchy. The stitching is nice on the exterior and has some small issues on the interior but none are bothersome and don’t compromise the durability of stitching. There was also a piece of plastic foil stitched under the exterior waistband, perhaps a leftover of some kind of a tag but I was able to rip it out.

Great fit, comfortable and teen-approved! I picked these up for my 13yo, who loves things baggy and "cool". They did not disappoint. The fabric is thicker than I expected (yay!! No threadbare jeans!), and the cut seemed to fit her well. They are long on her but as she's only 5'2" tall, I expected that. She usually wears Doc Martens anyway and likes a platform, so I expect the back cuff getting scuffed up well be an asset not a deterrent from wearing them. Well worth the price and they definitely impressed me.

Exactly what I was looking for In a world of “skinny” pants, jeggings, and pants that are too tight in the legs, I was looking for an inexpensive, comfortable, pull on pant that I could wear to work. This checks all the boxes for $20! There is elastic in the back. The front is flat and slimming. I am barely 5’5 and it falls perfectly with dress shoes on.

For curvy girls - these only need minor adjustment for great fit Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Curvy girl approved! These slacks are curvy girl approved. I usually wear a size 12. I ordered a 14 because you just never know when shopping online. I wish I had ordered the 12. Finally! Not too wide, not too skinny 5”6, 205ish, size 18 plus. Have been looking for black pants to wear to work that don’t have ridiculously wide legs or super skinny legging types that emphasize my chunky upper legs if my top doesn’t go down to my knees. Found them, yay! Great price, too. Length is perfect. I’m buying several pairs because after 2 -3wearings the knees seem to get a bit baggier than other (more expensive) pants I’ve previously bought. Probably the one “con” that I have. However, 2 pairs of these cost me what I’ve paid for 1 so I’ll rotate them more. As with all of my work pants I prefer to cold water wash & hang to dry. They seem a bit wrinkled when dry but the wrinkles are gone by the time I get to work. Overall very pleased and I may even buy some in the different colors!

Incredible, but sadly too short... Firstly, I'm 5'3 130lbs, and ordered these in small. Everything about them, other than length, is incredible!! They feel luxurious on and the elastic waistband makes them feel very comfortable. The color is the most perfect light beige.The only reason for the 3 stars is the length. These are definitely not long enough, unless you're under 5'3 and plan to wear them with flat shoes only - which is not what I do. I'm short myself, but was planning on wearing these with heels, which is impossible, they would look foolish. I need my pants to skim the floor in heels, and these would go up to my ankles. They need another 3 inches; very bummed about that bc otherwise they would be perfect!

Great quality This dress pant is nice. Great office pants or if you’re going to an event and have to dress business casual. The fabric is smooth and feels soft. I ordered small and my 5’3”, 130 lbs body frame fits nicely. You can easily pair it with a nice sweater or a bodysuit and complete the look with a coat for a chic look. Comfortable and nice quality pants.

Great pants - you need these!! Now I got these beautiful pants to wear to work but you can definitely play with this look. You can put a crop top on with it and some sneakers and it’s a look. Great value for a set of three casual pants! I ordered this set of three BVNSOZ Joggers for Women for myself. I definitely could use these pants to round out my casual, around the house wardrobe, and give me more pants for just hanging out or doing yoga in. In the description, it says these are sweatpants, they're definitely not sweatpants. These are fairly thin and are made of a lightweight material, but they're still very useable and very comfortable pants. Because these are fairly thin I don't expect them to last for years and years but because if the higher spandex content maybe they'll surprise me! I can be definitely see getting a couple of years wear out of them at any rate. These are soft, comfortable, and great for just hanging around the house. For reference, I normally wear a size XXL out women's size 18 these pants fit very comfortably. These pants are made of 92% polyester and 8% spandex, which is a higher spandex content than most pants of this style. The high spandex content makes them stretchy, lightweight, and resistant to perspiration, which makes these a great choice for my daily yoga sessions. Unfortunately, the spandex content also tends to make them more apt to make you sweat and tends to make the fabric a little less breathable. doesn't allow your skin to breathe easily, and is sensitive to heat. They're perfect for warm spring and autumn days, and cooler summer days when you're at home. Unfortunately, they're really too thin to wear out unless you have a top that comes down below. These pants have an elastic waist with long drawstring. They're true to size, long enough without being too long for people who are probably 5'6 or taller. They also have pockets on both sides and taper to a short loose cuff at the ankle. I've washed these pants a couple of times since getting them. These pants wash up nicely in cold water without much bleeding, no shrinkage and, so far, no pilling. Is best to dry on low heat or hang to dry especially if you want to ensure there's no shrinkage. This set of three women's joggers for under $30 is an incredible value! These are very soft, very comfortable pants for light workouts or just hanging out doing your daily household activities. This set of three pants would be perfect for anyone who wears these kinds of pants because they're a great value. Highly Recommended!

As expected though, leggings are supposed to be tight, so that aspect is okay for me. The length is a bigger issue since I'm 5'11". If I wear them inside of boots or something though they would be okay so you don't see how short they are.

Good for the price I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

Tall Girls Dream! Will stretch. Update: have owned for a couple of months now and they def do stretch out. I got the 8 and they were a touch whisker like on the crotch area but after a couple of wears, they stretch out to fit perfect, just like my navy ones did.i already owned these slacks in Navy that I purchased at Lohmans a bit ago. I'm 5'10 and a size 8. And the navy ones fit perfect and needed no tailoring. My old black slacks got lost so I purchased these in black and I hoped they'd fit like the Navy ones. If you are used to teen type sizes like H&M or Forever 21 than yes go down a size but if you are used to woman's brands like CK, Kenneth Cole, or Jones New York, than stay to your true size. At first I was nervous because they were a little tighter in the thighs than my navy ones but I wore them around the house for a day and they stretched to fit perfectly. These slacks do have lots of stretch which is great for comfort but know that they will give slightly once you're wearing them so a little tight when you first wear them is good. Otherwise they'll be baggy and nobody wants saggy slacks butt. They are long. The cut is perfect for women with a little hip. Not boot cut but wide enough at the bottom to give you a little balance. You can see in my photo that at 5'10 they go all the way to floor and at 39in hips fit pretty perfect. There's a little pull in photo from way I was standing but that's it. Good luck!

So these scrubs have gone kind of viral in the ER I work at, I was influenced and finally bought a pair. Let me tell you, they are sooo comfy and soft which I didn’t expect! The fabric is like butter and super lightweight,l which is perfect for running around a busy ER. They also look more flattering on than I expected - somewhat form fitting on top but more relaxed fit in the legs and thighs, my butt doesn’t look saggy which I was worried about. The elastic waistband is perfect, looks clean from the front but scrunches in the back; can adjust with drawcord as needed. Lots of pockets which I don’t really need in the ER but would have loved as a floor nurse. Very durable. I’ve washed multiple times and air dried so haven’t had any issues with shrinkage; no pilling either. I will say I sized down to a small/tall bc I didn’t want them super baggy and for the length I like my scrubs to basically touch the floor and am always worried they’ll look like high waters on me so if you want a more fitted look I’d size down. If you don’t mind them being more loose stay TTS. I’m about 5’5 and 145lbs for reference; smaller on top with bigger booty and thighs. Great value for money highly recommend!

Trial and error I own 2 pairs of the tan Carhartt Original Fit straight leg pants, purchased from Buchheits. One size 14 and one size 16. They sit at / above my belly button which was a selling point because I had a csection and I still have pain. I attempted to purchase another pair from Carhartts website but they're sold out. First, finding the exact pant is hard because what is says on the tag is different than the website. I found the grey color on Amazon and ordered a 14 since that size fits me better. Received them and I couldn't even button them. No where close. Also, the labeling said "loose fit" not original fit. I requested the size 16 which came promptly in the mail. They sit lower than my other pairs, which okay, I could deal with, but the legs are HUGE compared to my tan ones (pictured). I looked at the tag and I was sent 16 Tall. I'm 5'4 and these looked ridiculous on me. They also seemed more bootcut than straight leg. I double checked my order and made sure I ordered the standard 16 regular. I'm disappointed because Carhartts sizing isn't consistent, my boyfriend has the same issues with their clothes. We both work construction and its nice having womens durable clothing but it's a pain in the ass to find a pair that works. Returning and will continue to look for the right pair.

Moms .. you want these I have been in the healthcare field for 12 years I bought these pants because I’m finally in nursing school and the scrubs that they gave us at the school are terrible they’re just so uncomfortable they are the drawstring unisex scrubs, like the black sheep of all scrubs. I have three children, my twins and my baby girl all via C-sections so my scar is very uncomfortable with different pants and I still have a little mom pooch from my last one I hate when scrub pants roll down or when you do find pants that are “high waisted” but are so tight it’s just uncomfortable however these pants stayed in place all 12 hours I wore them and they are extremely stretchy but they stay where they need to be. You can see that there’s a drawstring so if you buy them Long they’re definitely long enough to look professional with Dansko‘s or clogs but also if you like joggers you could probably buy them short and pull on the drawstring and they’ll kind of be against your ankle rather than hanging over your shoes (check out my pictures) either way these things are the bomb they are so comfortable I cannot get over how comfortable they are I only ordered one pair for clinical and after wearing them for one day I ended up ordering more and I ended up ordering a bunch for my actual job because I don’t think I’m gonna wear any other pants for work & school! THE ONLY SLIGHT DOWNFALL that some people may find is the material it’s like the polyester material it’s not cotton and I don’t think there’s any cotton except for the waistband I know some people sweat while these pants are good for comfort they might not be the best option if you tend to sweat let’s be honest I’m just gonna say it, if you tend to easily get “swamp booty” then I would recommend wearing 100% cotton panties with these bc honestly the material is so clingy and soft it’s won’t ride up your butt. Petite Small vs. Petite Medium I found the sizing chart to be somewhat confusing, so I have notes on both Petitie Small and Petite Medium! I posted a picture of me wearing the Petite Small and the other picture is me in the Petite Medium. It's difficult to tell by the photo but the Medium is looser. Petite Small (actual pants measurements): Waist 30" circumference Hips 38.5" Calf 13.5 Hip to crotch: 11.5" approx Petite Medium (actual pants measurement): Waist 31" Hips: 40" Calf: 16" Me: waist 28" Hips 35" Calf 13" The small fits on me, but snug in the calf. The medium also fits. The waist band is thick 2.5" but does have stretch. The actual material of the pants does not have stretch. Overall, the medium is looser like sweat pants but looks fine with a fitted top! I wear both the small and medium! A lot of my coworkers wear these in a tighter fit especially if they are pear-shaped and these pants look like leggings on them. :)

great flattering scrub pants I am obsessed with these scrubs. I am 5'4" with a 30" waist and 39" hips. I am usually a medium in pants and generally prefer a looser fit, but with the waist not being adjustible I decided to order both a Small regular and Medium petite. The mediums were just too loose in the waist and the moment I bent down the waistline fell with it. The length on the petites look better to me with my height, but I have a very athletic build and the cuff was too tight over my muscular calves. On the other hand, the size smalls fit PERFECTLY in the waist. I was worried they'd be too tight in the hips/rear but the fabric has a surprising amount of stretch and I've been very comfortable with them at work (I work in a veterinary clinic so I spend a lot of time squatting and wrangling animals). They did shrink ever so slightly with my covid-compliant hot wash/hot dry cycles (the photos of the small size pants were taken post-wash) but again, still an amazing fit, highly recommend

The small size had a perfect ankle cuff fit but going to a M the ankle cuff seems to jump 4+ inches in circumference which affects the aesthetics as well we the overall leg fit.

Fantastic for talls I'm 5'11" and scrubs that are too short is all too common for me. I love the cherokee brand, but I love the talls even more! Great fit. Very little give though make sure they are your proper size.

Good but runs large I find Cherokee tends to run large. For example, these are a medium. According to the size chart a medium should fit hips 39-41. You can't really see the tape measure but that is measuring 23" across, which would be a 46" hip. Comfy trousers and nice colour. I ordered the XS petite and I’m a ladies UK size 8 (5’1” and ~121lbs). The length was still 3.5” too long so I had them altered.

Nice fabric, different fit I've worn scrubs for the past 40 years of my life -Old school scrubs, stiff material, drawstring closure, unisex fit. The newer material is nice. It's softer. I like a little bit of stretch in it. I do not, however like a tight fit to scrubs, these are work clothes, for the operating room and not fashion per se. I found a pair of Dickie scrubs that I really liked and of course after 6 months they no longer exist. This is my attempt to find something similar. The waistband is similar, albeit without a drawstring, that is a downer for me because I tend to carry my phone and other items in pockets. Without a drawstring these do tend to be less secure at the waist and they slide a little bit with weightier things in the pocket. I am 5'7 and 190 lb. An extra large slid down without anything in the pockets, but I liked how the legs fit. I returned those for a large and the waistband sits just below my navel and the legs are okay, not tight but not loose fit scrubs. The stitching that comes right across the knee is just weird. When you walk you have that feeling of having long shorts on because the band is not stretchy. The length is good and I like the flare at the ankle. I guess we'll see how the color holds up. Not a fan of the waistband if you were going to tuck your scrubs in, but I've conceded to ordering more Missy fit scrub tops that will be worn outside of my scrub pants.
