If people followed the size charts there wouldn't be so many 1 star reviews If you are reading the reviews and saying "oh no! so many negative reviews are saying these pants are huge!" and are wondering what size to order or if you should order them at all... Well I have attached photos of the Cherokee brand size charts so you don't have to wonder. As you can see, the LETTER sizes on Cherokee's size charts don't match up with a standard US size chart. For example, I wear a US size 18 which would typically translate to letter sizes XXL or 2x. However, if I go by my number size, an 18 on this Cherokee brand size chart would have me order a letter size XL. In addition to that, my normal inseam is 33" so I would need to order the XL Tall, because Cherokee size chart says Tall inseam is 33-34" length. It appears that Cherokee's Petite inseam length is 27.5 - 28.5", and Regular inseam length is 30 - 31" for reference. As someone who reads reviews to help me decide if I want to order an item or not, this listing is one that I will not rely on the negative reviews. Most of the negative reviews are people who ordered pants based on their standard letter size, even though this Cherokee brand size chart is sized differently. If you go by the Cherokee size chart, you might have better luck finding a good fit for these pants. I hope this helps a little bit. As for my personal review of this style of Cherokee pants... I ordered the size XL Tall, and it fits just fine. These give a little extra wiggle room which I kind of like. For reference I'm number size US 18, my height is 5'10", and I weigh somewhere around 235 lbs. I have a large butt, and thicc thighs. My inseam is 33". The Cherokee brand size XL Tall in this style of pants is a little bit too tall for me, so I could probably hike these pants up a bit or get them hemmed. These pants cover my bum and are loose enough on my thighs for comfort to move around in. If you want a more fitted scrub pants, these are probably not the style for you. These are comfortably loose all around. I don't work in a field that requires these for work. I actually got them because I needed some comfortable cotton blend pants due to a severe eczema flare up. My legs are covered in itchy skin and unfortunately even some scratch wounds. If you've ever dealt with eczema, the struggle is real. I don't want to live in cotton pajama pants all the time, and would like to leave the house looking like a normal person at some point. These pants allow me to be comfortable and feel normal again. I have to say goodbye to my polyester pants and replace them with cotton blends. I appreciate that these are affordable so I can buy a few of them for my wardrobe update. I hope this review helps someone with their decision.

My waist 37.5" Hips 45" Height 5.4ft. I read all the reviewers comments but not much help to make a good decision on what size should I get. Following the size chart I took XL. But actual XL pants hips was 52" instead of max 49" as stated in size chart. I paid SGD43.34 for the 1st scrub pants. If I chose to return I had to bear SGD20 return shipping fees. So I had it altered and paid SGD15. Size Large can fit but still on the loose side which I dislike, I sent back 3 of them for refund, rather lost SGD20 than get them altered. Finally I bought Medium, It fits nicely except the length still too long. I had them shortened 2.5" Material is soft and comfortable but non stretch. Color similar as advertised. If seller has provided accurate size chart, I wouldn't have to bear SGD47 in total for return shipping fees/alteration. With so many reviews complaining size chart inaccurate, I sincerely hope seller be responsible enough to update and provide an accurate size chart as per actual items sold. Usually 1-3cm difference in actual items is acceptable. For this I gave 3 stars.

It’s a pretty thin material.So if you wear a women’s size 7 or 8 in regular jeans I highly recommend these because they are pretty cheap compared to the others.

I Like These Pants I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Great Jeans! I'm so happy with these jeans! I have a belly, so jeans that hit right under the belly button just accentuate it. These go above my belly button and since there's no zipper or button, they look much smoother than traditional jeans. They're comfortable, too! I weigh 206 and am 5'4. I got the size 18. They're a little loose in the waist, but if I'd have gotten the 16, they would have been too tight everywhere else. They're also a bit long. I might have to get them tailored to be 1-2 inches shorter, but they will look good with boots or a little heel as is.

Tummy control - no. Material - good. I’m 5’5” 170lbs I bought a 12. I would say these fit just right however I like my pants with a little more room I could have sized up. I bought the dark denim these are perfect for denim color office slacks. They can pass for slacks. All day wear will be comfortable. If you have a bloated bottom area on your stomach these will show that. There’s a nice top waist band about 4” of hold then it shoots into a thinner material. These have no lower tummy control. But for $20. I’m happy with my purchase.

I like the fit of these. For a petite sizes the length on these are just right. I’m 5’4” 140lbs and the size 8 fit fairly snug. Nice fit on the butt, but would prefer a bit more waist room so I ordered a size 10. Just a comfortable pair of every day jeans.

I followed the size chart & ordered a medium. Way too big. Exchanged for an XS. They fit great. Minus 1 star for cheap, non breathable fabric & misleading size. But you get what you oay for.

Super cute! LOVE! They are super cute on and figure flattering. They are trendy so it’s nice not to overspend as this style won’t last! Materials not the greatest but comfortable enough. True to size. 5’3 - 110 lbs - small was perfect for more. The look is oversized!

Super cute! LOVE! They are super cute on and figure flattering. They are trendy so it’s nice not to overspend as this style won’t last! Materials not the greatest but comfortable enough. True to size. 5’3 - 110 lbs - small was perfect for more. The look is oversized!

Mid Waist, natural waist, Leopard print, size medium, color wine. I love these pants. These jeans are printed, in a blend of rich earthy tones, ruddy and rusty. I love the style. They are a wide leg, cropped length, trouser. The fit is up at the natural waist about navel level. They are not mom jeans and in no way are disfiguring or horrible looking in the bum area. They allow for curves, and allow the curves to show, and are also loose fit and wide down the leg. There is no stretch, 100% cotton, which I love. However they are unforgiving. I used both size charts and they are accurate for me. The inseam is 71.3 cm, which is about 28 inches, HOWEVER, these jeans have a 25" inseam crotch to hem. That is too short on me, but with the trouser, cropped style being stylish right now, worn with boots or funky pumps or sandals, socks, they are fabulously hip in the shorter, ankle revealing length. I love them. I didn't want to have a tight fit with these because I like the loose look and perhaps the options to let them ride low slung. These fit just below the natural waist for me. I am 5'8" 140 lbs, 29 inch to 30 inch waist at the belly button. 38 inch hips, and a 34" mid rise. My inseam is more like 31" in reality and most jeans are 30" regular. I order long length when available. I weight 140 now, but usually weigh 130, so these are more fitted than loose and roomy. I would like these styles to offer longer length, but the baggy, wide and Barrel look seems to come with super short inseams with a crotch that hangs down. Not my favorite. These fit comfortable, however. The colors and the fit shown in the ads is accurate. I did have to be considerate when deciding on size. I have had to be more thoughtful with most jean ads lately to be aware of materials, stretch or no, inseams and size charts. 5 stars!!!!

RAWR!!! I ordered these CIDER Denim Mid Waist Leopard Wide Leg Pants in Wine Red in a Size Small for my 10 year old daughter... and it's giving all the RAWR vibes! For reference, she is 5'1" tall and 110ish pounds. She wears size 14 girls clothing from a Big Box store. These pants fit her great! The quality is EXCELLENT! They are a thick denim material that isn't super stretchy. It is more structured. They are fashionably cut with a barrel leg and mid-waist. The colors are bright and vibrant. The print is clear and crisp. They wash up and dry nicely, without shrinking too much. These pants are perfect to wear to a holiday party, on a fun occasion like a birthday party, or just around town-- every time my daughter wears them she gets lots of compliments on them! Mom and daughter approved... We highly recommend them!!!
Yess I am absolutely in love with this. The style, quality and fit is on point. Probably not for tall ladies since I’m 5ft 154 lbs and the length was perfect.

Perfect fit These pants are great. There's a lining like shorts inside and it doesn't ride up. You notice it a little bit when you're walking, like the hem of the shorts, but it's not very noticeable. It might be more visible if you're pretty dark skinned, though. They're flowy and the wrinkles steamed out easily--however, they wrinkle when you're sitting for a bit (I was at my desk half of the day) and those new wrinkles didn't go away when you get up. Not a deal breaker for me, but worth noting. I love that the waist fit me perfectly. There's no elastic, no stretch, and I used the measurements in the at size chart. These will prob be too long if you're short. I'm 5'8" and had on 2.5" heels and they were just above the floor maybe an inch. For size reference, I ordered XL and it's perfect. I'm 5'8", 173#, about a size 10. My mid waist is 34 and my hips are 40 inches.

Cute pants with some flaws I was really excited for these pants and love the way they look online. In person I was disappointed a little with some aspects. For reference I am 5‘,5“ tall and 115 pounds. My measurements are 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered the size extra small and it fit perfectly. It was even slightly snug in the waist and hips on me, but not a dealbreaker. I was disappointed online the color is a very pretty light sky blue. However in person it’s a darker, dusty blue color. I already have pants this color and really wanted a light pale, blue pant. The other thing I didn’t care for are the front pleats they’re not very flattering and add a little bit of bulk to me. Finally they were also a little bit short. I’m only 5‘5“ tall and these fell right at my ankles to where I really need to wear flats with them in order for them to not look like high waters. I typically like my pants longer, and to cover most of my shoe. All in all they are an OK pair of pants. I wasn’t thrilled at the color in person is not the same and didn’t particularly like the front pleats but I will still wear them. Hope that helps!

Very big & very unflattering! I am pear shaped, 5’4” 150lbs. I saw reviews that said these pants ran small, and since I am bottom heavy, I sized up to an XL. Big mistake, these pants are huge! Not for me, they make me look huge as well! In the photos, I am shown wearing the A-Brown color option. The pants themselves are well made and the weight of the fabric drapes well, they are unlined but not see though in the darker color. I wish I went with my usual size large, usually in pants I am a size 10/12. Check the size charts and take note of the pant length. If I decide to keep these, I will need to alter the length, waist, and seat. Made in China from 97% Polyester, 3% Spandex the manufacturer recommends machine washing, line drying.

Meh These slacks are ok. They come in a plastic bag to protect them yet mine arrived with a big stain & there’s no tears on the packaging. The sizing is off. Also, they put a clasp very close to the button so you have to maneuver a bit to get the connection. The pleating on the front makes me feel huge in them. There’s also a weird pleat-like seam in the back. The product page only shows a clasp, not a button & no pleat seam on back. Things I like: The color is nice. It’s like a slate blue. The pants are washable in cold water delicates. Rating: 3.5*

Runs a little small, but great jeggings 2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

This is the first pair of jeggings I've purchased, and I LOVE them!!!!!! They look and feel like real denim jeans! I'm not a fan of 'skinny' jeans, but I decided to give them a go (because I can't find anything similar in boot cut or flare without a hefty price tag). I've seen pairs of jeggings at Walmart that looked.. well... not good lol. I've also seen other people wearing jeggings and you can totally tell that they are, in fact, jeggings. They look kinda cheap? But not these, they look amazing. I've never felt more confident in any other pair of pants (is it technically 'bottoms'?). I'm very happy with my purchase. I will give you a break down of my body shape and weight. Something that I absolutely hate!!! I have fluctuated up in down weight wise my whole life. I have some extra skin as a reminder. But, here goes... I am roughly 5'8" and weigh 240. I am sort of awkwardly proportioned. According to me lol. I carry most of my fat in my belly. I have a hard time finding real jeans that actually fit me properly, because my legs aren't that big. My natural waist is 44, my hips are 54, and my lower hip measurement is 34. So when I try on jeans in a size I can get up high enough with my tum tum, the legs always look baggy and weird. Anyway, I think it's important to remember that I could be standing next to someone that is the same height and weight, and we will not necessarily be the same size. My weight is in my belly, but some are fortunate enough to have there's evenly distributed. I ordered an XL, because I fit in to a XL in yoga pants, and leggings. They fit me perfectly. The length, feel, and fit was amazing. They aren't as thin as leggings, but they aren't as thick as some denim jeans. They structure to them, so they don't highlight every bulge. I hope this helps someone.
Very cute! I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs. I got a medium and they fit perfectly. Sure hope they don't shrink!!!! 🙏 Perfect length. I can see me wearing these with lots my new birthday tops!

Love them!! I love these pants! At 5’5” and 220 it was hard to find cute jeans/pants in the “skinny” style. I needed something much more fitted at the ankle than my big girl jeans! I have now lost 30 lbs and I’m still wearing them! No need to buy every size as I’m losing weight. XL fit then and fits now. I might not have to buy again until I get into a medium!! Those reviewers whose pants split right away were likely wearing them waaay too small. If you’re on the fence, go for it!

Capri S vs Regular XS I am 5' 3/4" and about 118 lbs. I usually wear around size 2 in jeans and size xs or s in shirts. Amazon recommended that I get size S based on my shopping histroy. At first I ordered these joggers in size S, but they were too long for me. I was torn between trying capris in S or getting the XS in order to get basically a full-length jogger that fits right on my short legs. I got both, and here are the results. I hope this helps a fellow short gal out. The green pants are the size S capris. The grey are the XS regular length. I decided to return the green and keep the grey. The green capris are definitely tighter on my legs and they may look ok, but they were just not comfortable being that tight. The XS regular were surprisingly not too tight, and I think they are a perfect full-length jogger for me. After having these a few months, I love these joggers and recommend them. They are very comfortable and look good too.

Excellent travel pants! Long length, needed hem, water resistant, stylish, comfortable and verrrry flexible. Love. I've been calling these my airplane pants. We just recently took a lovely trip back home to Nova Scotia. It's summer, I don't really need the heavy warmth of regular jeans but I thought that something thin and flexible for wearing on the lonnng plane flight would be good. I wanted something lightweight, dark to hide spills, flexible and something that could withstand hiking about the woods in rural Canada. These were perfect! Mind you that was after I hemmed them. I am not sure who these pants were made for length wise, lady giants? I had to cut off a good 4 inches of length and re-hem them before they were the perfect length for my leg. Not a big deal but if you are short on time, want pants like these for a specific travel purchase as I did, keep the long length in mind. I am 5'6", so what I consider average height and found these reallllly long. I'l post pics of what I cut off and what the final length was. The material is indeed lightweight and has a beautiful stretch to it. That stretch means when you hem you'll need an appropriate stretch stitch so your stitches don't bust. I spent some quality time googling my hem options and familiarizing myself with my sewing machine's stretch stitch option before beginning. I was a bit nervous cutting my new pants but I went slow and ironed them, pinned them, took lots of precautions and they turned out perfect. Can't even tell they are not the factory hem unless you get down at ankle level and stare hard at the stitches... The pants are so lightweight and rolled into such a tiny little ball of fabric that I, quite geekily, weighed them! They weigh the same amount as a single pair of my most worn/favorite cotton cargo shorts. Suuuhhhweet. As for space in my luggage they hardly took up any. As I mentioned though I wanted these first for wearing on the air plane, it was an overnight flight and wanted maximum comfort. I was indeed verrrrry comfortable. My one complaint...WHY DO MANUFACTURERS NOT GIVE WOMEN DECENT POCKETS????? Seriously. It's stupid. Put the deep pockets in and let us decide if we want to stuff them and add pack mule like bulges to our thighs. If I am traveling I don't care if my cell phone bulges my front pockets or a wad of tissue or cough drops...I'd just like the option of carrying those things. Women's pant's pockets are notoriously short and I find it insulting. I purchased a pair of Columbia brand hiking pants for my husband at the same time as mine and they of course had wonderful, usable pockets. I won't ding them a star because it didn't affect my opinion of the over all feel, use and comfort and also it seems to just be an overall annoying clothing trend. But Columbia.....you'd impress me if you changed this antiquated habit of short, unusable pockets for future pants. I read up on these pants before committing to the purchase so I knew they were water resistant but WOW. They are VERY water resistant. Like if you take a handful of water and toss on your leg (there's that geek testing again) the water just rolls off. Of course if you submerge the material they will get wet but for spills, splashes, light rain the water literally rolls off. When we were outside in some light showers I just gave them a flick on the way inside and knocked the water beads off. They dry pretty quickly as well. We had access to a washer and dryer on our trip but I suspect these would be the sort of clothing you could hand wash in a sink and wring and dry in the air. I will try that out some time. I was careful the first time I dried them, keeping my eye on them and checking every few minutes. I find with these modern fabrics they either go nutso and shrink or do something weird or they are super star space age modern marvels, these are modern marvels. I followed the instructions, cool water, gentle cycle for drying (no high heat). They washed well. They can wrinkle a little, so if you fold or hang them right out of the dryer they look best. However I do find the wrinkles relax away after wearing or hanging so again, no big deal. We were in a humid environment, East Coast summer so quite moist, we didn't need long pants like these the whole time and I did find they got a bit stuffy after the sun was up. Not a biggy, just letting you know they're not as cool as shorts, despite their light weight etc. They look like dress pants. This is actually really awesome. They look like I'm "dressing up" instead of heading out to hike a trail or wander around the woods. I received many compliments on them when I was wearing a nice white t-shirt with them and kept having an internal mental giggle because I was wearing hiking clothes...nothing "fancy". I am a size 18 usually for most average department store pants. Considered "plus size" These pants fit my hips and legs etc really great. In fact for the first time in a while I wondered if I should have went a size smaller... These were actually a little loose on me but again I wanted maximum comfort not maximum style so loose sounded good for long airplane travel. I may try the size 16 in the future because for one, you save moolah in the smaller sizes and also it might be nice to have that slimmer option.. I did find after an all day wear the pants got even looser, stretched a bit...but they tighten back up after a wash. They have a VERY HANDY draw string cord as well as the snap and zip. I saw some one else mention the zipper falling down. THANK-YOU to who ever commented on that. I took the precaution of black undies for travel and halfway through the airport I realized things were feeling a bit breezy and lo and behold my zip was down..er....not cool...well it was cooling but not "cool". Anyways, be aware of that as well. It only happened once but once was enough for me to check every once in a while ;) I did find cat hair really clung to these, just remembered that...but I didn't have any dryer sheets at the place we were staying so maybe that's not an issue if they've been dryed with those..hmm, I'll look in to that further. I was visiting my Mom who had a verrrrry cat-hairy spot to sit and after I stood up I realized I'd taken half the cat hair with me haahaha. Let's see...That's all I can think of for now. These are really awesome pants and my minor complaints are just that, minor and don't feel they warrant dinging a star. Their supreme comfort and stretch that let me do anything from deep squats, hiking up a hill, sit for long periods in a plane seat are what I wanted and these excelled there. They satisfied all my needs and I hope they are long lasting in their wear. I'll do a longer term update in 6 months or a year or something.

Love these pants but... Love these pants and fit. I am glad I chose a size 6R for 125-128lbs weight and 5' 4" ht. The size 4 fit but looked like legging tight in the thigh. Size 6 is more sporty look for my weight. The pants are perfect for travel and hot weather. They have good stretch so movement is easy and they are light weight and quickdry, all the features they are desirable for travel to a tropical place. But the only negative is that even after following the washing and drying instructions, the laces got tangled in the pants and the result was a very wired and tangled lace that I was fortunately able straighten by ironing. But when I put it back in I noticed some grey stuff/ material come out which I cannot put back in ( see pics).

Comfy outdoor pants I bought these originally to wear for a trip around Yosemite, but have worn them for hiking a good deal since then. Overall, they are lightweight, breathable, stretchy, and fairly flattering. For colder temps I layered thermal underpants underneath, and they still looked good from the outside. My complaints are that after a few hours of activity, they stretch out and lose some of their shape. Also, the drawstring at the waist adds a bit of bulk in an area where none of us needs any extra. Finally, I'm getting a bit of pilling/fuzziness in a few spots after three months of use. I was hoping they would last longer. For sizing reference, I am 147 lbs and 5'6". Tried the 8 and 10 but ultimately went with the 8.

Crazy Static! I was looking for a pair of utility pants for camping/hiking and was hoping to avoid the pricey ‘outdoor brands’ that charge $90-100 per pair. These pants got good reviews and the price couldn’t be beat. They are definitely soft and comfortable. I’m 5’-2” and 130 lb and the 4 fit nicely- a little more flared at the bottom than I typically like, but the boot cut would definitely be ok with chunky hiking boots. I was ready to call the search over until I noticed how much static cling was happening after only wearing them for 1 minute! The pants picked up every single piece of lint, dust, dog hair within a 5 foot radius. Since I plan on wearing these in mostly dusty, dirty, rocky places, I would be annoyed if these picked up every little piece of dust and looked dirty constantly. There’s a chance I tried them on on a dryer than average day, but I’d rather not take the chance. Unfortunately ended up returning them.

warm and comfortable ski pants for winter adventures These ski pants are incredibly comfortable and keep me warm during winter and snowy days. The medium size fits me perfectly (I’m 5’7” and 145 pounds), and they’re true to size. They feature multiple pockets—two zipper pockets and two back pockets—which are convenient for storing small items. The pants are easy to wear, and overall, I’m really happy with my purchase. A great choice for outdoor winter activities!

Truly waterproof I am 5 ft 5 in and 135 pounds, usually wear womens pants size 6. I got a medium based on reviews and thinking I will wear layers under them. They are a little baggy, maybe should have gotten a small. I wore these in the shower and stayed completely dry! I only dislike that the cuff is quite wide, more like boot cut instead of tighter fitting. I have to wear these on the outside of rubber boots. It will only bother me I suppose when biking when you want no extra pant cuff. There is velcro on the cuff, but you can't make it skin tight, there will ways be a little flare. I got these for $41.50, which is nice. I will use them for biking, coxing, hiking, snowshoeing and farming in the rain. There is a drawstring and an inside id/key pocket. The mesh layer inside keeps pants from sticking to you. Why didn't I think of getting these sooner!? So many uses.

Waterproof and Fit Nicely These are an awesome alternative to gaiters. The ankle is wide enough so I can take them on/off without removing my hiking boots, but the Velcro sinches at ankle. I wish the leg was a little narrower but I would definitely not say the pants are too wide. These are great pants. Also wind resistant. Can wear fitted layers (leggings/soccer pants) underneath.

Great foul weather gear for fishing. Bought these for my daughter. We are big-time fishermen in S. Florida. I and my son also own a pair of these, and they are great in wet weather. Completely rainproof and breathable. When we are on the ocean in the winter with chop and swells running 5 to 6 feet, the waves crash over the side of the boat, and th cold rain is pelting you, these pants keep you nice and dry and warm. That way we can stay focused on the fishing and not hypothermia.

I'm 5' 4" and 105 pounds so there isn't much leg rubbing. Disappointing. The fit was good though. I ordered size Small and that fit well. Yes, they make a swishing sound but all rain pants do!

Nice, cool pants but longer than advertised My husband and I have some upcoming travel plans that will take us to some areas that will have temps in the upper 90’s… too hot! I’ve been looking for clothing that is not only cool but lightweight and wrinkle-resistant. These pants are perfect!!I’m a former ICU/ER nurse and had to wear scrubs daily at work. They were always comfortable, but it never occurred to me, now that I’m retired, to purchase scrubs for daily wear. These were really a revelation for me! Very neat, no ironing required, lightweight, and multiple pockets, what more could a girl ask for?These have an elastic waist that is very forgiving, as well as a drawstring for reinforcement. The fabric is not see through, but is rather thin, so although there are multiple pockets, I wouldn’t try to stuff them with bulky items. A really nice detail is the small slit in the outer seam at the bottom edges, it really makes the pants look nice. These are available in petite, regular and tall. I have a 32” inseam, so ordered the Tall size. Although the “Tall” states it is 32.5” inseam but measures 33.25”. I have no problem hemming them up an inch but just an FYI. I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

Just the right amount of baggy comfort I ordered these in the medium (unisex) size––I’m 5’5”, 140lbs and I found the medium to be the perfect amount of baggy without swimming in them so much that I feel like I have no shape. The elastic on both the ankle and waist is nice and not at all tight; the drawstring is just a simple cotton shoe-lace style rope; and there are no grommets to keep the string from disappearing into the elastic waistband. For me, the backside is a little saggy, but that’ll be different for everyone. Plus it will shrink over time because it’s cotton. The fabric is medium to lightweight, it’s slightly fleeced on the inside and a mostly soft outside with some texture to it, which is similar to a fine, corduroy stripe. Pepper is a great, dusty dark grey that goes with everything. I’m happy enough with them that I’m thinking of also buying them in blue.

I sized up because these are 100% cotton. Very comfortable, relaxed fit I recently got a pair of these grey (“pepper”) 100% cotton sweatpants. I decided to size up to an XL in case they shrink. Ordinarily, I wear a M or L depending on the brand, and for reference, I am 6’ tall and weigh 150 lbs. These sweatpants are medium weight, seem well made, and have a relaxed fit that is very comfortable. The waist is slightly large on me, but the rest of the fit is perfect, and there is a drawstring to keep them in place. I usually wear a 32-34” waist, and these are a bit bigger than that, which makes sense because I sized up to an XL. The pockets are medium to small in size, the length is good, and I expect these will get a lot of use.
Cute capri. I’m 5’6” 135# I bought medium and they fit great.

Couldn't decide what size to order so I ordered 3 sizes! My size info: 180lbs 5'6 hips 41" waist 35" I was having a hard time figuring out what size to order from other reviews stating these are compression pants order up. So i did. I usually wear an xl in yoga type pants but can fit in large as well. So i ordered these in xxl. I got them and they weren't compressing anything didn't have any of the popping sounds people talked about. So I ordered an xl and a large to try on. I will be keeping all three. They all fit. Attaching photos to show fit and I wrote the size on the mirror. I wear them just barely covering my belly button to suck in the spare tire between my hips and belly button. The white pattern ones do show thru if you are wearing bright undies. I put on some bright striped full coverage underwear to test it. I will definately wear nude colored panties with these in public. The xl made a couple popping noises while pulling them on. The L had several popping noises while pulling them on but they pulled right up no fighting or struggling to pull them on. They all were slightly baggy around calf area but were easy to pull up to a position where they fit snuggly and they didn't bunch up. So far they seem to stay in place but I have only worn a pair once so I will update my review after I have worn them more. The smaller the size the more compression there was without feeling like I was being crushed. These are my first pair of compression pants so cannot say how they compare to other compression pants. They feel comfortable and feel durable. I will update when there is something to update.

Comfortable, well made joggers! I ordered these joggers in the wine color. I am 5'9, 265 lbs and I ordered the 3X. I love the fabric, it is lightweight and the color is deep and even. They fit loosely and I love the jogger style ankle. The ankle closure isn't too tight for me, which can sometimes be a problem. I also really like that they are plenty long for my height, but even if you're not so tall you can use the elastic ankle to make sure that they fit you well. They have a drawstring waist, which I enjoy because then I can adjust them a little bit. I probably could have sized down one and still had a nice comfortable fit. They're exactly the type of lightweight pant I was looking for for a trip I'm taking next week. The mornings and evenings will be cool enough to need longer pants but they won't be too warm during the day because they're nice and light. Love them!

Thick and soft These sweatpants are thick, warm and comfortable, making them the perfect lounging pants for cool winter days. They are a regular jersey knit on the outside, but have a plush velvety lining. The lining makes them thick, but feels very plush and warm against the skin. They also have deep functional pockets. These sweatpants are thick, and heavy, so they are not the best for sports or gym because I find they tend to fall down. They do have a drawstring to help keep them in place, the aglets on the drawstring seem flimsy and will probably not last long, but they are metal. I ordered these in a 2xl, I was going based off of my waist measurement of 37, and found these fit about right. I might suggest sizing up if you have a larger rear than your average person, as the pants don’t really account for extra “junk in the trunk”, I probably could have done the 3xl for more space, but worry all the extra fabric on a 3xl would make these much heavier. These pants are great if you get cold easily and want a pair of THICK warm fuzzy pants to lounge around the house in.

The pockets are also a great feature and are nice and deep making them very functional.In terms of size, I would size up if you like a looser comfortable fit. I typically wear a 2X or 3X in similar type pants, however I ordered a 4X in these after looking at their size chart and knowing online clothes like this tend to run a little smaller. They fit perfectly and are very comfortable. I am also tall (5’11”) and sometimes have a problem with pants like this being too short, but these are a great length on me.

Not a fan of the material These scrub pants are cute and have a lot of pockets which I appreciate. The waistband is comfortable. They fit fairly well. They are slightly large on me but I intentionally sized up from my usual scrub bottom size of medium to a size large since I sometimes find jogger-style scrub bottoms fit a little tighter than I like. I could probably have worn the medium but I am happy with the extra space in the large, especially since the material is not very stretchy. For reference I am 5'9" around 160lb.My big complaint about these is the material. Right away I realized the material is sort of "plasticky" feeling, much stiffer and scratchier than other scrubs. This is especially problematic for the pants, since I wear an undershirt with the top but the pants are right against my skin. The material also makes a quiet "swishing" noise with every movement, which I don't like especially in a work environment. For context, I wear scrubs almost every day, I have 5 other brands of premium scrubs in my closet along with countless hospital-issue scrubs and these are the most uncomfortable ones I own. I wouldn't get these again.

Great pants with stretch, bit short in the legs Six month update: I wear these once a week at most and wash them... infrequently. Even so, my friend noticed that a tear is appearing on the back around the waistband. I love the fabric, feel, and taper, but I think I’ll stick with Columbia from now on (for $0-$15 more and better stitching). I’m ~115 lbs and 5’7” and leggy, and the XS fit perfectly in the waist, but are a bit short (like a 7/8 pant) in the legs for me. Great fabric for hiking and a bit more stylish than my Columbia pants (though not as breathable). The waistband is stretchy and comfy, and I wear these pants casually too! A few months in, they’re holding up well through hikes (no bushwhacking though) and machine washing (air dry). I will note that I ordered these in mountain green, and everything but the waist tie looks gray in every light (see picture). I didn’t mind the color so much so kept the pants, but do beware of ordering (at least the mountain green) if you’re deadset on the color.

Comfy, long enough, but not as thick as I hoped For reference: 5'7" 185lbs pear-shape and I ordered size XL. I bought these hoping they'd be a comfortable and casual option for colder days/nights this winter. (I live in a climate where the temp drops below 0°F often throughout the winter) The length was great because I'm 5'7" and I can wear them high waisted and the pant legs still reach my ankles. I've tried some joggers from crz yoga that leaves my ankles exposed, so that's obviously not ideal for the cold temps and I just prefer the look of longer pants on myself. The material of these pants is not exactly what I expected. It's very comfy and soft. I'm not sure how it will hold up for washing... But the inside of the pants are not fuzzy/fleece like I expected. They're almost a heavier legging material inside. I purchased the matching mock neck hoodie and the interior of the hoodie is soft and fleece inside, so I just wish that the pants were the same. That would lend to them being warmer and in my opinion generally more cozy. The other thing I'd like to note with this material is that it does show more texture than I'd like. The front pocket seams show through the pants which takes away from the elevated leisure look these are going for and when the fabric is flush with my skin, I can see the texture of my cellulite and I imagine you'd be able to see panty lines as well. I did get a light color, so perhaps the darker colors wouldn't have this issue, but an interior fleece lining would have solved this problem as well. Nice sweats I really like these sweats. The material is smooth with a terry-like texture inside. They're lighter weight and perfect for fall. I'm 5'9, 170lbs, size 10/12 or large, but I ordered an extra large because I don't like the tight sweats that almost look like leggings. They have a nice, relaxed fit in a size up. One thing to note is that these have a really straight fit and not much extra room in the rear if you're curvy. The length was a bit short on me so I scrunched them up. The elastic bands keep them in place on my calves. The quality seems really good and they washed up nicely. I washed them on cold and air dryed them.

Almost 5 Stars I really like these sweatpants but can’t give them 5 stars because the waist sizing is disproportionately big :/ The length is great - even for shorter women (I’m 5’0” and roll the ankle cuff up once); the leg width is not too baggy or too tight giving a neat & structured look and allowing to wear out of the house without looking sloppy; the waist is a true high-waisted fit, hitting just barely above my bellybutton (when drawstring is tied); and the material is soft inside and out and thick enough to be warm in cold weather. Despite all this, the waist is circumference is too big by several inches. If it weren’t for the drawstring being cinched in as much as possible, the pants wouldn’t be that flattering IMO - they would slide down onto the hips and defeat the purpose of being high-waisted. I still like them but this bugs me (especially at the price point) and am on the fence on ordering another pair. I ordered the XXS and have a 22-23” waist 32” hip.

not "butterluxe", XXS too big, 33.5 inseam too short still 5'8" with 34 inch inseam to ankles, 25 inch waist, 37 inch hip, size XXS in CRZ Yoga butterluxe and size XXS is too big in these flares. These are not the same material as the CRZ Yoga butterluxe leggings that I've loved for the past year. These are shinier than the leggings and they are not as soft or thin as the leggings are. I would say the leggings are like a second skin in the butterluxe material. These are closer to the "naked feel" material- which is slightly thicker and slightly shiny and just has a slippery feel to them. They don't breathe as well as the butterluxe leggings. The XXS in these are bigger at the waist and hip than the same size in the leggings. The XXS in the butterluxe leggings (same brand and rise) fit me PERFECTLY. In these flares, the waist is loose and shifts around when I move and the hips are bigger. It just feels overall like a size too large. The inseam length at 33.5 inches, is really great and i really appreciate the attempt. However, I really hope we can do some even longer. There is an entire population of women who have legs even longer than that. Like me. I'm 34 inches from crotch to ankle. If I'm wearing flares, then I need 36 inches so they go to my heel. Just a thought. I swear, if you would just OFFER 36 inch inseam on flares and leggings, you would INSTANTLY get ALL of us long legged ladies buying MULTIPLE colors of every style you offer. And please don't forget that some of us long legged women come in many shapes and sizes, and some are also thin, so XXS with 36 inch inseam would be EXCELLENT. Thank you.

Pants can be washed! Heat up nicely once I figured it out I ordered these and intend to wear them to cold weather Green Bay Packer games (on the "Frozen Tundra of Lambeau Field..."). I ordered the XL size so I could comfortably wear either leggings or even my old-but-fav skinny jeans under them. I am exactly 5'2" but these are not too long on me and will be fine with shoes on. There is both an inner fleece lining and an outer layer of fabric that is sweatshirt-type material. At first they did not seem to be heating up but I think that was user error. You attach the included battery pack to the USB-A connector in the front left pocket. Then you press the Power button that is visible on the front of the pants (lighted up in my photo in the mirror). I believe that is just ON and was red. Then you short-press to cycle through the temperatures of Low (white), Medium (blue) and High (red). As soon as I did that and got to red again, then I immediately felt the pants warming up. I included a photo from the instructions called Warm Tips. Mostly they are cautions. You should not spray any liquid on the heated pants and avoid sharp items, or heavy objects that apply excessive pressure on the heating elements. That does give me pause - what if some two-fisted-slopper spills his beer on me at the game?! Well I guess this is why is plan to have something on underneath, so that I can strip the pants off in a hurry, if needed. And these can be washed! The avoiding of liquid is only when they are connected to the battery pack and are heated. I included a photo of the label inside of the pants that say they can be washed on cold and then hung to dry. The cord that is permanently inside of the pocket (USB-A connection) has a cap which I'm sure you want to put in place before putting in the washing machine. Go, Pack, go!

Fantastic looking..REALLY warm These looked fantastic! Sadly, my butt is not large by any means and I have hip dips, yet these pants still made it look phenomenal (I think). I say these are pants because when I think of leggings, I think of light/spandex-y material—which these are not. For me, these are winter time only pants. They’re lined with fleece-like material and really keep your body heat in. It was about 70 degrees inside my home when I tried them on and I was sweating inside of the pants within 10mins—my skin was damp from sweat when I took them. I think the best thing I can compare how they keep heat in is like how a scuba diving wet suit keeps heat in. The pleather-like material can get a little bunched up on the outside if you don’t pull the material tight enough down the leg after putting them on. The way the stitching is across the butt is meant to fall under where your butt cheek is—which gives your butt the illusion of poking out further than it actually does. Again, I think these are super sexy, but only something I’ll, personally, wear in the winter/cold weather. I’m 5’4 135lbs with 32 inch waist, 37 inch hips for those who want size reference and I purchased a size large. I’d have loved to have tried on a size lower to see if they fit any tighter as I feel like there was a little bit of room.

Excellent for the price. They’re definitely not the original brand version but they give the illusion of a butt lift. These pants aren’t the original Freddy WR.UP brand, but not to shabby for a look-alike. I’m happy with this purchase for the price. I’m 5’2”, 115 lbs and I got the S size and it fit very well, actually! They’re probably going to be good for spring and winter months since they’re probably not very breathable. There is a fleece-ish material on the inside that may be too warm for summers in Florida or Arizona (for example). But I would recommend these pants for the price. There was indeed a smell when I first opened the package- probably from the manufacturer- but I will try and was as recommended by other buyers and see what happens. I will update my post once I know more! Nonetheless, for the price, I think it’s a great buy so far. The details are pretty good.

Cheap, thin material, prone to ripping I'm 5'7, 140lbs, usually a size 6, occasionally as big as an 8 and I ordered a size Large based off of the size chart, my measurements, and what other reviewers had said about the fit. Somehow they were still entirely too small, I couldn't even pull them up past my knees, and I don't have abnormally large thighs. I returned them and ordered the biggest size they had, an XL, still didn't fit even though it claims its for a size 10-12 (US). I got them on but the elastic waist was incredibly tight and unflattering. I smoothed everything out in the mirror, bent down to grab my shoes, and the crotch and as BOTH ripped simultaneously. At first I thought it was due to poor stitching but it wasn't even on the seams, the material itself was just so thin and cheap that it just popped like a balloon lol. I'm not even going to bother with a return, I just threw them in the garbage and told all my friends not to bother, they're garbage and not worth the money.

Practically Perfect in Every Way So like a lot of the reviews on here I bought these as a replacement for the old cotton danskin yoga pants I loved so much. Except I still had mine so I could do a side by side comparison. Now my old ones are like 10-15 years old (probably closer to 15), and I've gotten bigger in this time (surprise surprise), so the mediums I had in the original have stretched to fit me over time, and the fabric is wearing out some so it's stretched and almost pilling a bit. But for old yoga pants that have served me well all this time I can't complain. Now I loved the original fit, with the little bit of a relaxed flair at the end just below the knee, but since I got bigger the mediums lost that trait. I got these new ones and even though they're slightly different in some small ways, they're very much the same. Same quality, comfortable and stretchy fabric that breathes, same slight relaxed flair at the end below the knee (without being an actual flair). Fits well in all the right ways, very flattering. The only real difference I could find was that the waist band was slightly different? But honestly it doesn't wear any different on my waist. It's still around the same width to give that tummy coverage, and fits comfortably without too much give or being too tight. The extra stitching was just kind of decorative and I think it has been updated for the times or something so it's not a huge deal (though I did love the stitching). Overall 10/10. I've worn them like half a dozen times and washed them once and no change, still as comfy as ever.

Only real bad thing is I work in the Florida heat a little more than I like these days and when it's hot I sweat and sometimes the pant legs stick to me. Well these Relaxed fits are better then any pair I have had in the past. I must say that it's all about body type on how anything can fits. For me they work fine for my business center look or Shipping and receiving job.

Slightly too big, great quality The quality was great although a bit stiffer then I was expecting but it's Dickies so I think that's the point, I'd expect these to be quite durable. I was going to use these as casual black pants. A bit on the big side. I'm 5'4 140lbs waist is 31 and hip is almost 39. I recently had a baby and don't know my new size yet, (plus every company is a little different) I used the size chart and got an 8. It was definitely fit too long by maybe 3/4 inches or More, but I could've gotten it hemmed, for the quality of these pants it would have been worth it. At the waist it was way too loose. The butt area was ok, it could've been just a bit tighter. And at the crotch area it was visibly baggy (as much as stiff pants allow) and creasy. I will be returning them because of the fit only. I will not be exchanging to a smaller size because I prefer a lighter material for the summer for casual wear. I may get these a size down in the future. -1 star for not fitting, everything else was good.

definitely size up! update! after washing and drying completely they feel a little snug on the first wear but definitely loosen up during the day. they don’t bag out too badly, i was able to wear them about 3-4 times before washing and they still fit well. based on the other reviews i ordered 2 sizes bigger than what i wear in jeans and they fit pretty well! a little gaping at the back waist and the crotch scrunches up a little, as noted in other reviews. they almost come up to my belly button which is perfect for me —i can’t stand low rise pants! haven’t washed them yet so not sure if they’ll shrink or not, but if they do they’ll probably fit perfectly. the length is perfect for me! legs are pretty wide for bootcut. for reference i’m 5’9” and 160 pounds and i ordered a 10 reg. just needed them for work so i think they’ll work just fine.

Not true to size I bought a 4R, which is what I normally wear. They fit like a 6 in the waist and are long for me. I’m 5’5 115lbs so I didn’t think they would be this long. They are pretty comfortable and flexible at the knee.

Good quality but.. They fit waist well and are good quality, but the legs are a bit baggy-- hugs my waist and butt, but the legs are a little large... I have smaller legs though. I weigh 131 pounds and stand 5'5" and ordered a 4. Overall good buy

The pants dont last. I got these pants for work. I have no complaints on the fit. I am 5'11 and 180lbs. I purchased these because of the affordable price(still more than I usually pay)and length. They are also washed inside out. I also use detergent for dark clothes. And I hang to dry. I am very frustrated because I keep getting in trouble at work for the pants not being black enough. I cant afford to keep buying black pants over and over.

Good quality, horrible fit These pants were good quality but honestly just felt like I was wearing smaller mens pants with how they fit. The “straight leg” was more like boot cut, they were so long that I was stepping on the pants even with shoes, and because they fit like boot cut it looked absolutely ridiculous rolling them up. They are also somehow BIGGER than some size 12 pants I have… yet these are a size 10?? How is a 10 larger than a 12??? I’m so tired of womens clothing being inconsistent. I literally have no clue what size pants I even wear because they all fit differently :(( why can manufacturers do it for men’s clothing but women’s clothing is impossible? So frustrating. I’m ~5’7, 150lbs

Size down-- But great! For context: I'm 5'9", around 130 lbs, and usually a pant size of 26/27. I ordered the Size 4. They're great, they feel really nice and the pants are constructed enough that they feel sturdy, and are still stretchy enough to move. They do fit a little oddly. I was able to fit two fists comfortably between my stomach and the waistline, and they end up riding lower on my hips. Like most work pants I've gotten, they're very long and I'll be hemming them. I will be returning them for a smaller size because I personally don't like how low they fall on my hips and how low the crotch is from that. Otherwise, they fit great! They're snug around the thigh and loose down the leg.

Does not fit smaller waste, larger booty women 😂 The sizing of these pants on my body is laughable, and I really have never had this problem with any other brand. In the Slim cut, I originally ordered a 4 and had a huge gap in the waste from my back to the waist of the pants, even though the legs fit good. Inconsistent quality & size 1 pair good & 1 pair bad. Neither are thick/tough workwear, but not super thin nor too stretchy. Good pair has a few loose threads, placket over zipper is kind of small, so does that wonky thing of showing gold zip near button, but not too bad. I'm 8 or 10, & 10 gives me a bit of drape instead of skin tight thigh/calf. Does make flat rear look flatter, but got for physical labor, not spanx substitute. Both pairs 10R, labels all match, but lt. grey is 1-1.5" longer than navy. Unwashed, grey is long side of 32" inseam & navy short side of 31. Both inseams could work for me in other uses, but didn't want ankle-showing for these. Plus, the navy is poorly made - not just a ton of loose threads, but missed & messed up stiches that will come apart eventually. Worst, navy pair has really messed up zip placket, way small, off kilter, & permanently pressed all wrong. So, most of very bright gold zipper would show - from like a mile away - against the dark fabric. Grey pair will be annoying around the top, but navy is terrible - that full zipper display looks like too-tight, cheap pants, even though bit loose on me. Torn tag & human hairs indicate someone else also found this navy pair unwearable.

Size 4R works well for 5’8” 120lbs I ordered the Dickies relaxed fit cargo in rinsed grape leaf green in a size 4R (although Amazon said I would fit a 6- I tried the 6 and it was way too loose everywhere). I’m 5’8”, ~120 lbs, usually a size 4 (My measurements from 2 years ago: Bra: 32A/B - 34 A/B, Waist: 65cm, Hips 92cm). The 4R fit me well. Even though when I stand there is a little room at the waist, it isn’t noticeable unless I pull it out. It would not be comfortable if they were tighter there, especially when you sit, you need a little space around the waist. When I kneel (which I do in fieldwork), these are a bit tight in the thighs and cut in a bit behind the knees- but that is to be expected with this thickness of fabric. The length fits me perfectly. I’m 5’8” and I have a shorter torso and longer legs and these are at my heel. The pockets are large so you can fit lots of things, although when you sit that might not be very comfortable. My iPhone 13 Pro Max fits in my thigh pockets when standing, but when sitting, it jabs into my pelvis. It is a little too long for the thigh pockets; It sticks out such that you can’t Velcro it shut. These seem well made and Dickies is a brand I trust.

Fit really weird These pants had a really weird fit. I usually wear a size 6, I’m 5’3, 140-145lb and these were huge in the waist. I tried to putting them tighter but they were still too big. Another thing I found weird was that despite the size, they were super short. They came just above my ankles and raised even higher if I sat down. The material is a little weird too, doesn’t feel durable and doesn’t feel thick. The pants are lightweight which makes them breathable but they also feel cheap and doesn’t feel sturdy. They weren’t very flattering on me either and they were expensive so I ended up returning.

Vet techs: these REALLY DO repel hair! I normally wear dickies signature but really wanted to try out a pair of joggers when I got new scrubs for a new new job. I was skeptical of all the reviews from nurses that said how well they repel their pets hair because one pet is a lot different than the dozens of patients I see in a day. Comparing 4 Scrub Pants I bought 4 different brand scrib pants at the same time before deciding which to keep. Short story – I’m keeping the Dickies Scrub Pants Long story… 1.SIVVAN Scrubs for Women - Drawstring Cargo Scrub Pants (Small) -size Small fits as expected at waist based on sizing chart -stiffer material/thicker than Dickies -material bubbles up/poofy in front/hips and legs so less flattering if you are very slim -cargo pocket left side; phone completely hidden/deep pocket 7.5” -right cargo phone pocket deep 7.75” plus 4.25” deep phone pocket– wide enough for my phone in its case which sticks out ~2.25” at top -no front slash pockets or back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 29” inseam; total outside length 40.5”; 7.75” cuff width at the very bottom -returned this pant 2.WonderWink Women's Wonderwork Pull-On Cargo Scrub Pant -liked better than SIVVAN -size Small fits as expected at waist -noticeably softer than SIVVAN material, still puffy in front hips and legs so less flattering if you are very slim -cargo pocket left side; phone completely hidden/deep pocket 7.5” -right cargo phone pocket deep 7.50” plus 5” deep phone pocket– wide enough for my phone in its case which sticks out ~1.75” at top -nice front slash pockets -no left cargo pocket or back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 8.75” cuff width at the very bottom so very wide for skinny legs -returned this pant 3.Trend Uniforms Tailored Fit Scrub Pants for Women - Soft Stretch with 8 Pockets & Drawstring -overall more flattering than previous 2 -size X-Small fits as expected at waist based on sizing chart - 94% polyester and 6% spandex so more drapey and less stiff/puffy than previous 2 which are 65% Polyester, 35% Cotton), more flattering for me (very slim hips/legs); stretchy fabric that lays nicely -left and right cargo pockets both 6” deep outer and 5.75” deep inner phone pocket, BUT these cargo pockets sit lower 2-3” on the pant legs then previous 2 pants – I feel like a little too low. The previous two feel natural while I feel like I have to bend sideways too much to reach into the cargo pockets of these pants -nice front slash pockets and back pockets -legs – rather wide for slim legs; 30” inseam; total outside length 40.5”; 8.5” cuff width at the very bottom so very wide for skinny legs but less noticeable due to how the fabric drapes -may keep even though would prefer cargo pockets were located 2-3” higher on thighs 4.Dickies EDS Signature Women Scrubs Pant Natural Rise Tapered Leg Pull-on 86106 -liked best for fit/flattering and tapered legs -size XS fits well at the waist -softest material 55% Cotton, 45% Polyester and not puffy around front hips/legs BUT biggest lint magnet (much better after 1st washing); not stretchy -left cargo pocket 7” deep; right cargo pocket – mixed feelings. The 7” deep pocket has a stretchy red knit pocket inside 4.25” deep (not visible from outside). My phone fits VERY snugly here widthwise, maybe too snugly but if not using this pocket you tend to snag items on this knit pocket when trying to use the larger pocket. If I try to drop my phone into the 7” pocket it often catches on the inner knit pocket. Outside/below this large pocket is the outer cargo pocket 5.25” deep. What I’m surprised reviews don’t mention often is the numerous pen loops located inside the pocket. I took a photo (only posting on my Dickies review). I find these loops annoying and they keep preventing me from easily sliding items into the right cargo pocket. But if you carry lots of pens/small tools you find the numerous pen loops a plus.

In love with the pants :-) Reviewed by spouse: OK, I’m in love with these pants. They remind me of the 90s/early 2000s and I’m here for that! These look like the ones that Jen Aniston wore when she was running around LA with Brad Pitt…Pictures all over the Internet of it. They cinch around the waist and they cinch about halfway down your calf, but not at the bottom that is just regular elastic. I am wearing a medium and for sizing reference, I’m 5’3 and 155 and they are perfect! The orange is not a neon orange. Outstanding lounge pants!!! I snagged these in a size medium and I weigh about 135 pounds. These pants are super comfortable. They are just right around the waist and just right in length, I’m 5’4, and they’re a little long on me, but proper for a medium. I love the neutral khaki color and the visible seems that are a lighter cream. The best thing about these pants is that they have pockets and they are a really really fine velvety kind of micro suede material. I tried to take a picture of the Knit, but it’s hard to capture. The best way I can describe it is very very soft micro velvet. These pants are super comfortable and just the right thickness. I love them.

Exactly what I wanted! I really needed a pair of THICK sweatpants with the fleece lining that wouldn't be see through or feel like jersey thin material. I wanted the feeling of those old fruit of the loom sweats from Walmart but cuter. These were just that, 60% cotton, just as advertised. I love the fit as it's high waisted, tight at the ankles, pockets are unqiue, not too bulky. I got the size Small, pink, I have a 25 inch waist and 35 inch hips, 5'3". I could foresee these being too short for taller girls, they are exactly the perfect length for me. Pic was taken after they were washed. The color is a little more bubblegum pink than advertised, as I wanted a softer baby pink. But I am obssessed, and these are literally game changer sweats. Not see through, but I do have VPL's which isn't a dealbreaker for me. Will be ordering more in the future!

Love these pants The blue color is very pretty. Fabric is very soft & inside fuzzy fleece lined. The legs are not too long I'm 5'3". There are two large front pockets, nice elastic waist with a tie. Leg bottoms have elastic. These are awesome fleece pants. 100% recommend.

Warm, Soft, Comfortable Super soft fleece lined joggers with pockets and comfortable drawstring. Very warm! At 5'3" 125-30 I bought the medium for a looser fit. They shrunk just slightly, but stayed very roomy. Love the color and the pockets!

Extremely comfy I selected the purple color in a size large and I absolutely love them! First off they are extremely soft. I wouldn’t consider them fleece or jersey as the material feels like nothing I’ve worn before. The material isn’t thin but isn’t too thick either, to me it is just right. I have been able to wear them when it is cool out and wear them on warmer sunny days. They have been great to work out in as well. The elastic waistline fits perfectly on me but there is also a drawstring in the waist if you need to adjust them. For size reference I am 175-180 pounds and my normal size is large in similar bottoms. I feel they run true to size as I even have a little wiggle room but, if you want them baggy then I would consider sizing up. These do run very long though for my height of 5’4” (and on a side note they do not roll up well either as when rolled they fall out within a few steps so I had to hem them). I as well am thrilled that they wash and dry well too. I have washed them numerous times and they are still so soft and have had no shrinkage. I wash them in cold water and air dry them. They accidentally made it into the dryer once but even then the were just like new coming out of the dryer. I definitely recommend these a million times over!
Too big The size chart recommended an XL because I’m 5ft 10 and weigh 225lbs. I love the material and feel but I’d go two sizes down in my next purchase.

Perfect fit! I am very impressed by the overall quality of these flare leggings. They are very soft and comfortable. I like the pockets on each side. They have a lot of stretch. They are a little thin so they show everything. I would suggest wearing pantyhose under them if you want to go for a smooth look that is more flattering. The waist band fit just right, not too tight. The length was perfect for me. I am 5'4" and 125 lb. I bought a size medium. Overall I am happy with my purchase of these leggings. I would say they are a good value for the money. I would even consider buying more of these in the other color options.

Great cross-waist pants These are very soft, but with a slight sheen. Not too thick or thin. I hate yoga pants that dig into my stomach, so I only wear cross waisted ones. They give a little bit of tummy support and they lay nice and flat. I'm 5'3", 128 lbs, and I usually wear a medium. But I hate pants that pinch so I ordered a large. I could have gone with a medium for a better fit and a bit more compression. So, they're true to size. I'm petite, with a 28/29 inseam, and these drag a little bit under my heels. I cut an inch off so I can wear them to yoga in bare feet. They won't fray. The pockets on each side are nice and deep for a phone and keys. They have all the best features of great yoga pants. I could live in these all day, every day.

A must have in the every color! I own these cargo pants in just about every color, and I was shocked by the quality of the material. The fabric is smooth and gives the pants a tailored look, with an elastic band in the back that prevents gapping. There isn’t much stretch, but they are true to size. I typically wear a size 16 or 17 depending on the brand, and I purchased these in a size 18—they fit perfectly. The pants have functioning pockets and a real zipper, unlike some that just have decorative details. In my opinion, they are tall-friendly. At 5’6”, they look great with heels. These can easily be dressed up for an elevated casual look or paired with sneakers or flats and a tank top for a neat, casual style.

Will machine wash well and steam to perfection!

Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

THESE WILL SHRINK AND BREAK YOUR HEART I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

Not flattering if you are curvy!! I’m so disappointed. These are good quality, nice material and well made. So comfy The material is hard to explain, but it’s soft and stretchy. I rolled the top down once because the inseam is too long for me. Dress up or down! I’m 5’8”, 175 lbs, usually wear a 10/12 in pants. I bought these pants thinking that I could dress them up for work but feel like I had sweats on. Mission Accomplished! Love these ! These are my new favorite joggers ! Perfect for my job chasing kids around. Size small, 5’3 / 120 lbs. I love the material of the ones with print, not so much solids. Very cute :)

Overall cute pants These joggers are cute overall, a little dressier than workout attire as they're more of a silky fabric. I'm 5'8 130-135 and ordered a medium.

Lightweight material and comfy but not as flattering as expected This review is for: Dokotoo Womens Summer Boho Business Casual Smocked High Waisted Cargo Long Pants with Pockets These pants can wrinkle easily but with a steam iron all wrinkles came out easily. The pants are lightweight but comfy - but the downside is that they don’t look as flattering for a short person or someone with shorter legs. I’m 5’4” 120lbs and the size Small ordered had plenty of room to spare.

pleasantly surprised I read other reviews and was a bit nervous about purchasing but I wanted something loose and flowy for trip to Aruba. These were perfect. The pictures are after being packed (I did light iron them before packing) but not after. The 2nd two picture are after hand washing (thanks to a wine spill on them) and letting them air-dry. Great wide leg pants I really like these pants. They're dressy enough for work or an event like graduation, but are also very comfortable. THESE WILL SHRINK AND BREAK YOUR HEART I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

Not flattering if you are curvy!! I’m so disappointed. These are good quality, nice material and well made. They fit true to size (I’m 5’10” and 180 lbs and wear a large, which is the size I ordered) and they are long enough for us taller ladies. They have “shorts” under them as a liner which is nice. The problem is, they aren’t cut in a flattering way if you have curves! I’ve worn palazzo pants or wide legged pants before that looked beautiful on me, but these are not them. These have too much fabric up top and make me appear to be 2 sizes larger than I am! My whole family agreed. These might look nice on a tall, slender built woman.

Nice pants; a little too short for me I requested these pants on the chance that they are actually longer than the size chart indicates... but nope, the chart is spot on. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and I'm wearing a size Large; a Medium would have fit but I was going for baggy and long, which, together, are on-trend for FW24. They are really nice pants -- nice medium weight, full in all of the right places and the cargo pockets add a funky twist. These pants are a very nice length for someone of average height, but even positioned below my waist, they fall too short to be cute on me. They do have a half inch seam so I'll let them out. I think these pants are a great value -- really, really nice.

Cute and Comfy I purchased these pants for camping. They are really comfortable and have good stretch. I am 5’5 and 135 lbs. they fit perfect and I love the look of them. Definitely going to get some good use out of them. For outdoors and fall winter outfits!

Amazing fit and comfort I'm 5'3" and 117 pounds. I usually wear a size 4 and these pants in the size small fit perfectly on me, even the length. They're made of super soft cotton with a little spandex so they're super comfortable. I already ordered them in another color.
cute in red It becomes a little more baggy as the night goes on but I still wouldn’t go smaller than my normal size. Which is what I bought. I’m 5’5”, 145 lbs for reference and I am wearing a 6.
