Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

BEST PANT EVER. Super stretchy! Stretches out over time - good to size down I bought these first in white in a 6, and LOVED THEM, then they became baggy as I wore them (in a cute way though). So I bought the grey and red in a 4 and they fit perfect. I normally go between a 4-6 in normal pants so I'd recommend sizing down because of the stretch. These are my new favorite pants. I have worn them the last 4 times I have gone out. Perfect for any occasion, and great with a bodysuit. I have a small waist and wider hips and the 4's fit perfectly, no belt needed.

So comfortable! I got these for weekend hikes and Girl Scout field trips with my family but will also probably wearing them for much more than that as they are so cute and comfortable. I will now be buying a pair for my daughter as she loves them too. The material is very soft but incredibly sturdy. Definitely a quality pair of pants. I like that they came in a variety of colors and the side pockets take me back to my Junior high school days! I am 5'6 152lbs and generally wear a sz6 pants and these were slightly loose on my waist but very comfortable so I don't mind.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Just right for the price really pleased with these. I am 5'7'' and 150 and I got the 10. I got the green ones first and ordered these because I like them so much. For the price, I'm very happy with these.

Love these pants!! These are adorable. A little bit of stretch, but not enough to feel cheap. Not super thick. Slimming. Perfect length for today’s style. I ordered two other colors immediately. Wish these were a *tad* longer I wore these all day and I gotta say they are COMFY! I ordered a 4 and I'm a 26-27" waist and they fit really well, I probably could have sized down for a tighter fit on the waist without issue. Obviously the cut of these pants allows for a lot of movement but the fabric stretches and bounces back with ease. I could squat and goblin walk in these and be totally comfy while doing it (we all know that's the true test). I wasn't sure about the aesthetic of these but I got multiple compliments from coworkers and they really grew on me. I did notice that the white threads started to fuzz up by the time I got home. It seems like they may have used cotton thread for these accents which would account for that. My only hesitation is that I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I'm 5'9 and they're definitely long enough. That's why I removed a star, but I think for *most* people the length will be absolutely perfect. These are honestly really great and the quality exceeds the price. Much better than similar pants I've gotten for more $ from popular retailers

Excellent quality. I ordered these Dokotoo Women’s Casual Mid Waist cargo jeans for my daughter and she loved it. Cargo jeans are sewn with high quality and neatly. The seams are even throughout and the threads do not stick out anywhere. The inner seams are carefully finished. The fabric from which the jeans are sewn stretches well and is pleasant to the touch. Cargo jeans look good and complement the look nicely. Cargo jeans have a beautiful gray color. I ordered a size 12 for my daughter and it fit well. My daughter was deciding between 10 and 12 and chose 12 because she doesn't like jeans that are too tight. The daughter is 5.3" tall and weighs 140 lbs. Her hips are 41.5 inches and her waist is 32 inches. There is a size chart in the description, which is very convenient. The cargo jeans are a bit long for her height, but this can be easily fixed with iron-on adhesive tape.

My favorite pants! These are the softest, most comfortable pants I own. And I own a LOT of pants. Based on the size chart, I ordered up but I needn't have, so I'm hoping they shrink a little. But the length is perfect, so hopefully they don't shrink too much! I'd order my usual size but if you're between sizes you might want to order up. For reference, I'm 5'6" about 145 lb and normally wear a size 6 but ordered size 8. They're a bit loose in the waist and with so much stretch and always carrying my phone in my pocket, I'll need a belt! The fabric is amazingly soft - like peach fuzz - with a lot of stretch. They're light but thick enough to have good structure and quite flattering. So glad they're 98% cotton - they'll be breathable for summer comfort. And speaking of pockets - get a load of these bad boys! Nice, big phone pockets - for those of us who don't carry purses, my wallet phone case has no trouble fitting in here! I'd give these pants 10 stars if I could, they're so comfortable. They're my go-to pants for wearing every day and doing every thing! Highly recommended, A+++++ !!

My new favorite pair of pants! Absolutely loving these pants! Not only do they fit absolutely perfectly, but the fabric is super soft and feels amazing against my skin. Not Sure About These... I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. The material is similar to a lightweight chino or khakis, good for fall and spring. With some leggings underneath you could wear them in the winter as well. Overall, they are very comfortable, I just am not sure how to style them. They aren't tight at all, so if you like how they look on me, get your regular size, but double check the size chart measurements.

Sizing depends on the color, but soooo worth it!! 4'11", 135lb - typically a medium / size 6 with a 30" inseam. Pictures: black in XL, purlple in XL, and olive in medium. I will say this about the sizing... I originally ordered the vintage purple and olive green in medium and while they do fit, I wanted a more relaxed waist (especially on days when bloating is an issue). The mediums felt like I was cutting off circulation to my lower body by the end of the night. Waited to return them and ordered the same colors and another in black leopard (because I love the fabric) in x-large.... The purple and olive were perfect for what I want but the black seems to ACTUALLY be an XL! Nuts! Will be returning the leopard black for medium, but keeping the olive and purple in XL. Material is soft and warm - too warm for me to work out in, but this is just a personal preference. I love how comfortable they are! Not too hard to dress up with a nice shirt. TL;DR you'll love them, just don't be surprised if different colors have different sizes. Size up for a more relaxed fit.

If you don't like panty lines, do not purchase. I purchased these joggers because reviewers said they were a great dupe for the Lululemon Align Joggers. I was frustrated with how quickly my Align Joggers pilled, so I thought these might be a better alternative. I was wrong in so many ways. 1) The fabric shows every single panty line. The only way to avoid panty lines is not to wear underwear. It doesn't matter if you have seamless panties or even a thong; your underwear lines will show. 2) They pill just as bad and as quickly as the align joggers. 3) They are not as soft as the align joggers, and the fabric is much thinner. 4) They don't hold in your belly as well as the align joggers. I have never worn these outside the house because I hate showing panty lines. Personally, I would recommend just paying more for the real thing. Sizing - I am 5'4" and 130 lbs. My waist is 27 inches, and my hips are 38 inches. I purchased a medium, which fits similar to a lululemon 8. If I ordered a different pair, I'd probably get a small instead.

I am 5’11”, 160# and I got the medium, fits perfect, tight enough on my bum but not so tight it’s like yoga pants! Perfect lounge pants, but honestly these could be dressed up if u wanted! Good price, I’ve wanted thr lululemon ones but didn’t want to drop $100 on lounge pants, glad I tried these first. Will order more pairs for sure!

UPDATE: I ended up reordering the orange in XXL and they fit much better. So size up would be my recommendation.

Comfy and lightweight! I'm 5'4", weigh 132 pounds with an apple shape - 32" waist, and typically wear size 14/Large. I followed the seller's sizing chart and ordered a Large. When they arrived, I tried them on, and they were HUGE! They looked ridiculous! Since these pants are 100% cotton, I decided to machine wash (cold water) and dry them, hoping they would shrink up. Well, they did and fit much better now! They shrunk quite a bit and now fit great and are relaxed and comfortable feeling. I don't think I'll put them in the dryer again, I don't want them to shrink up anymore. I normally love pockets but the placement of these are too low and look sort of weird. They should've just not bothered with them, they're pretty useless where they are. For about $23, these pants are a good deal! I think I'll be wearing them a lot this summer!

Best travel pants! I love these pants! I bought the beige and the black pair. I also purchased to long sleeved sweater in beige. I wear this every time I fly. They are so comfortable and wash well. Sooo soft and cozy! I love these pants, they're so comfy! They are made of super soft sweater material. They are high waisted and fitted at the waist and hips, but wide in the legs. They fit true to size, I'm 5'9, 165lbs, and I ordered my normal size, medium. I sometimes buy larges in comfy pants for extra room, but these have lots of stretch, I didn't need to size up. The length hits me a few inches above my ankles. They have another seam sewn about four inches above the hem, I'm wondering if that is there so you could fold it up inside and sew it in case they are too long for you? If that is the case, it's a great idea. My only complaint about these pants is that they do show every lump and bump I have on my thighs and rear. That's not the pants' fault, though. They're really not that see through for being such a light color, you'll probably just not want to wear your bright red undies with these if you're wearing them in public. They are nice enough to wear out in public in my opinion, I'm just going to wear a long shirt with them to hide my imperfections. I love the "porcupine" color, it's a soft, pretty, light gray.

Flowy & comfortable palazzo pants, but long. The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.

Beautiful quality but will shrink! I think I have fallen in love with these linen pants. The quality is amazing. It’s lightweight when on and heavy when holding it because of great quality. This isn’t those cheap ones, these are the real deal. It’s very flattering and make your bum look fantastic. Pictures don’t do it justice. It also has real functional pockets as well as drawstrings. You could wear this low or high waisted and it looks good both ways. I must warn you though, it will shrink if you wash and dry them in the machine. They were long on me before washing but now they are shorter than I want. I think they are suppose to be almost below ankle length but I liked them longer especially since I’m barely 5ft tall and want to wear heels with them. Anyway I got the size XS in khaki (last picture attached is put against standard cardboard box color). If you want them more loose and long, go up a size especially if you wash them in washing machine and dry them in dryer. Overall, I still love them regardless if they shrunk. This is a must for spring, summer and early fall wardrobe.

Comfortable, maybe not stylish After reading the reviews, I decided to try these out given I'm looking into giving up my jeans and skorts this summer. Jeans can be hot, and skorts seem to all be too short (even though I'm 5'3"). I am a medium (145 lbs), but based on reviews I ordered an XL. When I tried them out of the bag, I thought he length is great, the leg fit is great, the bum section is baggy. The elastic waistband was so tight I couldn't believe it - I thought, well I'll give them a wash and decide - they can always be yard pants. They washed and hung dry well - but now the elastic waist is much looser! How does elastic stretch in one wash? I have sewing skills, so I've figured out how to make two tucks along the back waist. However the look just seems too baggy, especially as the bum is large. I think they will be fine for a hot summer day with a tunic type cotton or linen blouse. The pictures are after washing them and the waistband stretching.

Perfect for hiking! These are exactly what I wanted! I work at home and do yoga, so I live in leggings. I bought these so that I could throw them on over the yoga leggings when it is chilly, because we hike every morning. I can just peel them off when we get home. Since they go over the leggings, I can wear them a few days in a row and then toss them in the wash. They are water resistant, so dew or rain won't bother me at all. I will stay warm and dry. These are so much better looking than most pants of this nature. They actually have a style that isn't weird or baggy in the butt. They are a little long on me, but once you get a pair of hiking boots on it isn't a problem. I bought another pair so that I will always have one clean and ready to go. They run true to size. I typically wear a size 4, I purchased a size 4 and they fit nicely right over the leggings. Not too loose or too tight. I am 5'4" and 125 lbs. in this photo. They do have some zippered pockets in the front, but not in the back. I think it looks nicer, and I don't need any of the pockets anyway because I have a small bag that I carry my cell phone in with a few other things when we hike. I can grab it and go any time. They also make this same pair of pants with a fleece lining. I might give those a shot for the really cold weather. I highly recommend them!

Average - superior material, odd fit The person wearing these is 120 pounds and 5’6”. The pants are a size 2, which is the smallest size for women in this brand and type. The material is superior and I guess they are long legged for taller people with these small measurements - she is a 27 waist and normally a size 0. Different Fit For Different Colors! I am 5'9" and a pretty skinny 126 lbs. I bought 2 pairs in the exact same size - Red, Brown, Medium. I returned the red. They did not fit the same neither were they both of consistent quality. The red fit smaller & were narrower/tapered in the legs. The material was poly/cotton & thinner. The brown was a great fit, long enough and thick, comfy material. They are 65% Rayon, 35% Spandex! That means they will never shrink in the dryer. I don't detect any stretching from the Spandex content, which is odd, but for me, not a bad thing. Haven't washed them yet, so we'll see how they hold up otherwise. Maybe these pants were made in different countries, but now I'm looking to get a different color that is exactly the same as the brown pair. If you have bought other colors & you see Rayon/Spandex mix, post it in your review. I'll def order that color. It seems that I can't post 2 different reviews, Amazon keeps giving me the first review to edit for the second pair. So I've combined both reviews here. 2 stars for the Red, 4 stars for the brown .... Guess I'll average to 4 stars so the browns don't reflect badly.

Great for lounging, would not wear them for workouts. Fit to size. I am wearing these at home, they are so soft and light - you almost do not feel them. Comfy and lightweight lounge pants Based on the images, I thought this would have more of a slouchy fit and sit on the hip comfortably. I was picturing them being cozy for around the house, but even being based on the size guide, you have to really wear them high waisted otherwise they cut into my hips and that's coming from someone who's on the pretty petite side at 5"3 107 pounds. I say that just so you can get a feel for fit. They're very thin so don't leave a lot to the imagination, but they are soft and comfy material, and I can imagine wearing them still around the house or maybe for yoga or errands.

Ultrasoft & Extremely Comfortable I ordered these pajama pants for my girlfriend. She was specifically looking for a pair with pockets that would be both functional and comfy. These pants truly hit the mark. She loves them. She says they're extremely comfortable. They're ultrasoft and the material is not too thin or thick, just right.Her favorite feature is the pockets, which are perfectly placed and a practical depth. They're incredibly stretchy and roomy. I ordered an XL, but she said she'll order a size down for the next pair (because she definitely wants another). She is typically between a 14-16 and a 16-18 and is 5' tall. The XL is not only large in the waist, but also long on her.I ordered Dark Grey and she said the color's incredible. She even thinks if styled correctly, you could get away with wearing them out and about. These pajama pants are a great value.

Pajamas for warmer climates I am 5'4" and 135lbs. I ordered a medium. Great basic pajama bottoms. The fabric is lightweight so they're perfect for warmer climates. The fit is good. The length might be too short if you're very tall. Overall, I'm pleased with these.

Overall Great Product These pants are not a cotton material or actual flannel material, so they most likely won't shrink in the wash. They are very lightweight, and soft. It's hard to tell if they will be hot to sleep in. Even though I live in Florida, I'm cold natured so this most likely won't be an issue for me. However, sometimes polyester type material can be warm, despite these feeling lightweight. So keep that in mind when purchasing. The colors are very vibrant as well. I really love that they have pockets. My last pair didn't. Depending on how you wear PJs, is how I would suggest choosing size options. I usually almost wear a size small in lounge pants. Size 2-4 in pants, 115 lbs and 5'3. I'm petite but I have larger hips. I decided to go with a size medium because I wanted them to be more baggy. I usually wear my lounge pants low on my hips, so even though these pants were pretty big, they fit nicely this way because they have an elastic waistband with a tie string. I've uploaded pics so you can see that if you wear your pants higher on your waist and you have a smaller waist like me, you may want to order true to your size. Even with them being worn high waisted, I like that the crotch didn't ride up. There was still plenty of room. Unfortunately, they were too big for me to wear high waisted, even with the string tightly tied. I'll stick with wearing them lower on my hips, until I get pregnant. These were also pretty long on me, but I don't mind that. Overall, I'm happy with these and can't wait to wear them in December, since I purchased them to match hubby's flannel Christmas pj's.

Ordered a Large I'm 5'3 and 175lbs So far I absolutely love these, they are soft and very comfortable. However there's a couple of things that I didn't particularly care for about them the first would be is I wasn't expecting printed material. So its a good thing I bought a large or I would've had that stretched whiteout appearance on my larger area, if you get what I'm saying 😬 Buying the large however means that there made for a taller person? So the waist band of the pj pants almost meets my bra. Which is perfect if your looking to put your tummy in. The 4th picture is what the material looks like all stretched out the 5th still has my hand behind it. So if you grab the wrong size it won't look so good. But as a side note the material is not see through otherwise. The tag though, was awful. But no biggie I seam ripped it out. Advice to the producers bigger tag that is sewn down all the way around maybe that would help, shouldn't take anymore time because of how tiny the current one is. Thank you for the comfy pj pants 🙂

I actually like these & they're incredibly comfortable - review from a fluffy mom I am reviewing this as a mom with a bit of a fluffy body (I am EXTREMELY self-conscious about my waist and belly so no photos of the waistband on), 5'5"ish and usually wear an 8/10 -- so definitely not young and cute like the model. I ordered a Large in these pants and I am very pleasantly surprised.First, they are a cotton-twilly material that doesn't feel particularly soft initially but actually moves pretty well when wearing the pants. They have a boxy feel -- but that is typical of the cargo look (and my lack of an actual waist). I give the waist band 100 stars -- it is a mix of elastic and button/zipper so it has some give, doesn't compress on my belly or slip below a muffin top and it and is really super comfortable. I won't show any photos, but the band comes right above my belly button and securely above my hips and covers the small of my back completely-- so they cover everything and sit in a very comfortable spot. Even when I sit they are very comfortable and stay in place above my belly and hips. There are belt loops too just in case you need them, but waist wise the size is perfect for me, esp with the elastic!Length wise, these seem to run a bit long for me -- however I actually really find the rolled-up style with the sandals cute. For tennis shoes I rolled the hem up once and I think they are okay. The boots photos have no roll. The sandals photos took 3 full wide rolls on the hem. These could easily be hemmed, but I often like my pants on the longer side so I can wear them with different types of shoes and in different season -- so I am good with these. But, yes, I would say they run long.Lots and lots of pockets -- 2 back pockets, 2 hip pockets, and 4 cargo pockets. The cargo pockets are real and not just decoration. The top ones just have a flap, the bottom ones have a button with the flap. So lots of storage if you want it.I actually surprised myself and like these. I am always nervous about ordering online because I never feel anything fits me in a flattering way, and I second guessed my order a million times. But now that I have them on and see how incredibly comfortable they are on even with my body style, I am really glad that I pushed order. These are a keeper and I would definitely recommend.

Nice fabric, nicely tailored. Good fit. I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.

The thing I don’t love is that the elastic waist is not just in the back, so if you tuck in the front of your shirt you’re going to see the elastic which isn’t a polished look. I’m 5’5” with a 28” inseam, so you can see how long these are. I think they are just a tad too long for an ideal fit, so if you’re 5’6”-5’8” these may be perfect in length if you want the above-the-ankle look.

Cute corduroy pants! Super cute, comfortable corduroy pants! I like the light grey color, definitely make the corduroy stand out! They feel a bit vintage, which I really like! Elastic waist makes them look and feel more casual. Runs true to size chart, I ordered a size small. For reference I’m 5’7 approximately 135lbs, thick hips and thighs. 5-stars, great quality, great value! Do it, you’ll love them!

Super soft and fit great! I love these corduroys! Nicely soft, inside and out. I ordered the X-Large as per the size chart numbers and the fit is perfect. I would say the sizes are true with these pants. They look like they are a thick corduroy, even when you see them in person, but when you put them on then you can feel they are not as thick as they look. That is not meant as a negative, just something I noticed. I think it gives them an extended time period of wear, as in fall and early spring, instead of just the really cold wintery days like a heavier corduroy would have. They are so comfy when wearing them with nice roominess for moving around and nothing binds. Also, since I am 5'4", the length was nice for me as well. So many times things are too long with me being borderline short, lol. If I had to pick something to grumble on, I guess it would be that I am not a fan of the square front pockets. Just do not look so nice, to me. I prefer the sloped, side pockets like are usually the normal type. Fashions come and go though, so this season it is square pockets. Next season, who knows?! All in all, I am very glad I got these and look forward to the days when it is cool enough to be able to wear them now. I can definitely recommend these!

The waistband didn't pinch too much, and the length was just right. They were a little wrinkly outside the bag, but that's expected. Unfortunately I couldn't get past the 100% polyester fabric. Also, in the summer heat, humidity and polyester don't mix. These were returned.

My favorite pants! After looking at the dress code at my new job, my goal was to find dress pants that felt like sweatpants. Goal achieved, ladies! This is it! These pants are SO comfie, I now own them in 3 colors, and I plan on buying more. They are PERFECT! The waistband has enough stretch to be comfortable, and there are beltloops if you prefer wearing a belt. I'm 5'4, wearing a large, and the length is perfect for ballet flats or loafers. So these might not work for the tall girlies. At first, I wasn't sure about the pleated front; I was always told pleats in the front make you look bigger, and I'm already a hippie girl. I know pleats are in style so I gave it a try. They don't bother me at all; they make them look more like dress pants. And I didn't even get to the best part yet...the inside is a soft fleece material! So soft, so warm! It makes them feel like soft fleece sweatpants. I imagine these are going to keep my legs warm in the winter! Buy these pants! You won't regret it!

These are more like sweat pants than anything. I definitely would not try to pass these off as business casual. MAYBE you could get away with it with the black color, but I still think you’d be pushing it. The green is much brighter than I was hoping for, and kind of just makes them look like pajama pants. The material certainly doesn’t do my body any favors. Shrinks a bit in the wash. I’m 5’7” 200 lbs and ordered the XL. Just a smidge too short on me too, so not for the tall girlies. Wouldn’t recommend unless you’re looking for a strictly lounge pant, and then I’d still probably tell you to look elsewhere.

Soooo Comfortable I'm always worried about sizing when buying clothes online so I tend to order up a size no matter the reviews. The reviews were really mixed for these pants. I'm 5'5" 215lbs right now and I ordered the red in XXL. The fabric is crazy stretchy, soft, and medium weight (not too heavy, not too thin). Everyone's body is different, but I feel pretty confident I could go all the way down to a large, but I wanted the really baggy look so I am definitely keeping these. I don't need to use the zipper or button at all, they will just pull up. The waist band is elastic across the back. The length is long enough for me to roll up the bottoms the way the model pictures are. The red color is great, it's a deep wine color (looks a little brighter in my pics than in person), it looks great for fall. I will buy more when I can afford to. These will be a fall and winter staple for me. Comfy enough to wear working on the farm or dress up for going out.

So comfy! I ordered the rusty orange, and the color is exactly what I expected! The material is so nice, not heavy but not thin either. Feels like I’m wearing sweat pants at work! Shipping took around 3 weeks, but it was worth it! I am 145lb and 5”7, ordered a medium and the baggy-ness is perfect. I have a short torso, so the top is a little loose on me, I just wear a belt to keep it in place! I plan on ordering more colors.

These pants are amazing! I bought these for a concert and I have to say I'm very impressed with the quality! First thing is they are long. I am 5' 6" and they covered my feet I wore platform shoes and it was perfect. Second thing they are not itchy! They have a nice inner lining but my legs do rub together, thicc thighs and all, and the sequins caught a few times but that was after I had them on for a few hours. Styled with aviator sunglasses, gold dangling earrings and belt chain, a white tank, and mustard yellow faux leather jacket. I’m not quite 5’6” and am like 145 lbs, so do with that whatever you need to. The pros: these were perfect for the 70s theme birthday party I went to. I was in theme and felt cute which was all that really mattered to me. That said, the cons: I would have liked these to have been longer and for comfort around my soft midsection, went with a Large size. The sequins do catch, so I had to keep my legs/knees apart or I’d be pulling the two pant legs apart and tearing sequins in the process. Had to stand and walk strategically (aka did the Hustle) in order to minimize damage. Sensorywise, the pants were a nightmare… the sequins that got caught in the seam were scratchy against my thighs in a way that had me fantasizing about soft, comfy pjs. Takeaway: cute, not comfy. Would wear again but only for a specific event like Halloween, New Years, or I’ve become Taylor Swift.

Great Pants ! 5’3, 129lbs, ordered a small. The length was perfect with my V@ns sneakers. Very comfortable. I did snag together a couple times but was able to get in snagged easily. They were perfect for my costume but I would definitely wear these again for other occasions.

Love the pants! A little bit "tall" FYI Reviewed by spouse: These are so fun, I cannot wait to wear them! These will shrink IN LENGTH ONLY if you need them to These pants in Medium fit exactly as I expected them to on my 5'3" 150lb frame; comfortable around the waist & hip, flowy through the legs, and approximately half a mile too long. The first photo is them right out of the package. The second is them after a quick wash then dry on high heat. Fit around the waist & hip was the same. So if you do NOT want to lose any of the length, do not expose these to heat in the dryer. If you do need to lose some of that length, you have that option. Other than that, they're pretty straightforward palazzo pants. Possibly could have fit a size up as well I ordered a size down since there were multiple reviews saying the pants run large. I’ve run into this issue before with other brands and have had mediums falling off of me. So I went ahead and ordered a small tall. The fit is a little snug, so it’s possible a medium would have fit, but they’re still comfortable enough that I plan on keeping them. As long as they don’t shrink in the wash, I should be good. I ordered tall since at 5’7, I’m somewhat on the taller side for a woman. They do bunch up slightly at the bottom, so it’s possible I could have fit regular. I can’t say how long they will last since I just bought them. I plan on wearing them for work regularly. so I will try to remember to update this review later on. The material feels nice and I like that there are double pockets in the front.

A bit long for the petite girlies, no usable phone pocket I’m 5’2” and 185 pounds and normally wear an XL. I sized up as according to the size guide my measurements would put me in a XXL. I kinda wish I went with my normal size as the tummy compression in these are nice but I think they would be more flattering a bit tighter. The cut isn’t super flattering on the dreaded “fupa” either. Not sure if it would be any better with a tighter size or not because the hem line is right along the bottom of my belly so I imagine it may accentuate it even more. The length of this style is “regular” and as someone petite they are definitely on the long side and run me about 2.5-3.5 inches too long. The regulars would be ideal for someone 5’5” and up probably. I am able to hike them up a little so they don’t drag on the floor while I’m wearing them but obviously not ideal. They are super comfortable and very soft. They feel buttery—almost velvety without all the fuzz that comes with true velvet. They are a little on the warm side but more so “cozy” than hot. The style is very cute and flattering with the bell bottom split hem. It makes my small calves look more balanced with my curvy physique and makes me appear less top heavy. They are also completely squat-proof which is a huge plus. They do tend to attract fuzz and pet hair for some reason, although it wipes off easily. My biggest issue is the one pocket they have in the waistband is much to small to function as a phone pocket for my iPhone 10 Pro Max. It can fit vertically about halfway but feels very insecure and there’s no way it can fit sideways as it’s much too small. It’s a weirdly shaped pocket because it’s more wide than it is deep. It’s maybe 3 inches deep and 3.5 inches wide. Probably better to be used for just cards and keys. A pocket that holds my phone comfortably it a must for yoga pants that are this expensive for me, so I’m taking off a star for that. Otherwise they are pretty great! If that isn’t a deal breaker for you, then I would definitely recommend these if you are 5’5” or taller.

Very comfy and stylish! My daughter likes it shes 5ft and got a small size. Perfect fit for her and fabric are quality.

So cute and comfy, but shrunk in the wash I got this in both Apricot and Khaki XS and am in love. They’re so so comfy and cute and I feel like they can actually be dressed up or down depending on what you’re going for. It feels like you’re wearing pajamas but they still look elevated and presentable. They have a linen look with very little stretch (they’re so loose that I wasn’t bothered by lack of stretch). The tag says they’re 70% cotton and 30% linen. For reference, I’m 4’10”, 108-110 lbs, 26-27” waist, 36-37” hips and I had to get around 4” hemmed for these to fit as they do in the photos. WARNING: the attached photos were all taken prior to washing; after washing according to the tag’s care instructions (cold machine wash and line dry) they appear to have shrunk around 1 inch, which is unfortunate considering I paid to have these hemmed and apparently could’ve gotten an inch less and avoided this.

Comfy Casual but Put together 5’3” 29” waist ~145lbs Fabric : Nice drapey linen blend(?) fabric does well in heat and humidity, and has a flattering flow to it. Fit: has a real draw string, with no hidden elastic waist, so they are easily adjustable. I ordered the XL, for a drapier fit, and they did shrink a bit in length after the first cool wash. Definitely wash on cold to avoid shrinkage. Color & Transparency: Color is as pictured, slightly transparent but wearably so- i have tattoos on my legs and bum and if pulled tight you can see them, but for normal wear they’re just fine. You’re not seeing right through them. Overall very versatile, good for hot and humid weather, comfortable and flowy, and look really put together with monochromatic looks. Can be dressed up or down a bit depending on what you pair with them. I’d say they’re a good value.

Good quality, breathable, comfortable, stylish I am 5’0”, 120lbs. I bought a size small. These pants were perfect for a summer day outdoors walking about the farmers market. They kept me cool and were surprisingly comfortable and breathable. These ended up being a little long on me (I will need to hem them an inch or two) but with the right shoes I was able to wear these without the alterations. My other concern was that the light color and texture would be transparent/see through at all. I was slightly disappointed to find them slightly transparent but with nude/skin color undergarments it is not an issue. I would recommend these linen pants as they are flowy, fun and very cute for summer/spring!

Not great but they’ll work I’ve been searching for light weight summer pants and these are ok. It never looks like the model but I’ll keep. My husband says I look like I’m on vacation. I’m 5’8 and 142 lbs and glad I got a medium for the loose relaxed look.

I’ll buy more 5’6”, 165 pound, bought medium These are soooo comfortable. They’re thicker material so not see through at all. I recommend these! Only con is that they wrinkle easily. I have to steam them everytime and even if I steam and then pack them in a suitcase they come out wrinkled again. Otherwise, great pants!

To Size Up Or True To Fit? Going through the reviews it was challenging to decide whether or not to size up or not. If you have thicker legs and a booty, I would size up so you have a bit more room and it gives you the wide leg look. If you have thinner legs, I suggest purchasing your normal size. I’m 5’5” and 155 pounds, pear shaped. Pic 1 is a large and pic 2 is an extra large. I kept the xl and returned the l, normally I wear an 8. There’s not much of a difference outside of it giving me more room in the hips and bum area. The added benefit of sizing up was to minimize the panty line showing or any marking. The label says cotton and linen, it’s a medium thickness fabric. This is the perfect, lounge, work or travel pants. I loved it enough to buy a second pair in apricot. Oh! If you’re shorter than 5’7” you’ll probably have to get the pants hemmed. Sharing for mental preparation.

The Pants For ANY Occasion I have these pants in rust and apricot and I really love them. For reference: I am 5' 6" and about 125lbs. Pros: drawstring waist, lightweight, pockets, flattering for a flatter figure despite being a more flowy cut. They can be dressed up or down - I can wear them with almost any crop top...so great for travelling if you want to pack light but still have options. I could wear flats without tripping on the hem. Cons: I read reviews where they shrunk in the wash - I have avoided this potential issue by doing the lazy thing and not washing them. The lighter pair IS see-through, but I just wore nude spandex to remedy this issue.

Pants of the spring / summer! I got these for a trip to Jamaica this Spring and I absolutely loved them. I've worn them many times since - dressed up to work, casual drinks with friends, and to travel in. They are very comfortable and light to wear in hot temperatures but they also have some weight to them to be comfortable when it isn't hot. They were not see through, although I would recommend wearing nude undergarments. I love that you can adjust the waist - great for when bloated! I am 5'4 130lbs so they are a tad long when I wear shoes without a heel but I also have offset that by wearing them higher on my waist. Highly recommend these!

I love these so much that I bought more! (Colors and sizing are issues, though) These are SO cute and comfy!! I initially only ordered one pair, but I liked them so much that I ordered another two pairs! In terms of colors, however... The green is a much lighter shade compared to the deep emerald depicted in the listing. Also, like some other reviewers, I'm not sure if they sent me the pink instead of purple, or if that's genuinely the color of the "purple" (but since these are just loungewear for home, I'm not really bothered). The grey blue is pretty spot on, though. Keep in mind that these DEFINITELY run small - I'm normally a medium/large-ish in USA women's (5'3" and roughly ~150lbs), but I ordered these in XL and I feel like they fit appropriately (slightly baggy). I do have to roll up the bottoms 2 times, but that's super easy because of the elastic. All in all, if you're not too worried about the exact color, I think these are a great option for comfy lounge joggers.

Not The Same Costco Quality Because we all know 5 star reviews rise to the top on Amazon - I wanted to make sure mine is honest. For those of you hoping these Felina leggings are the same as the ones from Costco, you will be disappointed to learn these are not. The Costco brand is 90% polyester and 10% Spandex. These new Felina leggings are 88% polyester and 12% elastane. Sure elastane and spandex are essentially the same but the polyester percentage change between the two has certainly made a difference in texture. Truly, I would give these about 3 stars because while they are acceptable they are not comfortable. They have a slick texture and are shiny compared to my old Felina from Costco. The sizing is way off as well. The Felina I got from Costco is a size small and fit rather loosely (but comfortable in the thighs and hips). They are also super high waist. These new ones from Amazon are tight! Like pressing into my stomach tight and at 5’1 I weigh only 109 lbs! They cling to my skin and feel a bit too much like tights for biking or better yet, they remind the of the same material you might find in swimwear. It’s just not what I was expecting. I plan on returning them.

I’m quite thin— between a zero and a 2, and these leggings don’t give me the greatest confidence when I’m even a little bloated. Any tummy you may have will be highlighted by these if out in the sun. Definitely lounge leggings for home. Still love the length as I’m 5’11” and they aren’t 7/8ths length — they actually go down to my ankle. Love the fabric, love the high waist, but ankles could be a bit more fitted. Perfect lounge leggings, but not quite enough compression to feel fully comfortable wearing them outside in the sun, without a covered bum/groin. I’m 5’11” 115lbs and got the xsmall. Great all-around leggings These leggings are a must-have staple in my closet! They’re perfect for everyday loungewear and light exercise, making them very versatile. The fabric is a thick, cotton-like material - so it you’re looking no for heavy-duty compression workout leggings, these aren’t it, but they’re great for everything else. Great leggings, not see thru! I really like these leggings. They are really soft, truly high waisted, squat proof, pockets on legs plus a pocket in the waist band that is big enough to fit a slim wallet, ID, keys or a snacky snack. I wore these for about 12 -14 hours to see how they would hold up and they did really well! Very minimal pulling them back in place which is great! I’m in between sizes right now on my weight loss journey, so currently a X-Large would be too snug for my liking and the 2Xlarge has a little bit of wiggle room for when my food baby gets bloated. I’m 5’8, 260 pounds - bottom heavy, waist 42 inch and hips 49/50inches. You can see from the pictures my body type and how they look. I also noticed I don’t see as much of my cellulite through these as I do my other leggings, which my thighs and butt is ALL cellulite. Really good price for two for really good quality. I have a hard time finding leggings that are not see thru that I do not have to constantly keep pulling up to try and get to stay around my waist. I plan to order another set.

Very flattering fit, soft, and hits the waist perfectly When I got these, the first thing I noticed was a slight smell to them, either a heavy plastic smell or something, and likely came from the packaging. I'm sure it will wash away once I wash these. I got the black and the grey in the long version. For reference, I am 5'0" and 118 Ibs. These are a little more relaxed fit than the usual stuff I get (I usually get things that are super tight and hold and hoist things up... but I am just getting back into working out and figured it wasn't necessary to do that), but they were comfortable and clung in all the right places, the high waist certainly does hit the waist dead on. There is obviously more length to these, but as a short person since these are a tight fit, it doesn't get under the feet or snag, so I'm not bothered, I usually try and move anything too loose around a bit. I did notice that there was a mild panty line with these, but nothing worth complaining about, I will just use seamless undergarments with these. The first pair I tried on was the grey pair, and they felt more relaxed than the black pair for some reason, not significantly, but it was noticeable enough once I put on the black pair which fit a little more tightly. Both pairs are noticeably soft, with a nice polyester/spandex mix that is very comfortable and pretty breathable. I have yet to try them when working out but I will be sure to update this review once I've washed them and tried them out (So I will review them to see if their quality changes with a mild washing and air dry) and then working out in them.

What a Surprise...Squat Proof! I liked these leggings so much that I ordered another set. It holds in my stomach which decrease my lower back discomfort when doing sit ups and the waistband doesn't roll. Soft material against the skin. I have washed both pair several times with no shrinkage or fading. I place it in the washer on delicate with the rest of my gym clothes and let air dry. Wash instructions are on the label. Great pants but lenght is a little short These heated pants are nice. They are heavy duty and well lined. They seem to be good quality. The pants have back pockets, front pockets, and side cargo pockets. The battery pack sits in the left cargo pocket. I was excited to see the option to pick different temperatures for each area as well. The pants heated up fairly quickly and got quite warm. The battery pack came fully charged. It also has 3 dots for a battery indicator so you can keep an eye on your battery power remaining.I did reduce my review by 1 star for 2 reasons. 1. I don't like the front button closure. I ordered small because that is what I usually wear but I have to wear them with a droopy crotch area to barely get them long enough. They would be capris if I wore them pulled all the way up. Awesome, but be wary of the sizing!! Okay, so the reason I’m writing this review is because these leggings are so awesome, however the sizing is so weird! I got two sizes because I wasn’t sure, and I’ll be keeping both, I’m usually in between a medium and a large with leggings depending on the brand. I am a size 9 or 11 in jeans, I weigh 170lbs and I’m 5’6”, so I’m not always sure if it’s a new brand I’m trying. I got both sizes of the black, medium and large, I tried the medium first, and they fit amazingly and are SO comfy and have just enough stretch and I could tell I’m going to work out in these too! Then I try on the large and they’re actually TIGHTER than the medium?? I had my boyfriend check the size tag on the back just to make sure I wasn’t losing my mind and sure enough, they were in fact the large? So how is a large tighter on me than the medium?? I’m not really sure haha but either way, both fit, both are comfortable and both make my butt look FAB. Like, covers all cellulite and shows off all my curves like no other leggings ever has! I havnt worked out in them yet, or done much other than stare at my ass and show my boyfriend over and over how good it looks hahaha! Anyway, just keep in mind the sizing issues it looks like I’m not the only one having issues with the sizes but I highly recommend these leggings and will 100% be buying more!! I hope this helped some of you trying to pick sizes out! I was nervous because people were saying they were like 140lbs and getting the medium, but they fit me great and I’m 170lbs and 5’6”!!

They are decent… UPDATE- So I decided to give them another try lol. I ordered the style on this listing without the pockets in size small (one size down from the size I originally ordered) and they fit much better and I like them better overall. Still keeping my star rating at 3 stars because they aren’t as opaque as I had hoped, like I mentioned in my original review. I do like them much better though without the pocket (weird seam in front) and in a smaller size. Ugh I had such high hopes for these leggings! 😒 They are just ok. Not bad, but decent. For reference I’m 5’4” 136lbs. I ordered size medium. It fits but feels a little loose all over for a legging. I wish I would’ve gotten a size small. I shouldn’t have listened to the reviews that said size up. The length is too long for me it bunches at the ankles and I didn’t find that flattering. The material is really soft and stretchy. I got the ones with the pockets and the pockets hold my iPhone X snugly. I don’t know how it would hold a plus because my phone is pretty snug in there. You could probably get it in there because the material is stretchy but it would be tight! There are two vertical seams (coming down from the pockets) going down the front of the leggings that I didn’t care for. The “butt scrunch” part was just like advertised. It did not give me a wedgie, lol. I was worried about that. 😆 The area where these leggings let me down the most is they aren’t as thick and opaque as my other amazon leggings. 😞 They aren’t necessarily super see through or anything but in comparison to my other leggings it’s a let down. The texture definitely helps disguise whatever you are wearing under the leggings, but I just imagined they would be completely squat proof. They are only sorta squat proof. They are fine bending over to pick something up but if you are really doing squats I would worry about panties showing through. I’m Keeping them because they are very soft and comfortable. I’ll probably wear them around the house and with longer tops. I had planned using these for working out but I won’t be doing that. I would rate them 3.5 stars out of 5 if I could because they aren’t a total let down, I just had slightly higher hopes for them. If you don’t mind the bunching at the ankles and the fact they aren’t 100% squat proof then I say go for it. They probably are better quality than a lot of the cheap “tik tok style” leggings out there.

Great quality, not that enhancing if you’re lacking in the behind region Don’t let the username fool you, I took advantage of my parents prime membership to order these after canceling mine lol! I did have high expectations for these leggings. I will say the quality is superb, they’re very thick and at first upon inspection they looked like they were going to be see through, but the texture keeps them from being as such. I did follow what people were saying about true to size, but I’m not a small girl at 5’6” and somewhere between 155-165, I honestly don’t know. I will say I do have big hips, but smaller legs and a flat butt, genes are my kryptonite. I ordered a medium, and I actually would’ve been fine with a small to be honest, maybe my butt would’ve looked a little better, but if you’re a girl like me who has a pancake butt, don’t expect a magic lift. In my pictures I’m wearing butt enhancing parties and a waist trainer and it’s still looking a little lacking in what I want. Don’t get me wrong, I do my squats to try to achieve that coveted booty look, but I will say, don’t expect some magic leggings to help you get the look if you don’t have them. I will say I do like the leggings a lot, and they make my legs look great, but I just didn’t get the booty look I was hoping for 😭 still four stars for the quality. I would give 5 for that reason, but I’m deducting a star for what I was hoping for. All in all, they’re still quality leggings and I will wear them a lot, but I would definitely order smaller next time.

GREAT value! Wow, great bang for your buck! I ordered these at the last minute when I saw that there would be significant rain on my multi-day hike. I wore them over my hiking pants, between 4-8 hours a day, hiking in the rain for 4 days and they stood the test for sure. At first they felt a bit restrictive (but you're wearing 2 pants, so come on), then I got used to them; they were definitely breathable as well.I knock off a star for a couple of reasons: 1) you have to take your shoes off in order to put these on-- no wide pant cuffs; and 2) no pockets. The pants do seem to be "stitched" together in the seams by tape-- I'm not sure if they're sewn underneath and the tape is for reinforcement. If these pants would have ripped in the butt, the majority of my lower half would still have been dry. If they were to have broken on me on the 2nd or 3rd day I still would have gotten my money's worth because I really put them through rough conditions for a significant period of time. If you don't plan on tons of outdoor activities in the rain and don't want to invest in super expensive pants, then these are for you.Lastly, I'll share that I'm approximately 130 lbs and a size 6-8. I bought a small, and they were just right. If I were about 5 lbs heavier or wearing insulated pants, they would not have fit and would have to size up. Hope this helps!P.S. to the people who say these are not fashionable.... who ARE you?! They're RAIN PANTS!

Not for curvy girls These pants were not made for anyone with hips. I ordered the xxl, which should have been large enough to fit leggings or skinny jeans underneath, according to the size chart. I am 5'4" and very pear shaped, so I am used to pants running a little long, but these are unusable. I could barely walk in them, let alone sit down.

Good quality and comfy! I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

Differences between fabric styles is important! I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

So chic and classy, perfection!!! PETITE FRIENDLY I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

Quality, Comfort, Value —💯 These pants are fantastic! I was so surprised at how much I love them. They are soft and oh-so comfy! I got the lightweight version and they are exactly that—lightweight, yet not see through. Absolutely perfect for summer! They look so much more expensive than what they cost. Thrilled to add these to my wardrobe. Don’t know about durability yet as this is my first time wearing them, but they seem like they’ll be ok in the wash. My only CON—and not a dealbreaker—is that there’s this funky closure. There’s a normal tab closure with a hook & eye, but then there’s a faux belt with the double rings where you pass the fabric through and tighten it by pulling the end—I hope that makes sense. Anyway, it was only on the front by the tab closure and cheapened the pants significantly so I removed it (of course before I thought to take a pic). Plus, idk how I’d wear a belt on top of it. Idk why the manufacturer would put that there. It’s not shown in any picture either. Again, not a dealbreaker, but wanted to mention it so it’s no surprise like it was for me. Here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 135 lbs. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but it’s not awful. My measurements are 39” (or 34DD), 29”, 39”. I ordered the SMALL in lightweight white. I hope you found my review !

cute slacks if you dont wanna splurge I'm 5'1.5 with a long torso so my biggest problem is the "high waisted" needs to go beyond my belly button. these pants are good if: - you're looking to not break the bank - need thigh/leg room in your pants - you have a long torso like me - need stretch to accommodate a growing body - you're over ~5'2 i returned them b/c: - material is not giving in my opinion, not terrible either just wasn't soft, comfy - the pants are kinda heavy - i wore a 1in heel with the pants on and it's still kinda long - didn't give me any body shape - not even close to the A&F Sloane pants / Aritzia dupe TLDR: not for the 5'1 girlies unless you want it a little long, decent material just not my preference.

I'm going to buy this in more colors! For reference, I am 4'10" and I bought an XS. These pants are a bit long when I stand barefoot, but are perfect when I stand on my tiptoes or when I wear heels. I think I will hem the bottoms just a bit, but otherwise I absolutely love these pants and I will be purchasing them in multiple colors. The scrunched waist band in the back grabs my waist and keeps the pants tight to my body. There is a lot of room in the thighs and I feel like I can move around! The material is thick enough that I do not have to worry about underwear showing through, but I also bought these in July in Southern California and am able to walk around in them comfortably without being too hot! I am so excited to wear these to work as well as to semi casual things.

Good quality and comfy! I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

Differences between fabric styles is important! I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

So chic and classy, perfection!!! PETITE FRIENDLY I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

Quality, Comfort, Value —💯 These pants are fantastic! I was so surprised at how much I love them. They are soft and oh-so comfy! I got the lightweight version and they are exactly that—lightweight, yet not see through. Absolutely perfect for summer! They look so much more expensive than what they cost. Thrilled to add these to my wardrobe. Don’t know about durability yet as this is my first time wearing them, but they seem like they’ll be ok in the wash. My only CON—and not a dealbreaker—is that there’s this funky closure. There’s a normal tab closure with a hook & eye, but then there’s a faux belt with the double rings where you pass the fabric through and tighten it by pulling the end—I hope that makes sense. Anyway, it was only on the front by the tab closure and cheapened the pants significantly so I removed it (of course before I thought to take a pic). Plus, idk how I’d wear a belt on top of it. Idk why the manufacturer would put that there. It’s not shown in any picture either. Again, not a dealbreaker, but wanted to mention it so it’s no surprise like it was for me. Here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 135 lbs. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but it’s not awful. My measurements are 39” (or 34DD), 29”, 39”. I ordered the SMALL in lightweight white. I hope you found my review !

Great price and quality This is my second pair of these trousers. Both in the thick fabric. This green pair I ordered a size L, the other pair I ordered in an XL. I’m around 180 pounds, 5’1. I have a very high waist and short torso and long legs for my height (note: my legs are still short AF, but since my torso is so short and my waist so high my legs at least look longer than my 5’1 height would make you think). The XL fit nice and were comfy and flowy, but they sat lower than my natural waist which annoyed me. The L fits exactly at my waist. They are a touch snug though, not uncomfortably so, but if there was a size inbetween the L and XL they would be perfect. I got the regular length in both sizes. The length in the L is perfect, they are a touch long on the XL since they sit below my natural waist but both work well with boots/heels. The XL works with flats/loafers if they have a platform sole. The only negative is that they don’t flatter my big bubble butt the way I’m used to with different style pants but the booty doesn’t ALWAYS have to be pippin’ I suppose. Regardless, love these pants. I haven’t tried the thin fabric style yet.
